text.NRSV: Paul, a servant of Jesus Christ, called to be an apostle, set apart for the gospel of God,
text.Rotherham: Paul, a servant of Jesus Christ, a called apostle, separated unto the glad-message of God—
text.DRB: Paul the servant of Jesus Christ, called to be an Apostle, separated into the Gospel of God,
text.KJV: Paul, a servant of Jesus Christ, called to be an apostle, separated unto the gospel of God,
text.NABRE: I. Address Chapter 1 Greeting. Paul, a slave of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle and set apart for the gospel of God,

text.NRSV: which he promised beforehand through his prophets in the holy scriptures,
text.Rotherham: Which he promised beforehand, through his prophets, in holy scriptures—
text.DRB: which before he had promised by his Prophets in the holy Scriptures.
text.KJV: (Which he had promised afore by his prophets in the holy scriptures,)
text.NABRE: which he promised previously through his prophets in the holy scriptures,

text.NRSV: the gospel concerning his Son, who was descended from David according to the flesh
text.Rotherham: Concerning his Son,—who came to be of the seed of David, according to flesh,
text.DRB: of his Son, (who was made to him of the seed of David according to the flesh,
text.KJV: Concerning his Son Jesus Christ our Lord, which was made of the seed of David according to the flesh;
text.NABRE: the gospel about his Son, descended from David according to the flesh,

text.NRSV: and was declared to be Son of God with power according to the spirit of holiness by resurrection from the dead, Jesus Christ our Lord,
text.Rotherham: Who was distinguished as the Son of God—by power, according to a Holy Spirit, through means of a resurrection of the dead,—Jesus Christ our Lord;
text.DRB: who was predestinate the Son of God in power, according to the spirit of sanctification, by the resurrection of our Lord Jesus Christ from the dead,
text.KJV: And declared to be the Son of God with power, according to the spirit of holiness, by the resurrection from the dead:
text.NABRE: but established as Son of God in power according to the spirit of holiness through resurrection from the dead, Jesus Christ our Lord.

text.NRSV: through whom we have received grace and apostleship to bring about the obedience of faith among all the Gentiles for the sake of his name,
text.Rotherham: Through whom we have received favour and apostleship, for obedience of faith among all the nations, in behalf of his name,
text.DRB: by whom we have received grace and Apostleship for obedience to the faith in all Nations for the name of him,
text.KJV: By whom we have received grace and apostleship, for obedience to the faith among all nations, for his name:
text.NABRE: Through him we have received the grace of apostleship, to bring about the obedience of faith, for the sake of his name, among all the Gentiles,

text.NRSV: including yourselves who are called to belong to Jesus Christ,
text.Rotherham: Among whom are, ye also, called of Jesus Christ:
text.DRB: among whom are you also the called of Jesus Christ:)
text.KJV: Among whom are ye also the called of Jesus Christ:
text.NABRE: among whom are you also, who are called to belong to Jesus Christ;

text.NRSV: To all God’s beloved in Rome, who are called to be saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Unto all that are in Rome, beloved of God, called saints, favour unto you, and peace, from God our Father and Lord Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: to all that are at Rome the beloved of God, called to be saints. Grace to you and peace from God our father, and our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: To all that be in Rome, beloved of God, called to be saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: to all the beloved of God in Rome, called to be holy. Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because your faith is proclaimed throughout the world.
text.Rotherham: First, indeed, I give thanks unto my God, through Jesus Christ, concerning you all, because your faith is being announced throughout the whole world.
text.DRB: First I give thanks to my God through Jesus Christ for all you, because your faith is renowned in the whole world.
text.KJV: First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for you all, that your faith is spoken of throughout the whole world.
text.NABRE: Thanksgiving. First, I give thanks to my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because your faith is heralded throughout the world.

text.NRSV: For God, whom I serve with my spirit by announcing the gospel of his Son, is my witness that without ceasing I remember you always in my prayers,
text.Rotherham: For God is, my witness,—unto whom I am rendering divine service in my spirit, in the glad message of his Son,—how incessantly, I am making mention of you
text.DRB: For God is my witness, whom I serve in my spirit in the Gospel of his Son, that without intermission I make a memory of you
text.KJV: For God is my witness, whom I serve with my spirit in the gospel of his Son, that without ceasing I make mention of you always in my prayers;
text.NABRE: God is my witness, whom I serve with my spirit in proclaiming the gospel of his Son, that I remember you constantly,

text.NRSV: asking that by God’s will I may somehow at last succeed in coming to you.
text.Rotherham: At all times in my prayers,—making supplication—if, by some means, even now, at any time, I may have a way opened, in the will of God, to come unto you;
text.DRB: always in my prayers, beseeching, if by any means I may sometime at the length have a prosperous journey by the will of God, to come unto you.
text.KJV: Making request, if by any means now at length I might have a prosperous journey by the will of God to come unto you.
text.NABRE: always asking in my prayers that somehow by God’s will I may at last find my way clear to come to you.

text.NRSV: For I am longing to see you so that I may share with you some spiritual gift to strengthen you—
text.Rotherham: For I am longing to see you, that I may impart some spiritual gift unto you, to the end ye may be established,—
text.DRB: For I desire to see you, that I may impart unto you some spiritual grace, to confirm you:
text.KJV: For I long to see you, that I may impart unto you some spiritual gift, to the end ye may be established;
text.NABRE: For I long to see you, that I may share with you some spiritual gift so that you may be strengthened,

text.NRSV: or rather so that we may be mutually encouraged by each other’s faith, both yours and mine.
text.Rotherham: That is to say—there may be a mutual encouragement among you, each by the other’s faith, both yours and mine.
text.DRB: that is to say, to be comforted together in you by that which is common to us both, your faith & mine.
text.KJV: That is, that I may be comforted together with you by the mutual faith both of you and me.
text.NABRE: that is, that you and I may be mutually encouraged by one another’s faith, yours and mine.

text.NRSV: I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that I have often intended to come to you (but thus far have been prevented), in order that I may reap some harvest among you as I have among the rest of the Gentiles.
text.Rotherham: I do not wish however that ye should be ignorant, brethren, that, many times, have I purposed to come unto you, but have been hindered, until the present,—in order that, some fruit, I might have among you also, even as among the other nations,
text.DRB: And I will not have you ignorant (brethren) that I have often purposed to come unto you (and have been stayed hitherto) that I may have some fruit in you, as also in the other Gentiles.
text.KJV: Now I would not have you ignorant, brethren, that oftentimes I purposed to come unto you, (but was let hitherto,) that I might have some fruit among you also, even as among other Gentiles.
text.NABRE: I do not want you to be unaware, brothers, that I often planned to come to you, though I was prevented until now, that I might harvest some fruit among you, too, as among the rest of the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: I am a debtor both to Greeks and to barbarians, both to the wise and to the foolish;
text.Rotherham: Both to Greeks and to Barbarians, both to wise and to unwise, a debtor, I am:
text.DRB: To the Greeks & the Barbarous, to the wise and the unwise I am debter.
text.KJV: I am debtor both to the Greeks, and to the Barbarians; both to the wise, and to the unwise.
text.NABRE: To Greeks and non-Greeks alike, to the wise and the ignorant, I am under obligation;

text.NRSV: hence my eagerness to proclaim the gospel to you also who are in Rome.
text.Rotherham: Thus, the eagerness on my part—unto you also who are in Rome, to announce the joyful message.
text.DRB: So (as much as is in me) I am ready to evangelize to you also that are at Rome.
text.KJV: So, as much as in me is, I am ready to preach the gospel to you that are at Rome also.
text.NABRE: that is why I am eager to preach the gospel also to you in Rome. II. Humanity Lost Without the Gospel God’s Power for Salvation.

text.NRSV: For I am not ashamed of the gospel; it is the power of God for salvation to everyone who has faith, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
text.Rotherham: For I am not ashamed of the joyful message; for it is God’s power unto salvation, to every one that believeth, both to Jew [first] and to Greek;
text.DRB: For I am not ashamed of the Gospel. For it is the power of God, unto salvation to every one that believeth, to the Jews first and to the Greek.
text.KJV: For I am not ashamed of the gospel of Christ: for it is the power of God unto salvation to every one that believeth; to the Jew first, and also to the Greek.
text.NABRE: For I am not ashamed of the gospel. It is the power of God for the salvation of everyone who believes: for Jew first, and then Greek.

text.NRSV: For in it the righteousness of God is revealed through faith for faith; as it is written, “The one who is righteous will live by faith.”
text.Rotherham: For, a righteousness of God, is therein revealed,—by faith unto faith: even as it is written—But, he that is righteous, by faith, shall live.
text.DRB: For the justice of God is revealed therein by faith into faith; as it is written: And the just liveth by faith.
text.KJV: For therein is the righteousness of God revealed from faith to faith: as it is written, The just shall live by faith.
text.NABRE: For in it is revealed the righteousness of God from faith to faith; as it is written, “The one who is righteous by faith will live.”

text.NRSV: For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and wickedness of those who by their wickedness suppress the truth.
text.Rotherham: For there is being revealed an anger of God from heaven—against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men who, the truth, in unrighteousness, do hold down;—
text.DRB: For the wrath of God from Heaven is revealed, upon all impiety and injustice of those men that detain the verity of God in injustice:
text.KJV: For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men, who hold the truth in unrighteousness;
text.NABRE: Punishment of Idolaters. The wrath of God is indeed being revealed from heaven against every impiety and wickedness of those who suppress the truth by their wickedness.

text.NRSV: For what can be known about God is plain to them, because God has shown it to them.
text.Rotherham: Inasmuch as, what may be known of God, is manifest among them, for, God, unto them, hath made it manifest,—
text.DRB: because, that of God which is known, is manifest in them. For God hath manifested it unto them.
text.KJV: Because that which may be known of God is manifest in them; for God hath shewed it unto them.
text.NABRE: For what can be known about God is evident to them, because God made it evident to them.

text.NRSV: Ever since the creation of the world his eternal power and divine nature, invisible though they are, have been understood and seen through the things he has made. So they are without excuse;
text.Rotherham: For, the unseen things of him, from a world’s creation, by the things made, being perceived, are clearly seen, even his eternal power and divinity,—to the end they should be without excuse;
text.DRB: For his invisible things, from the creation of the world are seen, being understood by those things that are made; his eternal power also and Divinity: so that they are inexcusable.
text.KJV: For the invisible things of him from the creation of the world are clearly seen, being understood by the things that are made, even his eternal power and Godhead; so that they are without excuse:
text.NABRE: Ever since the creation of the world, his invisible attributes of eternal power and divinity have been able to be understood and perceived in what he has made. As a result, they have no excuse;

text.NRSV: for though they knew God, they did not honor him as God or give thanks to him, but they became futile in their thinking, and their senseless minds were darkened.
text.Rotherham: Inasmuch as, having come to know God, not, as God, did they glorify him, or give him thanks, but were made fruitless in their reasonings, and darkened was their undiscerning heart,
text.DRB: Because, whereas they knew God, they have not glorified him as God, or given thanks: but are become vain in their cogitations, and their foolish heart hath been darkened.
text.KJV: Because that, when they knew God, they glorified him not as God, neither were thankful; but became vain in their imaginations, and their foolish heart was darkened.
text.NABRE: for although they knew God they did not accord him glory as God or give him thanks. Instead, they became vain in their reasoning, and their senseless minds were darkened.

text.NRSV: Claiming to be wise, they became fools;
text.Rotherham: Professing to be wise, they were made foolish,
text.DRB: For, saying themselves to be wise, they became fools.
text.KJV: Professing themselves to be wise, they became fools,
text.NABRE: While claiming to be wise, they became fools

text.NRSV: and they exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images resembling a mortal human being or birds or four-footed animals or reptiles.
text.Rotherham: And, exchanged the glory of the incorruptible God, for the likeness of an image of a corruptible man, and of birds and fourfooted beasts and reptiles:
text.DRB: And they changed the glory of the incorruptible God, into a similitude of the image of a corruptible man, and of fowls and four-footed beasts and of them that creep.
text.KJV: And changed the glory of the uncorruptible God into an image made like to corruptible man, and to birds, and fourfooted beasts, and creeping things.
text.NABRE: and exchanged the glory of the immortal God for the likeness of an image of mortal man or of birds or of four-legged animals or of snakes.

text.NRSV: Therefore God gave them up in the lusts of their hearts to impurity, to the degrading of their bodies among themselves,
text.Rotherham: Wherefore God gave them up in the covetings of their hearts unto impurity, so as to be dishonouring their bodies among them,—
text.DRB: (For the which cause God hath delivered them up unto the desires of their heart, unto uncleanness, for to abuse their own bodies among themselves ignominiously.)
text.KJV: Wherefore God also gave them up to uncleanness through the lusts of their own hearts, to dishonour their own bodies between themselves:
text.NABRE: Therefore, God handed them over to impurity through the lusts of their hearts for the mutual degradation of their bodies.

text.NRSV: because they exchanged the truth about God for a lie and worshiped and served the creature rather than the Creator, who is blessed forever! Amen.
text.Rotherham: Who, indeed, exchanged away the truth of God for the falsehood, and rendered worship and service unto the creature rather than unto the Creator,—who is blessed unto the ages. Amen!
text.DRB: Who have changed the verity of God into lying: and have worshipped & served the creature rather than the Creator, who is blessed for ever. Amen.
text.KJV: Who changed the truth of God into a lie, and worshipped and served the creature more than the Creator, who is blessed for ever. Amen.
text.NABRE: They exchanged the truth of God for a lie and revered and worshiped the creature rather than the creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.

text.NRSV: For this reason God gave them up to degrading passions. Their women exchanged natural intercourse for unnatural,
text.Rotherham: For this cause, God gave them up unto dishonourable passions; for, even their females, exchanged away the natural use into that which is against nature,—
text.DRB: Therefore God hath delivered them into passions of ignominy. For their women have changed the natural use, into that use that is contrary to nature.
text.KJV: For this cause God gave them up unto vile affections: for even their women did change the natural use into that which is against nature:
text.NABRE: Therefore, God handed them over to degrading passions. Their females exchanged natural relations for unnatural,

text.NRSV: and in the same way also the men, giving up natural intercourse with women, were consumed with passion for one another. Men committed shameless acts with men and received in their own persons the due penalty for their error.
text.Rotherham: In like manner also, even the males, leaving the natural use of the female, flamed out in their eager desire one for another, males with males, the indecency, effecting,—and, the necessary recompence of their error, within themselves, duly receiving;—
text.DRB: And in like manner the men also, leaving the natural use of the woman, have burned in their desires one toward another, men upon men working turpitude, & the reward of their error (which they should) receiving in themselves.
text.KJV: And likewise also the men, leaving the natural use of the woman, burned in their lust one toward another; men with men working that which is unseemly, and receiving in themselves that recompence of their error which was meet.
text.NABRE: and the males likewise gave up natural relations with females and burned with lust for one another. Males did shameful things with males and thus received in their own persons the due penalty for their perversity.

text.NRSV: And since they did not see fit to acknowledge God, God gave them up to a debased mind and to things that should not be done.
text.Rotherham: And, even as they did not approve to be holding, God, in acknowledgment, God gave them up unto a disapproved mind to be doing the things that are not becoming,
text.DRB: And as they liked not to have God in knowledge; God delivered them up into a reprobate sense, to do those things that are not convenient:
text.KJV: And even as they did not like to retain God in their knowledge, God gave them over to a reprobate mind, to do those things which are not convenient;
text.NABRE: And since they did not see fit to acknowledge God, God handed them over to their undiscerning mind to do what is improper.

text.NRSV: They were filled with every kind of wickedness, evil, covetousness, malice. Full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, craftiness, they are gossips,
text.Rotherham: Filled with all unrighteousness, wickedness, greed, baseness, full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, evil disposition,
text.DRB: replenished with all iniquity, malice, fornication, avarice, wickedness, full of envy, murder, contention, guile, malignity, whisperers,
text.KJV: Being filled with all unrighteousness, fornication, wickedness, covetousness, maliciousness; full of envy, murder, debate, deceit, malignity; whisperers,
text.NABRE: They are filled with every form of wickedness, evil, greed, and malice; full of envy, murder, rivalry, treachery, and spite. They are gossips

text.NRSV: slanderers, God-haters, insolent, haughty, boastful, inventors of evil, rebellious toward parents,
text.Rotherham: Whisperers, detractors, haters of God, insolent, arrogant, vain boasters, inventors of vices, unto parents unyielding,
text.DRB: detractors, odible to God, contumelious, proud, haughty, inventors of evil things, disobedient to parents,
text.KJV: Backbiters, haters of God, despiteful, proud, boasters, inventors of evil things, disobedient to parents,
text.NABRE: and scandalmongers and they hate God. They are insolent, haughty, boastful, ingenious in their wickedness, and rebellious toward their parents.

text.NRSV: foolish, faithless, heartless, ruthless.
text.Rotherham: Without discernment, regardless of covenants, without natural affection, unmerciful:—
text.DRB: foolish, dissolute, without affection, without fidelity, without mercy.
text.KJV: Without understanding, covenantbreakers, without natural affection, implacable, unmerciful:
text.NABRE: They are senseless, faithless, heartless, ruthless.

text.NRSV: They know God’s decree, that those who practice such things deserve to die—yet they not only do them but even applaud others who practice them.
text.Rotherham: Who, indeed, having acknowledged the righteous sentence of God,—that, they who such things as these do practise, are worthy of death, not only, the same things, are doing, but are even delighting together with them who are practising [them].
text.DRB: Who whereas they knew the justice of God, did not understand that they which do such things, are worthy of death: not only they that do them, but they also that consent to the doers.
text.KJV: Who knowing the judgment of God, that they which commit such things are worthy of death, not only do the same, but have pleasure in them that do them.
text.NABRE: Although they know the just decree of God that all who practice such things deserve death, they not only do them but give approval to those who practice them.

text.NRSV: Therefore you have no excuse, whoever you are, when you judge others; for in passing judgment on another you condemn yourself, because you, the judge, are doing the very same things.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, inexcusable, thou art, O man, whoever judgest; for, wherein thou judgest some one else, thyself, thou dost condemn,—for, the very things, thou dost practise, who art judging:
text.DRB: For the which cause thou art inexcusable, o man, whosoever thou be that judgest. For wherein thou judgest another, thou condemnest thyself. For thou doest the same things which thou judgest.
text.KJV: Therefore thou art inexcusable, O man, whosoever thou art that judgest: for wherein thou judgest another, thou condemnest thyself; for thou that judgest doest the same things.
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 God’s Just Judgment. Therefore, you are without excuse, every one of you who passes judgment. For by the standard by which you judge another you condemn yourself, since you, the judge, do the very same things.

text.NRSV: You say, “We know that God’s judgment on those who do such things is in accordance with truth.”
text.Rotherham: We know, however, that, the sentence of God, is according to truth against them who, such things as these, do practise.
text.DRB: For we know that the judgement of God is according to verity upon them that do such things.
text.KJV: But we are sure that the judgment of God is according to truth against them which commit such things.
text.NABRE: We know that the judgment of God on those who do such things is true.

text.NRSV: Do you imagine, whoever you are, that when you judge those who do such things and yet do them yourself, you will escape the judgment of God?
text.Rotherham: And reckonest thou this, O man—who dost judge them who such things do practise, and yet art doing the same, that, thou, shalt escape the sentence of God?
text.DRB: And doest thou suppose this, o man, that judgest them which do such things, and doest the same, that thou shalt escape the judgement of God?
text.KJV: And thinkest thou this, O man, that judgest them which do such things, and doest the same, that thou shalt escape the judgment of God?
text.NABRE: Do you suppose, then, you who judge those who engage in such things and yet do them yourself, that you will escape the judgment of God?

text.NRSV: Or do you despise the riches of his kindness and forbearance and patience? Do you not realize that God’s kindness is meant to lead you to repentance?
text.Rotherham: Or, the riches of his kindness, and forbearance, and long-suffering, dost thou despise,—not knowing that, the kindness of God, unto repentance, is leading thee?
text.DRB: Or doest thou contemn the riches of his goodness, and patience, and longanimity, not knowing that the benignity of God bringeth thee to penance?
text.KJV: Or despisest thou the riches of his goodness and forbearance and longsuffering; not knowing that the goodness of God leadeth thee to repentance?
text.NABRE: Or do you hold his priceless kindness, forbearance, and patience in low esteem, unaware that the kindness of God would lead you to repentance?

text.NRSV: But by your hard and impenitent heart you are storing up wrath for yourself on the day of wrath, when God’s righteous judgment will be revealed.
text.Rotherham: But, according to thy hardness, and [thine] impenitent heart, art treasuring up for thyself anger, in a day of anger and revelation of the righteous judgment of God,—
text.DRB: But according to thy hardness and impenitent heart, thou heapest to thyself wrath, in the day of wrath and of the revelation of the just judgement of God,
text.KJV: But after thy hardness and impenitent heart treasurest up unto thyself wrath against the day of wrath and revelation of the righteous judgment of God;
text.NABRE: By your stubbornness and impenitent heart, you are storing up wrath for yourself for the day of wrath and revelation of the just judgment of God,

text.NRSV: For he will repay according to each one’s deeds:
text.Rotherham: Who will render unto each one according to his works:—
text.DRB: who will render to every man according to his works:
text.KJV: Who will render to every man according to his deeds:
text.NABRE: who will repay everyone according to his works:

text.NRSV: to those who by patiently doing good seek for glory and honor and immortality, he will give eternal life;
text.Rotherham: Unto them, on the one hand, who, by way of endurance in good work, are seeking, glory, honour and incorruption, life age-abiding,
text.DRB: to them truly that according to patience in good work, seek glory and honour and incorruption, life eternal;
text.KJV: To them who by patient continuance in well doing seek for glory and honour and immortality, eternal life:
text.NABRE: eternal life to those who seek glory, honor, and immortality through perseverance in good works,

text.NRSV: while for those who are self-seeking and who obey not the truth but wickedness, there will be wrath and fury.
text.Rotherham: Unto them, on the other hand, who are of contention, and are not yielding unto the truth, but are yielding unto unrighteousness, anger and wrath,
text.DRB: but to them that are of contention, and that obey not the truth, but give credit to iniquity, wrath and indignation.
text.KJV: But unto them that are contentious, and do not obey the truth, but obey unrighteousness, indignation and wrath,
text.NABRE: but wrath and fury to those who selfishly disobey the truth and obey wickedness.

text.NRSV: There will be anguish and distress for everyone who does evil, the Jew first and also the Greek,
text.Rotherham: tribulation and anguish—against every soul of man who worketh out what is base, both of Jew first and of Greek,—
text.DRB: Tribulation and anguish upon every soul of man that worketh evil, of the Jew first and of the Greek:
text.KJV: Tribulation and anguish, upon every soul of man that doeth evil, of the Jew first, and also of the Gentile;
text.NABRE: Yes, affliction and distress will come upon every human being who does evil, Jew first and then Greek.

text.NRSV: but glory and honor and peace for everyone who does good, the Jew first and also the Greek.
text.Rotherham: But glory and honour and peace—unto every one who worketh what is good, both unto Jew first and unto Greek:
text.DRB: but glory and honour and peace to every one that worketh good, to the Jew first and to the Greek.
text.KJV: But glory, honour, and peace, to every man that worketh good, to the Jew first, and also to the Gentile:
text.NABRE: But there will be glory, honor, and peace for everyone who does good, Jew first and then Greek.

text.NRSV: For God shows no partiality.
text.Rotherham: For there is no respect of persons with God;—
text.DRB: For there is no acception of persons with God.
text.KJV: For there is no respect of persons with God.
text.NABRE: There is no partiality with God. Judgment by the Interior Law.

text.NRSV: All who have sinned apart from the law will also perish apart from the law, and all who have sinned under the law will be judged by the law.
text.Rotherham: For, as many as without law sinned, without law, also shall perish, and, as many as within law sinned, through law, shall be judged;
text.DRB: For whosoever have sinned without the Law, without the Law shall perish: and whosoever have sinned in the Law, by the Law shall be judged.
text.KJV: For as many as have sinned without law shall also perish without law: and as many as have sinned in the law shall be judged by the law;
text.NABRE: All who sin outside the law will also perish without reference to it, and all who sin under the law will be judged in accordance with it.

text.NRSV: For it is not the hearers of the law who are righteous in God’s sight, but the doers of the law who will be justified.
text.Rotherham: For, not the hearers of law, are righteous with God, but, the doers of law, shall be declared righteous;
text.DRB: For not the hearers of the Law are just with God: but the doers of the Law shall be justified.
text.KJV: (For not the hearers of the law are just before God, but the doers of the law shall be justified.
text.NABRE: For it is not those who hear the law who are just in the sight of God; rather, those who observe the law will be justified.

text.NRSV: When Gentiles, who do not possess the law, do instinctively what the law requires, these, though not having the law, are a law to themselves.
text.Rotherham: For, whensoever the nations which have not law, by nature, the things of the law, may be doing, the same, not having law, unto themselves, are a law,—
text.DRB: For when the Gentiles which have not the Law, naturally do those things that are of the Law; the same not having the Law, themselves are a law to themselves:
text.KJV: For when the Gentiles, which have not the law, do by nature the things contained in the law, these, having not the law, are a law unto themselves:
text.NABRE: For when the Gentiles who do not have the law by nature observe the prescriptions of the law, they are a law for themselves even though they do not have the law.

text.NRSV: They show that what the law requires is written on their hearts, to which their own conscience also bears witness; and their conflicting thoughts will accuse or perhaps excuse them
text.Rotherham: Who, indeed, shew the work of the law written in their hearts, their conscience therewith bearing witness, and, between one another, their reasonings accusing—or, even excusing, them:—
text.DRB: who shew the work of the Law written in their hearts, their conscience giving testimony to them, and among themselves mutually their thoughts accusing, or also defending,
text.KJV: Which shew the work of the law written in their hearts, their conscience also bearing witness, and their thoughts the mean while accusing or else excusing one another;)
text.NABRE: They show that the demands of the law are written in their hearts, while their conscience also bears witness and their conflicting thoughts accuse or even defend them

text.NRSV: on the day when, according to my gospel, God, through Jesus Christ, will judge the secret thoughts of all.
text.Rotherham: In the day on which God judgeth the secrets of men according to my glad-message through Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: in the day when God shall judge the secrets of men, according to my Gospel, by Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: In the day when God shall judge the secrets of men by Jesus Christ according to my gospel.
text.NABRE: on the day when, according to my gospel, God will judge people’s hidden works through Christ Jesus. Judgment by the Mosaic Law.

text.NRSV: But if you call yourself a Jew and rely on the law and boast of your relation to God
text.Rotherham: If, however, thou, art taking the name of Jew, and resting thyself upon law, and boasting in God,
text.DRB: But if thou be surnamed a Jew, and restest in the Law, and doest glory in God,
text.KJV: Behold, thou art called a Jew, and restest in the law, and makest thy boast of God,
text.NABRE: Now if you call yourself a Jew and rely on the law and boast of God

text.NRSV: and know his will and determine what is best because you are instructed in the law,
text.Rotherham: And art taking note of his will, and testing the things that differ—when receiving oral instruction out of the law,
text.DRB: and knowest his will, and approvest the more profitable things, instructed by the Law,
text.KJV: And knowest his will, and approvest the things that are more excellent, being instructed out of the law;
text.NABRE: and know his will and are able to discern what is important since you are instructed from the law,

text.NRSV: and if you are sure that you are a guide to the blind, a light to those who are in darkness,
text.Rotherham: Art persuaded, moreover, that, thou thyself, art—a guide of the blind, a light of them that are in darkness,
text.DRB: presumest that thyself art a leader of the blind, a light of them that are in darkness,
text.KJV: And art confident that thou thyself art a guide of the blind, a light of them which are in darkness,
text.NABRE: and if you are confident that you are a guide for the blind and a light for those in darkness,

text.NRSV: a corrector of the foolish, a teacher of children, having in the law the embodiment of knowledge and truth,
text.Rotherham: A trainer of the simple, a teacher of babes, having the forming of knowledge and truth in the law,—
text.DRB: a teacher of the foolish, a master of infants, having the form of science & of verity in the Law.
text.KJV: An instructor of the foolish, a teacher of babes, which hast the form of knowledge and of the truth in the law.
text.NABRE: that you are a trainer of the foolish and teacher of the simple, because in the law you have the formulation of knowledge and truth—

text.NRSV: you, then, that teach others, will you not teach yourself? While you preach against stealing, do you steal?
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, that art teaching someone else, thyself, art thou not teaching? Thou that proclaimest—Do not steal! Art thou, stealing?
text.DRB: Thou therefore that teachest another, teachest not thyself: that preachest, men ought not to steal, thou stealest:
text.KJV: Thou therefore which teachest another, teachest thou not thyself? thou that preachest a man should not steal, dost thou steal?
text.NABRE: then you who teach another, are you failing to teach yourself? You who preach against stealing, do you steal?

text.NRSV: You that forbid adultery, do you commit adultery? You that abhor idols, do you rob temples?
text.Rotherham: That sayest—Do not commit adultery! Art thou, committing adultery? That abhorrest sacrilege, Art thou, robbing temples?
text.DRB: that sayest men should not commit adultery, thou committest adultery: that abhorrest idols, thou doest sacrilege:
text.KJV: Thou that sayest a man should not commit adultery, dost thou commit adultery? thou that abhorrest idols, dost thou commit sacrilege?
text.NABRE: You who forbid adultery, do you commit adultery? You who detest idols, do you rob temples?

text.NRSV: You that boast in the law, do you dishonor God by breaking the law?
text.Rotherham: That in law dost boast, Through the transgression of the law, art thou dishonouring God?
text.DRB: that doest glory in the Law, thou by prevarication of the Law doest dishonour God.
text.KJV: Thou that makest thy boast of the law, through breaking the law dishonourest thou God?
text.NABRE: You who boast of the law, do you dishonor God by breaking the law?

text.NRSV: For, as it is written, “The name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you.”
text.Rotherham: For the name of God, because of you, is defamed among the nations,—even as it is written.
text.DRB: ( For the name of God through you is blasphemed among the Gentiles, as it is written.)
text.KJV: For the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles through you, as it is written.
text.NABRE: For, as it is written, “Because of you the name of God is reviled among the Gentiles.”

text.NRSV: Circumcision indeed is of value if you obey the law; but if you break the law, your circumcision has become uncircumcision.
text.Rotherham: For, circumcision, indeed, profiteth—if, law, thou be practising; but, if thou be a transgressor of law, thy circumcision, hath become, uncircumcision
text.DRB: Circumcision indeed profiteth, if thou observe the Law: but if thou be a prevaricator of the Law, thy circumcision is become prepuce.
text.KJV: For circumcision verily profiteth, if thou keep the law: but if thou be a breaker of the law, thy circumcision is made uncircumcision.
text.NABRE: Circumcision, to be sure, has value if you observe the law; but if you break the law, your circumcision has become uncircumcision.

text.NRSV: So, if those who are uncircumcised keep the righteous requirements of the law, will not their uncircumcision be regarded as circumcision?
text.Rotherham: If then, the uncircumcision, be guarding the righteous requirement of the law, shall not, his uncircumcision, as circumcision be reckoned?—
text.DRB: If then the prepuce keep the justices of the Law; shall not his prepuce be reputed for circumcision?
text.KJV: Therefore if the uncircumcision keep the righteousness of the law, shall not his uncircumcision be counted for circumcision?
text.NABRE: Again, if an uncircumcised man keeps the precepts of the law, will he not be considered circumcised?

text.NRSV: Then those who are physically uncircumcised but keep the law will condemn you that have the written code and circumcision but break the law.
text.Rotherham: And the uncircumcision by nature, completing the law, shall judge, thee, who, notwithstanding letter and circumcision, art a transgressor of law!
text.DRB: and shall not that which of nature is prepuce, fulfilling the Law, judge thee, that by the letter and circumcision art a prevaricator of the Law?
text.KJV: And shall not uncircumcision which is by nature, if it fulfil the law, judge thee, who by the letter and circumcision dost transgress the law?
text.NABRE: Indeed, those who are physically uncircumcised but carry out the law will pass judgment on you, with your written law and circumcision, who break the law.

text.NRSV: For a person is not a Jew who is one outwardly, nor is true circumcision something external and physical.
text.Rotherham: For, not he who is one in appearance, is a Jew, nor is, that which is such in appearance in flesh, circumcision;
text.DRB: For not he that is in open shew, is a Jew, nor that which is in open shew, in the flesh, is circumcision:
text.KJV: For he is not a Jew, which is one outwardly; neither is that circumcision, which is outward in the flesh:
text.NABRE: One is not a Jew outwardly. True circumcision is not outward, in the flesh.

text.NRSV: Rather, a person is a Jew who is one inwardly, and real circumcision is a matter of the heart—it is spiritual and not literal. Such a person receives praise not from others but from God.
text.Rotherham: But, he who is one in secret, is a Jew,—and, [that is], circumcision, which is of the heart, in spirit, not in letter,—whose, praise, is not of men, but of God.
text.DRB: but he that is in secret, is a Jew; and the circumcision of the heart, in spirit, not in the letter: whose praise is not of men, but of God.
text.KJV: But he is a Jew, which is one inwardly; and circumcision is that of the heart, in the spirit, and not in the letter; whose praise is not of men, but of God.
text.NABRE: Rather, one is a Jew inwardly, and circumcision is of the heart, in the spirit, not the letter; his praise is not from human beings but from God.

text.NRSV: Then what advantage has the Jew? Or what is the value of circumcision?
text.Rotherham: What, then, is the preeminence of the Jew? Or what, the profit of his circumcision?
text.DRB: What preeminence then hath the Jew, or what is the profit of circumcision?
text.KJV: What advantage then hath the Jew? or what profit is there of circumcision?
text.NABRE: Chapter 3 Answers to Objections. What advantage is there then in being a Jew? Or what is the value of circumcision?

text.NRSV: Much in every way. To begin with, the Jews were entrusted with the oracles of God.
text.Rotherham: Much, every way:—First, indeed, that they were entrusted with the oracles of God.
text.DRB: Much by all means. First surely because the words of God were committed to them.
text.KJV: Much every way: chiefly, because that unto them were committed the oracles of God.
text.NABRE: Much, in every respect. [For] in the first place, they were entrusted with the utterances of God.

text.NRSV: What if some were unfaithful? Will their unfaithfulness nullify the faithfulness of God?
text.Rotherham: For what? If some distrusted, shall their distrust make, the trust of God, void?
text.DRB: For what if certain of them have not believed? Hath their incredulity made the faith of God frustrate?
text.KJV: For what if some did not believe? shall their unbelief make the faith of God without effect?
text.NABRE: What if some were unfaithful? Will their infidelity nullify the fidelity of God?

text.NRSV: By no means! Although everyone is a liar, let God be proved true, as it is written, “So that you may be justified in your words, and prevail in your judging.”
text.Rotherham: Far be it! But let, God, prove to be, true, albeit, every man, be false! Even as it is written—That thou mightest be declared righteous in thy words, and overcome when thou art in judgment.
text.DRB: God forbid. But God is true, & every man a liar, as it is written: That thou mayest be justified in thy words, and overcome when thou art judged.
text.KJV: God forbid: yea, let God be true, but every man a liar; as it is written, That thou mightest be justified in thy sayings, and mightest overcome when thou art judged.
text.NABRE: Of course not! God must be true, though every human being is a liar, as it is written: “That you may be justified in your words, and conquer when you are judged.”

text.NRSV: But if our injustice serves to confirm the justice of God, what should we say? That God is unjust to inflict wrath on us? (I am speaking in a human way.)
text.Rotherham: But, if, our unrighteousness, commendeth, God’s righteousness, what shall we say? Surely, not unrighteous, is God who visiteth with his anger? After the manner of men, I am speaking,—
text.DRB: But if our iniquity commend the justice of God, what shall we say? Is God unjust that executeth wrath? (I speak according to man)
text.KJV: But if our unrighteousness commend the righteousness of God, what shall we say? Is God unrighteous who taketh vengeance? (I speak as a man)
text.NABRE: But if our wickedness provides proof of God’s righteousness, what can we say? Is God unjust, humanly speaking, to inflict his wrath?

text.NRSV: By no means! For then how could God judge the world?
text.Rotherham: Far be it! Else how shall God judge the world?
text.DRB: God forbid; otherwise how shall God judge this world?
text.KJV: God forbid: for then how shall God judge the world?
text.NABRE: Of course not! For how else is God to judge the world?

text.NRSV: But if through my falsehood God’s truthfulness abounds to his glory, why am I still being condemned as a sinner?
text.Rotherham: But, if, the truth of God, by my falsehood, hath the more abounded unto his glory, why, any longer, am, even I, as a sinner, to be judged?
text.DRB: For if the verity of God hath abounded in my lie, unto his glory, why am I also yet judged as a sinner,
text.KJV: For if the truth of God hath more abounded through my lie unto his glory; why yet am I also judged as a sinner?
text.NABRE: But if God’s truth redounds to his glory through my falsehood, why am I still being condemned as a sinner?

text.NRSV: And why not say (as some people slander us by saying that we say), “Let us do evil so that good may come”? Their condemnation is deserved!
text.Rotherham: And why not, according as we are injuriously charged, [and] according as some affirm that we say, Let us do the bad things, that the good ones may come? whose sentence is, just.
text.DRB: and not (as we are blasphemed, and as some report us to say) let us do evil, that there may come good? whose damnation is just.
text.KJV: And not rather, (as we be slanderously reported, and as some affirm that we say,) Let us do evil, that good may come? whose damnation is just.
text.NABRE: And why not say—as we are accused and as some claim we say—that we should do evil that good may come of it? Their penalty is what they deserve. Universal Bondage to Sin.

text.NRSV: What then? Are we any better off? No, not at all; for we have already charged that all, both Jews and Greeks, are under the power of sin,
text.Rotherham: What then? do we screen ourselves? Not at all! For we have before accused both Jews and Greeks of being, all under sin;
text.DRB: What then? do we excel them? No, not so. For we have argued the Jews and the Greeks, all to be under sin;
text.KJV: What then? are we better than they? No, in no wise: for we have before proved both Jews and Gentiles, that they are all under sin;
text.NABRE: Well, then, are we better off? Not entirely, for we have already brought the charge against Jews and Greeks alike that they are all under the domination of sin,

text.NRSV: as it is written: “There is no one who is righteous, not even one;
text.Rotherham: Even as it is written—there is none righteous, not even one,
text.DRB: as it is written: That there is not any man just,
text.KJV: As it is written, There is none righteous, no, not one:
text.NABRE: as it is written: “There is no one just, not one,

text.NRSV: there is no one who has understanding, there is no one who seeks God.
text.Rotherham: There is none that discerneth, There is none that seeketh out God:
text.DRB: there is not that understandeth, there is not that seeketh after God.
text.KJV: There is none that understandeth, there is none that seeketh after God.
text.NABRE: there is no one who understands, there is no one who seeks God.

text.NRSV: All have turned aside, together they have become worthless; there is no one who shows kindness, there is not even one.”
text.Rotherham: All, have turned aside, together, have become useless, There is none that doeth kindness, not so much as one:
text.DRB: All have declined, they are become unprofitable together: there is not that doeth good, there is not so much as one.
text.KJV: They are all gone out of the way, they are together become unprofitable; there is none that doeth good, no, not one.
text.NABRE: All have gone astray; all alike are worthless; there is not one who does good, [there is not] even one.

text.NRSV: “Their throats are opened graves; they use their tongues to deceive.” “The venom of vipers is under their lips.”
text.Rotherham: A sepulchre opened, is their throat, with their tongues, have they used deceit, the poison of asps, is under their lips,
text.DRB: Their throat is an open sepulchre, with their tongues they dealt deceitfully. The venom of asps under their lips.
text.KJV: Their throat is an open sepulchre; with their tongues they have used deceit; the poison of asps is under their lips:
text.NABRE: Their throats are open graves; they deceive with their tongues; the venom of asps is on their lips;

text.NRSV: “Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness.”
text.Rotherham: Whose mouth, of cursing and bitterness, is full,
text.DRB: Whose mouth is full of malediction and bitterness:
text.KJV: Whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness:
text.NABRE: their mouths are full of bitter cursing.

text.NRSV: “Their feet are swift to shed blood;
text.Rotherham: Swift, are their feet to shed blood;
text.DRB: Their feet swift to shed blood.
text.KJV: Their feet are swift to shed blood:
text.NABRE: Their feet are quick to shed blood;

text.NRSV: ruin and misery are in their paths,
text.Rotherham: Destruction and misery, are in their ways,
text.DRB: Destruction & infelicity in their ways:
text.KJV: Destruction and misery are in their ways:
text.NABRE: ruin and misery are in their ways,

text.NRSV: and the way of peace they have not known.”
text.Rotherham: And, the way of peace, have they not known:
text.DRB: and the way of peace they have not known.
text.KJV: And the way of peace have they not known:
text.NABRE: and the way of peace they know not.

text.NRSV: “There is no fear of God before their eyes.”
text.Rotherham: There is no fear of God before their eyes.
text.DRB: There is no fear of God before their eyes.
text.KJV: There is no fear of God before their eyes.
text.NABRE: There is no fear of God before their eyes.”

text.NRSV: Now we know that whatever the law says, it speaks to those who are under the law, so that every mouth may be silenced, and the whole world may be held accountable to God.
text.Rotherham: Now we know that, whatsoever things the law saith, to them who are within the law, it speaketh, in order that, every mouth, may be stopped and all the world come, under penal sentence, unto God.
text.DRB: And we know that whatsoever the Law speaketh, to them it speaketh that are in the Law; that every mouth may be stopped, & all the world may be made subject to God:
text.KJV: Now we know that what things soever the law saith, it saith to them who are under the law: that every mouth may be stopped, and all the world may become guilty before God.
text.NABRE: Now we know that what the law says is addressed to those under the law, so that every mouth may be silenced and the whole world stand accountable to God,

text.NRSV: For “no human being will be justified in his sight” by deeds prescribed by the law, for through the law comes the knowledge of sin.
text.Rotherham: Inasmuch as, by works of law, shall no flesh be declared righteous before him,—through law, in fact, is discovery of sin.
text.DRB: because by the works of the Law no flesh shall be justified before him. For by the Law is the knowledge of sin.
text.KJV: Therefore by the deeds of the law there shall no flesh be justified in his sight: for by the law is the knowledge of sin.
text.NABRE: since no human being will be justified in his sight by observing the law; for through the law comes consciousness of sin. III. Justification Through Faith in Christ Justification Apart from the Law.

text.NRSV: But now, apart from law, the righteousness of God has been disclosed, and is attested by the law and the prophets,
text.Rotherham: But now, apart from law, a righteousness of God hath been manifested, borne witness to by the law and the prophets,—
text.DRB: But now without the Law the justice of God is manifested; testified by the Law and the Prophets.
text.KJV: But now the righteousness of God without the law is manifested, being witnessed by the law and the prophets;
text.NABRE: But now the righteousness of God has been manifested apart from the law, though testified to by the law and the prophets,

text.NRSV: the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ for all who believe. For there is no distinction,
text.Rotherham: A righteousness of God, through faith in [Jesus] Christ, unto all that have faith; for there is no distinction,—
text.DRB: And the justice of God by faith of Jesus Christ, unto all and upon all that believe in him. For there is no distinction.
text.KJV: Even the righteousness of God which is by faith of Jesus Christ unto all and upon all them that believe: for there is no difference:
text.NABRE: the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ for all who believe. For there is no distinction;

text.NRSV: since all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God;
text.Rotherham: For, all, have sinned and fall short of the glory of God;
text.DRB: For all have sinned; and do need the glory of God.
text.KJV: For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God;
text.NABRE: all have sinned and are deprived of the glory of God.

text.NRSV: they are now justified by his grace as a gift, through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus,
text.Rotherham: Being declared righteous freely by his favour through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus:—
text.DRB: Justified gratis by his grace, by the redemption that is in Christ Jesus,
text.KJV: Being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus:
text.NABRE: They are justified freely by his grace through the redemption in Christ Jesus,

text.NRSV: whom God put forward as a sacrifice of atonement by his blood, effective through faith. He did this to show his righteousness, because in his divine forbearance he had passed over the sins previously committed;
text.Rotherham: Whom God hath set forth as a propitiatory covering, through faith in his blood, for a showing forth of his righteousness, by reason of the passing-by of the previously committed sins,
text.DRB: whom God hath proposed a propitiation, by faith in his blood, to the shewing of his justice, for the remission of former sins,
text.KJV: Whom God hath set forth to be a propitiation through faith in his blood, to declare his righteousness for the remission of sins that are past, through the forbearance of God;
text.NABRE: whom God set forth as an expiation, through faith, by his blood, to prove his righteousness because of the forgiveness of sins previously committed,

text.NRSV: it was to prove at the present time that he himself is righteous and that he justifies the one who has faith in Jesus.
text.Rotherham: In the forbearance of God,—with a view to a showing forth of his righteousness in the present season, that he might be righteous even when declaring righteous him that hath faith in Jesus.
text.DRB: in the toleration of God, to the shewing of his justice in this time: that he may be just, and justifying him that is of the faith of Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: To declare, I say, at this time his righteousness: that he might be just, and the justifier of him which believeth in Jesus.
text.NABRE: through the forbearance of God—to prove his righteousness in the present time, that he might be righteous and justify the one who has faith in Jesus.

text.NRSV: Then what becomes of boasting? It is excluded. By what law? By that of works? No, but by the law of faith.
text.Rotherham: Where, then, the boasting! It is excluded. Through what kind of law? Of works? Nay! but through a law of faith:
text.DRB: Where is then thy boasting? it is excluded. By what law? of deeds? No, but by the law of faith.
text.KJV: Where is boasting then? It is excluded. By what law? of works? Nay: but by the law of faith.
text.NABRE: What occasion is there then for boasting? It is ruled out. On what principle, that of works? No, rather on the principle of faith.

text.NRSV: For we hold that a person is justified by faith apart from works prescribed by the law.
text.Rotherham: For we reckon that a man is to be declared righteous by faith, apart from works of law.
text.DRB: For we account a man to be justified by faith without the works of the Law.
text.KJV: Therefore we conclude that a man is justified by faith without the deeds of the law.
text.NABRE: For we consider that a person is justified by faith apart from works of the law.

text.NRSV: Or is God the God of Jews only? Is he not the God of Gentiles also? Yes, of Gentiles also,
text.Rotherham: Or, [is God] the God, of Jews only, and not of the nations also? Yea! of the nations also:—
text.DRB: Is he God of the Jews only? is he not also of the Gentiles? Yes of the Gentiles also.
text.KJV: Is he the God of the Jews only? is he not also of the Gentiles? Yes, of the Gentiles also:
text.NABRE: Does God belong to Jews alone? Does he not belong to Gentiles, too? Yes, also to Gentiles,

text.NRSV: since God is one; and he will justify the circumcised on the ground of faith and the uncircumcised through that same faith.
text.Rotherham: If, at all events, God is one, who will declare righteous—the circumcision by faith, and the uncircumcision through their faith.
text.DRB: For it is one God, that justifieth circumcision by faith, and prepuce by faith.
text.KJV: Seeing it is one God, which shall justify the circumcision by faith, and uncircumcision through faith.
text.NABRE: for God is one and will justify the circumcised on the basis of faith and the uncircumcised through faith.

text.NRSV: Do we then overthrow the law by this faith? By no means! On the contrary, we uphold the law.
text.Rotherham: Do we then make, law, void through means of our faith? Far be it! On the contrary, law, we do establish!
text.DRB: Do we then destroy the Law by faith? God forbid, but we do establish the Law.
text.KJV: Do we then make void the law through faith? God forbid: yea, we establish the law.
text.NABRE: Are we then annulling the law by this faith? Of course not! On the contrary, we are supporting the law.

text.NRSV: What then are we to say was gained by Abraham, our ancestor according to the flesh?
text.Rotherham: What then shall we say—as touching Abraham our forefather?
text.DRB: What shall we say then that Abraham did find, our father according to the flesh?
text.KJV: What shall we say then that Abraham our father, as pertaining to the flesh, hath found?
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 Abraham Justified by Faith. What then can we say that Abraham found, our ancestor according to the flesh?

text.NRSV: For if Abraham was justified by works, he has something to boast about, but not before God.
text.Rotherham: For, if Abraham by works was declared righteous, he hath whereof to boast;—nevertheless, not towards God,—
text.DRB: For if Abraham were justified by works: he hath glory, but not with God.
text.KJV: For if Abraham were justified by works, he hath whereof to glory; but not before God.
text.NABRE: Indeed, if Abraham was justified on the basis of his works, he has reason to boast; but this was not so in the sight of God.

text.NRSV: For what does the scripture say? “Abraham believed God, and it was reckoned to him as righteousness.”
text.Rotherham: For what doth the Scripture say? And Abraham believed in God, and it was reckoned unto him as righteousness.
text.DRB: For what saith the Scripture? Abraham believed God, & it was reputed him to justice.
text.KJV: For what saith the scripture? Abraham believed God, and it was counted unto him for righteousness.
text.NABRE: For what does the scripture say? “Abraham believed God, and it was credited to him as righteousness.”

text.NRSV: Now to one who works, wages are not reckoned as a gift but as something due.
text.Rotherham: Now, unto him that worketh, the reward is not reckoned by way of favour but by way of obligation,
text.DRB: But to him that worketh, the reward is not imputed according to grace but according to debt.
text.KJV: Now to him that worketh is the reward not reckoned of grace, but of debt.
text.NABRE: A worker’s wage is credited not as a gift, but as something due.

text.NRSV: But to one who without works trusts him who justifies the ungodly, such faith is reckoned as righteousness.
text.Rotherham: Whereas, unto him that worketh not but believeth on him that declareth righteous the ungodly, his faith is reckoned as righteousness.
text.DRB: But to him that worketh not, yet believeth in him that justifieth the impious, his faith is reputed to justice according to the purpose of the grace of God.
text.KJV: But to him that worketh not, but believeth on him that justifieth the ungodly, his faith is counted for righteousness.
text.NABRE: But when one does not work, yet believes in the one who justifies the ungodly, his faith is credited as righteousness.

text.NRSV: So also David speaks of the blessedness of those to whom God reckons righteousness apart from works:
text.Rotherham: Just as David also affirmeth the happiness of the man unto whom God reckoneth righteousness apart from works:—
text.DRB: As David also termeth the blessedness of a man, to whom God reputeth justice without works:
text.KJV: Even as David also describeth the blessedness of the man, unto whom God imputeth righteousness without works,
text.NABRE: So also David declares the blessedness of the person to whom God credits righteousness apart from works:

text.NRSV: “Blessed are those whose iniquities are forgiven, and whose sins are covered;
text.Rotherham: Happy, they whose lawlessnesses have been forgiven and whose sins have been covered,
text.DRB: Blessed are they, whose iniquities be forgiven, and whose sins be covered.
text.KJV: Saying, Blessed are they whose iniquities are forgiven, and whose sins are covered.
text.NABRE: “Blessed are they whose iniquities are forgiven and whose sins are covered.

text.NRSV: blessed is the one against whom the Lord will not reckon sin.”
text.Rotherham: Happy, the man whose sin the Lord will in nowise reckon.
text.DRB: Blessed is the man to whom our Lord hath not imputed sin.
text.KJV: Blessed is the man to whom the Lord will not impute sin.
text.NABRE: Blessed is the man whose sin the Lord does not record.”

text.NRSV: Is this blessedness, then, only for the circumcised, or also for the uncircumcised? We say, “Faith was reckoned to Abraham as righteousness.”
text.Rotherham: This happiness, then, [is it] for the circumcision, or for the uncircumcision? for we say—His faith was reckoned unto Abraham as righteousness:
text.DRB: This blessedness then doth it abide in the circumcision, or in the prepuce also? For we say that unto Abraham faith was reputed to justice.
text.KJV: Cometh this blessedness then upon the circumcision only, or upon the uncircumcision also? for we say that faith was reckoned to Abraham for righteousness.
text.NABRE: Does this blessedness apply only to the circumcised, or to the uncircumcised as well? Now we assert that “faith was credited to Abraham as righteousness.”

text.NRSV: How then was it reckoned to him? Was it before or after he had been circumcised? It was not after, but before he was circumcised.
text.Rotherham: How, then, was it reckoned? When he was in circumcision or in uncircumcision? Not in circumcision, but in uncircumcision;
text.DRB: How was it reputed? in circumcision, or in prepuce? Not in circumcision, but in prepuce.
text.KJV: How was it then reckoned? when he was in circumcision, or in uncircumcision? Not in circumcision, but in uncircumcision.
text.NABRE: Under what circumstances was it credited? Was he circumcised or not? He was not circumcised, but uncircumcised.

text.NRSV: He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that he had by faith while he was still uncircumcised. The purpose was to make him the ancestor of all who believe without being circumcised and who thus have righteousness reckoned to them,
text.Rotherham: And, a sign, he received [namely] of circumcision, a seal of the righteousness of the faith which he had while yet uncircumcised; to the end he might be father of all that believe during uncircumcision, to the end [the same] righteousness might be reckoned unto them,—
text.DRB: And he received the sign of circumcision, a seal of the justice of faith that is in prepuce: that he might be the father of all that believe by the prepuce, that unto them also it may be reputed to justice:
text.KJV: And he received the sign of circumcision, a seal of the righteousness of the faith which he had yet being uncircumcised: that he might be the father of all them that believe, though they be not circumcised; that righteousness might be imputed unto them also:
text.NABRE: And he received the sign of circumcision as a seal on the righteousness received through faith while he was uncircumcised. Thus he was to be the father of all the uncircumcised who believe, so that to them [also] righteousness might be credited,

text.NRSV: and likewise the ancestor of the circumcised who are not only circumcised but who also follow the example of the faith that our ancestor Abraham had before he was circumcised.
text.Rotherham: And father of circumcision—unto them who are not of circumcision only, but who also walk in the steps of the faith, while yet uncircumcised, of our father Abraham.
text.DRB: and might be father of circumcision, not to them only that are of the circumcision, but to them also that follow the steps of the faith that is in the prepuce of our father Abraham.
text.KJV: And the father of circumcision to them who are not of the circumcision only, but who also walk in the steps of that faith of our father Abraham, which he had being yet uncircumcised.
text.NABRE: as well as the father of the circumcised who not only are circumcised but also follow the path of faith that our father Abraham walked while still uncircumcised.

text.NRSV: For the promise that he would inherit the world did not come to Abraham or to his descendants through the law but through the righteousness of faith.
text.Rotherham: For, not through means of law, doth the promise belong unto Abraham or unto his seed,—that he should be heir of the world; but, through a righteousness by faith.
text.DRB: For not by the Law was the promise to Abraham, or to his seed, that he should be heir of the world; but by the justice of faith.
text.KJV: For the promise, that he should be the heir of the world, was not to Abraham, or to his seed, through the law, but through the righteousness of faith.
text.NABRE: Inheritance Through Faith. It was not through the law that the promise was made to Abraham and his descendants that he would inherit the world, but through the righteousness that comes from faith.

text.NRSV: If it is the adherents of the law who are to be the heirs, faith is null and the promise is void.
text.Rotherham: For, if they who are of law are heirs, made void is faith and of no effect is the promise.
text.DRB: For if they that are of the Law, be heirs; faith is made void, the promise is abolished.
text.KJV: For if they which are of the law be heirs, faith is made void, and the promise made of none effect:
text.NABRE: For if those who adhere to the law are the heirs, faith is null and the promise is void.

text.NRSV: For the law brings wrath; but where there is no law, neither is there violation.
text.Rotherham: For, the law, worketh out anger, but, where there is no law, neither is there transgression.
text.DRB: For the Law worketh wrath. For where is no Law, neither is there prevarication.
text.KJV: Because the law worketh wrath: for where no law is, there is no transgression.
text.NABRE: For the law produces wrath; but where there is no law, neither is there violation.

text.NRSV: For this reason it depends on faith, in order that the promise may rest on grace and be guaranteed to all his descendants, not only to the adherents of the law but also to those who share the faith of Abraham (for he is the father of all of us,
text.Rotherham: For this cause, it is by faith, in order that it may be by way of favour, so that the promise is firm unto all the seed,—not unto that by the law only, but unto that also [which is such] by the faith of Abraham; who is father of us all,—
text.DRB: Therefore of faith: that according to grace the promise may be firm to all the seed, not to that only which is of the Law, but to that also which is of the faith of Abraham, who is the father of us all, (as it is written:
text.KJV: Therefore it is of faith, that it might be by grace; to the end the promise might be sure to all the seed; not to that only which is of the law, but to that also which is of the faith of Abraham; who is the father of us all,
text.NABRE: For this reason, it depends on faith, so that it may be a gift, and the promise may be guaranteed to all his descendants, not to those who only adhere to the law but to those who follow the faith of Abraham, who is the father of all of us,

text.NRSV: as it is written, “I have made you the father of many nations”)—in the presence of the God in whom he believed, who gives life to the dead and calls into existence the things that do not exist.
text.Rotherham: Even as it is written—Father of many nations, have I appointed thee: before him whom he believed—God, who causeth the dead to live, and calleth the things that are not as things that are:—
text.DRB: For, a father of many Nations have I appointed thee ) before God, whom thou didst believe, who quickeneth the dead; calleth those things that are not, as those things that are.
text.KJV: (As it is written, I have made thee a father of many nations,) before him whom he believed, even God, who quickeneth the dead, and calleth those things which be not as though they were.
text.NABRE: as it is written, “I have made you father of many nations.” He is our father in the sight of God, in whom he believed, who gives life to the dead and calls into being what does not exist.

text.NRSV: Hoping against hope, he believed that he would become “the father of many nations,” according to what was said, “So numerous shall your descendants be.”
text.Rotherham: Who, past hope, upon hope believed, so that he became father of many nations,—according to what had been said—So shall be thy seed;—
text.DRB: Who contrary to hope believed in hope; that he might be made the father of many nations, according to that which was said to him: So shall thy seed be, as the stars of Heaven, and the sand of the sea.
text.KJV: Who against hope believed in hope, that he might become the father of many nations; according to that which was spoken, So shall thy seed be.
text.NABRE: He believed, hoping against hope, that he would become “the father of many nations,” according to what was said, “Thus shall your descendants be.”

text.NRSV: He did not weaken in faith when he considered his own body, which was already as good as dead (for he was about a hundred years old), or when he considered the barrenness of Sarah’s womb.
text.Rotherham: And, without becoming weak in his faith, he attentively considered his own body, already deadened—he being a hundred years old, the deadening also of Sarah’s womb;
text.DRB: And he was not weakened in faith; neither did he consider his own body now quite dead, whereas he was almost an hundred years old, and the dead matrice of Sara.
text.KJV: And being not weak in faith, he considered not his own body now dead, when he was about an hundred years old, neither yet the deadness of Sara’s womb:
text.NABRE: He did not weaken in faith when he considered his own body as [already] dead (for he was almost a hundred years old) and the dead womb of Sarah.

text.NRSV: No distrust made him waver concerning the promise of God, but he grew strong in his faith as he gave glory to God,
text.Rotherham: In respect, however, of the promise of God, he was not led to hesitate by unbelief, but received power by his faith, giving glory unto God,
text.DRB: In the promise also of God he staggered not by distrust; but was strengthened in faith, giving glory to God:
text.KJV: He staggered not at the promise of God through unbelief; but was strong in faith, giving glory to God;
text.NABRE: He did not doubt God’s promise in unbelief; rather, he was empowered by faith and gave glory to God

text.NRSV: being fully convinced that God was able to do what he had promised.
text.Rotherham: And being fully persuaded (that),—what he hath promised, able is he also to perform:
text.DRB: most fully knowing that whatsoever he promised, he is able also to do.
text.KJV: And being fully persuaded that, what he had promised, he was able also to perform.
text.NABRE: and was fully convinced that what he had promised he was also able to do.

text.NRSV: Therefore it was “reckoned to him as righteousness.”
text.Rotherham: Wherefore [also], it was reckoned unto him as righteousness.
text.DRB: Therefore was it also reputed him to justice.
text.KJV: And therefore it was imputed to him for righteousness.
text.NABRE: That is why “it was credited to him as righteousness.”

text.NRSV: Now the words, “it was reckoned to him,” were written not for his sake alone,
text.Rotherham: Now it was not written for his sake alone that it was reckoned unto him,
text.DRB: And it is not written only for him, that it was reputed him to justice:
text.KJV: Now it was not written for his sake alone, that it was imputed to him;
text.NABRE: But it was not for him alone that it was written that “it was credited to him”;

text.NRSV: but for ours also. It will be reckoned to us who believe in him who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead,
text.Rotherham: But for our sakes also—unto whom it is to be reckoned,—even unto them that believe upon him who raised Jesus our Lord from among the dead:
text.DRB: but also for us, to whom it shall be reputed believing in him, that raised up Jesus Christ our Lord from the dead,
text.KJV: But for us also, to whom it shall be imputed, if we believe on him that raised up Jesus our Lord from the dead;
text.NABRE: it was also for us, to whom it will be credited, who believe in the one who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead,

text.NRSV: who was handed over to death for our trespasses and was raised for our justification.
text.Rotherham: Who was delivered up on account of our offences and was raised on account of the declaring us righteous.
text.DRB: who was delivered up for our sins, and rose again for our justification.
text.KJV: Who was delivered for our offences, and was raised again for our justification.
text.NABRE: who was handed over for our transgressions and was raised for our justification.

text.NRSV: Therefore, since we are justified by faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ,
text.Rotherham: Having, therefore, been declared righteous by faith, let us have, peace, towards God, through our Lord Jesus Christ,—
text.DRB: Being justified therefore by faith, let us have peace toward God by our Lord Jesus Christ;
text.KJV: Therefore being justified by faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ:
text.NABRE: Chapter 5 Faith, Hope, and Love. Therefore, since we have been justified by faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ,

text.NRSV: through whom we have obtained access to this grace in which we stand; and we boast in our hope of sharing the glory of God.
text.Rotherham: Through whom also we have had, our introduction, [by our faith] into this favour wherein we stand; and let us boast in hope of the glory of God.
text.DRB: by whom also we have access through faith into this grace wherein we stand, and glory, in the hope of the glory of the sons of God.
text.KJV: By whom also we have access by faith into this grace wherein we stand, and rejoice in hope of the glory of God.
text.NABRE: through whom we have gained access [by faith] to this grace in which we stand, and we boast in hope of the glory of God.

text.NRSV: And not only that, but we also boast in our sufferings, knowing that suffering produces endurance,
text.Rotherham: And, not only so, but let us boast also in our tribulations; knowing that, our tribulation, worketh out endurance.
text.DRB: And not only this; but also we glory in tribulations, knowing that tribulation worketh patience:
text.KJV: And not only so, but we glory in tribulations also: knowing that tribulation worketh patience;
text.NABRE: Not only that, but we even boast of our afflictions, knowing that affliction produces endurance,

text.NRSV: and endurance produces character, and character produces hope,
text.Rotherham: And, our endurance, a testing, and, our testing, hope,
text.DRB: and patience, probation; and probation, hope;
text.KJV: And patience, experience; and experience, hope:
text.NABRE: and endurance, proven character, and proven character, hope,

text.NRSV: and hope does not disappoint us, because God’s love has been poured into our hearts through the Holy Spirit that has been given to us.
text.Rotherham: And, our hope, putteth not to shame. Because, the love of God, hath been poured out in our hearts, through the Holy Spirit that hath been given unto us:
text.DRB: and hope confoundeth not: because the charity of God is poured forth in our hearts, by the Holy Ghost which is given us.
text.KJV: And hope maketh not ashamed; because the love of God is shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Ghost which is given unto us.
text.NABRE: and hope does not disappoint, because the love of God has been poured out into our hearts through the holy Spirit that has been given to us.

text.NRSV: For while we were still weak, at the right time Christ died for the ungodly.
text.Rotherham: Seeing that, Christ, we being weak as yet, seasonably, in behalf of such as were ungodly, died.
text.DRB: For why did Christ, when we as yet were weak, according to the time die for the impious?
text.KJV: For when we were yet without strength, in due time Christ died for the ungodly.
text.NABRE: For Christ, while we were still helpless, yet died at the appointed time for the ungodly.

text.NRSV: Indeed, rarely will anyone die for a righteous person—though perhaps for a good person someone might actually dare to die.
text.Rotherham: For, scarcely in behalf of a righteous man, will one die,—in behalf of the good man indeed, peradventure one even dareth to die;
text.DRB: For, scarce for a just man doth any die: for perhaps for a good man durst some man die.
text.KJV: For scarcely for a righteous man will one die: yet peradventure for a good man some would even dare to die.
text.NABRE: Indeed, only with difficulty does one die for a just person, though perhaps for a good person one might even find courage to die.

text.NRSV: But God proves his love for us in that while we still were sinners Christ died for us.
text.Rotherham: But God commendeth his own love unto us in that—we as yet being sinners, Christ in our behalf died.
text.DRB: But God commendeth his charity in us: because, when as yet we were sinners, Christ died for us.
text.KJV: But God commendeth his love toward us, in that, while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us.
text.NABRE: But God proves his love for us in that while we were still sinners Christ died for us.

text.NRSV: Much more surely then, now that we have been justified by his blood, will we be saved through him from the wrath of God.
text.Rotherham: Much more, then, having now been declared righteous by his blood, shall we be saved through him from the anger.
text.DRB: Much more therefore now being justified in his blood, shall we be saved from wrath by him.
text.KJV: Much more then, being now justified by his blood, we shall be saved from wrath through him.
text.NABRE: How much more then, since we are now justified by his blood, will we be saved through him from the wrath.

text.NRSV: For if while we were enemies, we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son, much more surely, having been reconciled, will we be saved by his life.
text.Rotherham: For, if being enemies we were reconciled unto God through the death of his Son, much more, having been reconciled, shall we be saved by his life.
text.DRB: For if, when we were enemies, we were reconciled to God by the death of his Son; much more being reconciled, shall we be saved in the life of him.
