text.KJV: For if, when we were enemies, we were reconciled to God by the death of his Son, much more, being reconciled, we shall be saved by his life.
text.NABRE: Indeed, if, while we were enemies, we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son, how much more, once reconciled, will we be saved by his life.

text.NRSV: But more than that, we even boast in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, through whom we have now received reconciliation.
text.Rotherham: And, not only, so, but are even boasting in God, through our Lord Jesus [Christ],—through whom, now, the reconciliation we have received.
text.DRB: And not only this; but also we glory in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom now we have received reconciliation.
text.KJV: And not only so, but we also joy in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom we have now received the atonement.
text.NABRE: Not only that, but we also boast of God through our Lord Jesus Christ, through whom we have now received reconciliation.

text.NRSV: Therefore, just as sin came into the world through one man, and death came through sin, and so death spread to all because all have sinned—
text.Rotherham: For this cause,—just as, through one man, sin into the world entered, and through sin, death,—and, so, unto all men death passed through, for that all had sinned;—
text.DRB: Therefore, as by one man sin entered into this world, and by sin death; and so unto all men death did pass, in which all sinned.
text.KJV: Wherefore, as by one man sin entered into the world, and death by sin; and so death passed upon all men, for that all have sinned:
text.NABRE: Humanity’s Sin Through Adam. Therefore, just as through one person sin entered the world, and through sin, death, and thus death came to all, inasmuch as all sinned —

text.NRSV: sin was indeed in the world before the law, but sin is not reckoned when there is no law.
text.Rotherham: For, until law, sin was in the world, although sin is not reckoned when there is no law,—
text.DRB: For even unto the Law sin was in the world: but sin was not imputed, when the Law was not.
text.KJV: (For until the law sin was in the world: but sin is not imputed when there is no law.
text.NABRE: for up to the time of the law, sin was in the world, though sin is not accounted when there is no law.

text.NRSV: Yet death exercised dominion from Adam to Moses, even over those whose sins were not like the transgression of Adam, who is a type of the one who was to come.
text.Rotherham: Yet still, death reigned from Adam until Moses, even over them who had not sinned after the likeness of the transgression of Adam,—who is a type of the Coming One;—
text.DRB: But death reigned from Adam unto Moyses, even on them also that sinned not after the similitude of the prevarication of Adam, who is a figure of him to come.
text.KJV: Nevertheless death reigned from Adam to Moses, even over them that had not sinned after the similitude of Adam’s transgression, who is the figure of him that was to come.
text.NABRE: But death reigned from Adam to Moses, even over those who did not sin after the pattern of the trespass of Adam, who is the type of the one who was to come.

text.NRSV: But the free gift is not like the trespass. For if the many died through the one man’s trespass, much more surely have the grace of God and the free gift in the grace of the one man, Jesus Christ, abounded for the many.
text.Rotherham: But, not as the fault, so, [also] the decree of favour, for, if, by the fault of the one, the many died, much more, the favour of God and the free-gift in favour, by the one man Jesus Christ, unto the many superabounded;
text.DRB: But not as the offence, so also the gift. For if by the offence of one, many died; much more the grace of God and the gift, in the grace of one man Jesus Christ, hath abounded upon many.
text.KJV: But not as the offence, so also is the free gift. For if through the offence of one many be dead, much more the grace of God, and the gift by grace, which is by one man, Jesus Christ, hath abounded unto many.
text.NABRE: Grace and Life Through Christ. But the gift is not like the transgression. For if by that one person’s transgression the many died, how much more did the grace of God and the gracious gift of the one person Jesus Christ overflow for the many.

text.NRSV: And the free gift is not like the effect of the one man’s sin. For the judgment following one trespass brought condemnation, but the free gift following many trespasses brings justification.
text.Rotherham: And, not as through one that sinned, is that which is freely given, for, the sentence of judgment, indeed, was—out of one [fault] into condemnation, whereas, the decree of favour, is—out of many faults, into a recovery of righteousness.
text.DRB: And not as by one sin, so also the gift. For judgement indeed is of one, to condemnation: but grace is of many offences, to justification.
text.KJV: And not as it was by one that sinned, so is the gift: for the judgment was by one to condemnation, but the free gift is of many offences unto justification.
text.NABRE: And the gift is not like the result of the one person’s sinning. For after one sin there was the judgment that brought condemnation; but the gift, after many transgressions, brought acquittal.

text.NRSV: If, because of the one man’s trespass, death exercised dominion through that one, much more surely will those who receive the abundance of grace and the free gift of righteousness exercise dominion in life through the one man, Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: For, if, by the fault of the one, death reigned through the one, much more, they who the superabundance of the favour and of [the free-gift of] the righteousness do receive, in life, shall reign through the one, Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: For if in the offence of one, death reigned by one; much more they that receive the aboundance of grace and of donation & of justice, shall reign in life by one, Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: For if by one man’s offence death reigned by one; much more they which receive abundance of grace and of the gift of righteousness shall reign in life by one, Jesus Christ.)
text.NABRE: For if, by the transgression of one person, death came to reign through that one, how much more will those who receive the abundance of grace and of the gift of justification come to reign in life through the one person Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: Therefore just as one man’s trespass led to condemnation for all, so one man’s act of righteousness leads to justification and life for all.
text.Rotherham: Hence then, as through one fault, [the sentence was] unto all men unto condemnation, so, also, through one recovery of righteousness, [the decree of favour] is unto all men for righteous acquittal unto life;
text.DRB: Therefore as by the offence of one, unto all men to condemnation; so also by the justice of one, unto all men to justification of life.
text.KJV: Therefore as by the offence of one judgment came upon all men to condemnation; even so by the righteousness of one the free gift came upon all men unto justification of life.
text.NABRE: In conclusion, just as through one transgression condemnation came upon all, so through one righteous act acquittal and life came to all.

text.NRSV: For just as by the one man’s disobedience the many were made sinners, so by the one man’s obedience the many will be made righteous.
text.Rotherham: For, just as, through the disobedience of the one man, sinners, the many were constituted, so, also, through the obedience of the one, righteous, the many shall be constituted—
text.DRB: For as by the disobedience of one man, many were made sinners; so also by the obedience of one, many shall be made just.
text.KJV: For as by one man’s disobedience many were made sinners, so by the obedience of one shall many be made righteous.
text.NABRE: For just as through the disobedience of one person the many were made sinners, so through the obedience of one the many will be made righteous.

text.NRSV: But law came in, with the result that the trespass multiplied; but where sin increased, grace abounded all the more,
text.Rotherham: Law, however, gained admission, in order that the fault might abound, but, where the sin abounded, the favour greatly superabounded:
text.DRB: But the Law entered in, that sin might abound. And where sin abounded, grace did more abound.
text.KJV: Moreover the law entered, that the offence might abound. But where sin abounded, grace did much more abound:
text.NABRE: The law entered in so that transgression might increase but, where sin increased, grace overflowed all the more,

text.NRSV: so that, just as sin exercised dominion in death, so grace might also exercise dominion through justification leading to eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.
text.Rotherham: In order that—just as sin reigned in death, so, also, favour, might reign through righteousness unto life age-abiding, through Jesus Christ our Lord.
text.DRB: That as sin reigned to death; so also grace may reign by justice to life everlasting, through Jesus Christ our Lord.
text.KJV: That as sin hath reigned unto death, even so might grace reign through righteousness unto eternal life by Jesus Christ our Lord.
text.NABRE: so that, as sin reigned in death, grace also might reign through justification for eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.

text.NRSV: What then are we to say? Should we continue in sin in order that grace may abound?
text.Rotherham: What then shall we say? are we still to continue in sin that favour may abound?
text.DRB: What shall we say then? Shall we continue in sin that grace may abound?
text.KJV: What shall we say then? Shall we continue in sin, that grace may abound?
text.NABRE: IV. Justification and the Christian Life Chapter 6 Freedom from Sin; Life in God. What then shall we say? Shall we persist in sin that grace may abound? Of course not!

text.NRSV: By no means! How can we who died to sin go on living in it?
text.Rotherham: Far be it! we who have died unto sin, how, any longer, shall we live therein?
text.DRB: God forbid. For we that are dead to sin, how shall we yet live therein?
text.KJV: God forbid. How shall we, that are dead to sin, live any longer therein?
text.NABRE: How can we who died to sin yet live in it?

text.NRSV: Do you not know that all of us who have been baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?
text.Rotherham: Or know ye not that, we, as many as were immersed into Christ [Jesus], into his death were immersed?
text.DRB: Are you ignorant that all we which are baptized in Christ Jesus, in his death we are baptized?
text.KJV: Know ye not, that so many of us as were baptized into Jesus Christ were baptized into his death?
text.NABRE: Or are you unaware that we who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?

text.NRSV: Therefore we have been buried with him by baptism into death, so that, just as Christ was raised from the dead by the glory of the Father, so we too might walk in newness of life.
text.Rotherham: We were, therefore, buried together with him through our immersion into his death, in order that—just as Christ was raised from among the dead through the glory of the Father, so, we also, in newness of life should walk.
text.DRB: For we are buried together with him by Baptism into death: that as Christ is risen from the dead by the glory of the Father, so we also may walk in newness of life.
text.KJV: Therefore we are buried with him by baptism into death: that like as Christ was raised up from the dead by the glory of the Father, even so we also should walk in newness of life.
text.NABRE: We were indeed buried with him through baptism into death, so that, just as Christ was raised from the dead by the glory of the Father, we too might live in newness of life.

text.NRSV: For if we have been united with him in a death like his, we will certainly be united with him in a resurrection like his.
text.Rotherham: For, if we have come to be grown together in the likeness of his death, certainly, in that of his resurrection also, shall we be.
text.DRB: For if we be become complanted to the similitude of his death, we shall be also of his resurrection.
text.KJV: For if we have been planted together in the likeness of his death, we shall be also in the likeness of his resurrection:
text.NABRE: For if we have grown into union with him through a death like his, we shall also be united with him in the resurrection.

text.NRSV: We know that our old self was crucified with him so that the body of sin might be destroyed, and we might no longer be enslaved to sin.
text.Rotherham: Of this taking note—that, our old man, was crucified together with him in order that the sinful body might be made powerless, that we should, no longer, be in servitude to sin;
text.DRB: Knowing this, that our old man is crucified with him, that the body of sin may be destroyed, to the end that we may serve sin no longer.
text.KJV: Knowing this, that our old man is crucified with him, that the body of sin might be destroyed, that henceforth we should not serve sin.
text.NABRE: We know that our old self was crucified with him, so that our sinful body might be done away with, that we might no longer be in slavery to sin.

text.NRSV: For whoever has died is freed from sin.
text.Rotherham: For, he that hath died, hath become righteously acquitted from his sin.
text.DRB: For he that is dead, is justified from sin.
text.KJV: For he that is dead is freed from sin.
text.NABRE: For a dead person has been absolved from sin.

text.NRSV: But if we have died with Christ, we believe that we will also live with him.
text.Rotherham: Now, if we have died together with Christ, we believe that we shall also live together with him;
text.DRB: And if we be dead with Christ, we believe that we shall live also together with Christ.
text.KJV: Now if we be dead with Christ, we believe that we shall also live with him:
text.NABRE: If, then, we have died with Christ, we believe that we shall also live with him.

text.NRSV: We know that Christ, being raised from the dead, will never die again; death no longer has dominion over him.
text.Rotherham: Knowing that, Christ having been raised from among the dead, no more dieth,—death, over him, no more, hath lordship,—
text.DRB: Knowing that Christ rising again from the dead, now dieth no more, death shall no more have dominion over him.
text.KJV: Knowing that Christ being raised from the dead dieth no more; death hath no more dominion over him.
text.NABRE: We know that Christ, raised from the dead, dies no more; death no longer has power over him.

text.NRSV: The death he died, he died to sin, once for all; but the life he lives, he lives to God.
text.Rotherham: For, in that he died, unto sin, died he once for all, but, in that he liveth, he liveth unto God.
text.DRB: For that he died, to sin he died once: but that he liveth, he liveth to God.
text.KJV: For in that he died, he died unto sin once: but in that he liveth, he liveth unto God.
text.NABRE: As to his death, he died to sin once and for all; as to his life, he lives for God.

text.NRSV: So you also must consider yourselves dead to sin and alive to God in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: So, ye also, be reckoning yourselves to be—dead indeed unto sin, but, alive unto God in Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: So think you also, that you are dead to sin, but alive to God in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.KJV: Likewise reckon ye also yourselves to be dead indeed unto sin, but alive unto God through Jesus Christ our Lord.
text.NABRE: Consequently, you too must think of yourselves as [being] dead to sin and living for God in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: Therefore, do not let sin exercise dominion in your mortal bodies, to make you obey their passions.
text.Rotherham: Let not sin, therefore, reign in your death-doomed body, that ye should be obedient to its covetings;
text.DRB: Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body, that you obey the concupiscences thereof.
text.KJV: Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body, that ye should obey it in the lusts thereof.
text.NABRE: Therefore, sin must not reign over your mortal bodies so that you obey their desires.

text.NRSV: No longer present your members to sin as instruments of wickedness, but present yourselves to God as those who have been brought from death to life, and your members to God as instruments of righteousness.
text.Rotherham: Neither be presenting your members as weapons of unrighteousness unto sin, but present yourselves unto God as though alive from among the dead, and your members as weapons of righteousness unto God;
text.DRB: But neither do ye exhibit your members instruments of iniquity unto sin: but exhibit yourselves to God as of dead men, alive; and your members instruments of justice to God.
text.KJV: Neither yield ye your members as instruments of unrighteousness unto sin: but yield yourselves unto God, as those that are alive from the dead, and your members as instruments of righteousness unto God.
text.NABRE: And do not present the parts of your bodies to sin as weapons for wickedness, but present yourselves to God as raised from the dead to life and the parts of your bodies to God as weapons for righteousness.

text.NRSV: For sin will have no dominion over you, since you are not under law but under grace.
text.Rotherham: For, sin, over you, shall not have lordship, for ye are not under law, but under favour.
text.DRB: For sin shall not have dominion over you. For you are not under the Law, but under grace.
text.KJV: For sin shall not have dominion over you: for ye are not under the law, but under grace.
text.NABRE: For sin is not to have any power over you, since you are not under the law but under grace.

text.NRSV: What then? Should we sin because we are not under law but under grace? By no means!
text.Rotherham: What then? Shall we sin, because we are not under law but under favour? Far be it!
text.DRB: What then? shall we sin, because we are not under the Law, but under grace? God forbid.
text.KJV: What then? shall we sin, because we are not under the law, but under grace? God forbid.
text.NABRE: What then? Shall we sin because we are not under the law but under grace? Of course not!

text.NRSV: Do you not know that if you present yourselves to anyone as obedient slaves, you are slaves of the one whom you obey, either of sin, which leads to death, or of obedience, which leads to righteousness?
text.Rotherham: Know ye not that, unto whom ye are presenting yourselves as servants for obedience, servants ye are unto [him unto] whom ye are obedient, whether of sin unto death or of obedience unto righteousness?
text.DRB: Know you not that to whom you exhibit yourselves servants to obey, you are the servants of him whom you obey, whether it be of sin, to death, or of obedience, to justice.
text.KJV: Know ye not, that to whom ye yield yourselves servants to obey, his servants ye are to whom ye obey; whether of sin unto death, or of obedience unto righteousness?
text.NABRE: Do you not know that if you present yourselves to someone as obedient slaves, you are slaves of the one you obey, either of sin, which leads to death, or of obedience, which leads to righteousness?

text.NRSV: But thanks be to God that you, having once been slaves of sin, have become obedient from the heart to the form of teaching to which you were entrusted,
text.Rotherham: But thanks be unto God, that—whereas ye were servants of sin, ye became obedient out of the heart unto the mould of teaching into which ye were delivered;
text.DRB: But thanks be to God, that you were the servants of sin, but have obeyed from the heart, unto that form of doctrine, into the which you have been delivered.
text.KJV: But God be thanked, that ye were the servants of sin, but ye have obeyed from the heart that form of doctrine which was delivered you.
text.NABRE: But thanks be to God that, although you were once slaves of sin, you have become obedient from the heart to the pattern of teaching to which you were entrusted.

text.NRSV: and that you, having been set free from sin, have become slaves of righteousness.
text.Rotherham: And, being freed from sin, ye were made servants unto righteousness;—
text.DRB: And being made free from sin, you were made servants to justice.
text.KJV: Being then made free from sin, ye became the servants of righteousness.
text.NABRE: Freed from sin, you have become slaves of righteousness.

text.NRSV: I am speaking in human terms because of your natural limitations. For just as you once presented your members as slaves to impurity and to greater and greater iniquity, so now present your members as slaves to righteousness for sanctification.
text.Rotherham: In human fashion am I speaking, because of the weakness of your flesh;—for, just as ye presented your members as servants unto impurity and unto lawlessness [for lawlessness], so now, present ye your members as servants unto righteousness for sanctification.
text.DRB: I speak an human thing, because of the infirmity of your flesh. For as you have exhibited your members to serve uncleanness and iniquity, unto iniquity; so now exhibit your members to serve justice, unto sanctification.
text.KJV: I speak after the manner of men because of the infirmity of your flesh: for as ye have yielded your members servants to uncleanness and to iniquity unto iniquity; even so now yield your members servants to righteousness unto holiness.
text.NABRE: I am speaking in human terms because of the weakness of your nature. For just as you presented the parts of your bodies as slaves to impurity and to lawlessness for lawlessness, so now present them as slaves to righteousness for sanctification.

text.NRSV: When you were slaves of sin, you were free in regard to righteousness.
text.Rotherham: For, when ye were servants of sin, ye were free as to righteousness;—
text.DRB: For when you were servants of sin, you were free to justice.
text.KJV: For when ye were the servants of sin, ye were free from righteousness.
text.NABRE: For when you were slaves of sin, you were free from righteousness.

text.NRSV: So what advantage did you then get from the things of which you are now ashamed? The end of those things is death.
text.Rotherham: What fruit, therefore, had ye then—in things for which ye now are taking shame to yourselves? For, the end of those things, is death.
text.DRB: What fruit therefore had you then in those things, for which now you are ashamed? For the end of them is death.
text.KJV: What fruit had ye then in those things whereof ye are now ashamed? for the end of those things is death.
text.NABRE: But what profit did you get then from the things of which you are now ashamed? For the end of those things is death.

text.NRSV: But now that you have been set free from sin and have become slaves of God, the advantage you get is sanctification. The end is eternal life.
text.Rotherham: Whereas, now, having been freed from sin, and made servants unto God, ye have your fruit for sanctification and, the end, life age-abiding.
text.DRB: But now being made free from sin, and become servants to God, you have your fruit unto sanctification, but the end, life everlasting.
text.KJV: But now being made free from sin, and become servants to God, ye have your fruit unto holiness, and the end everlasting life.
text.NABRE: But now that you have been freed from sin and have become slaves of God, the benefit that you have leads to sanctification, and its end is eternal life.

text.NRSV: For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.Rotherham: For, the wages of sin, is death; but, God’s gift of favour, is life age-abiding, in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.DRB: For the stipends of sin, death. But the grace of God, life everlasting in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.KJV: For the wages of sin is death; but the gift of God is eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.
text.NABRE: For the wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.

text.NRSV: Do you not know, brothers and sisters—for I am speaking to those who know the law—that the law is binding on a person only that person is alive?
text.Rotherham: Or are ye ignorant, brethren,—for unto them that understand the law am I speaking, that, the law, hath lordship over a men as long as he liveth?
text.DRB: Are you ignorant, brethren, (for I speak to them that know the Law) that the Law hath dominion over a man as long time as he liveth?
text.KJV: Know ye not, brethren, (for I speak to them that know the law,) how that the law hath dominion over a man as long as he liveth?
text.NABRE: Chapter 7 Freedom from the Law. Are you unaware, brothers (for I am speaking to people who know the law), that the law has jurisdiction over one as long as one lives?

text.NRSV: Thus a married woman is bound by the law to her husband as long as he lives; but if her husband dies, she is discharged from the law concerning the husband.
text.Rotherham: For, the married woman, unto her living husband is bound by law; but, if her husband have died, she hath received a full release from the law of her husband.
text.DRB: For the woman that is under a husband; her husband living is bound to the law. But if her husband be dead, she is loosed from the law of her husband.
text.KJV: For the woman which hath an husband is bound by the law to her husband so long as he liveth; but if the husband be dead, she is loosed from the law of her husband.
text.NABRE: Thus a married woman is bound by law to her living husband; but if her husband dies, she is released from the law in respect to her husband.

text.NRSV: Accordingly, she will be called an adulteress if she lives with another man while her husband is alive. But if her husband dies, she is free from that law, and if she marries another man, she is not an adulteress.
text.Rotherham: Hence then, her husband being alive, an adulteress, shall she be called—if she become another man’s, but, if the husband have died, she is free from the law; so that she is not an adulteress, though she become another man’s.
text.DRB: Therefore her husband living, she shall be called an adulteress if she be with another man: but if her husband be dead she is delivered from the law of her husband: so that she is not and adulteress if she be with another man.
text.KJV: So then if, while her husband liveth, she be married to another man, she shall be called an adulteress: but if her husband be dead, she is free from that law; so that she is no adulteress, though she be married to another man.
text.NABRE: Consequently, while her husband is alive she will be called an adulteress if she consorts with another man. But if her husband dies she is free from that law, and she is not an adulteress if she consorts with another man.

text.NRSV: In the same way, my friends, you have died to the law through the body of Christ, so that you may belong to another, to him who has been raised from the dead in order that we may bear fruit for God.
text.Rotherham: So, then, my brethren, ye also, were made dead unto the law through the body of the Christ, to the end ye might become another’s—his who from among the dead was raised, in order that we might bring forth fruit unto God.
text.DRB: Therefore my brethren you also are made dead to the Law by the body of Christ; that you may be another man's who is risen again from the dead, that we may fructify to God.
text.KJV: Wherefore, my brethren, ye also are become dead to the law by the body of Christ; that ye should be married to another, even to him who is raised from the dead, that we should bring forth fruit unto God.
text.NABRE: In the same way, my brothers, you also were put to death to the law through the body of Christ, so that you might belong to another, to the one who was raised from the dead in order that we might bear fruit for God.

text.NRSV: While we were living in the flesh, our sinful passions, aroused by the law, were at work in our members to bear fruit for death.
text.Rotherham: For, when we were in the flesh, the susceptibilities of sins which were through the law, used to be energized in our members unto the bringing forth of fruit unto death;
text.DRB: For when we were in the flesh, the passions of sins that were by the Law, did work in our members, to fructify unto death.
text.KJV: For when we were in the flesh, the motions of sins, which were by the law, did work in our members to bring forth fruit unto death.
text.NABRE: For when we were in the flesh, our sinful passions, awakened by the law, worked in our members to bear fruit for death.

text.NRSV: But now we are discharged from the law, dead to that which held us captive, so that we are slaves not under the old written code but in the new life of the Spirit.
text.Rotherham: But, now, we have received full release from the law, by dying [in that] wherein we used to be held fast, so that we should be doing service—in newness of spirit and not in obsoleteness of letter.
text.DRB: But now we are loosed from the law of death wherein we were detained: in so much we serve in newness of spirit, and not in the oldness of the letter.
text.KJV: But now we are delivered from the law, that being dead wherein we were held; that we should serve in newness of spirit, and not in the oldness of the letter.
text.NABRE: But now we are released from the law, dead to what held us captive, so that we may serve in the newness of the spirit and not under the obsolete letter.

text.NRSV: What then should we say? That the law is sin? By no means! Yet, if it had not been for the law, I would not have known sin. I would not have known what it is to covet if the law had not said, “You shall not covet.”
text.Rotherham: What, then, shall we say? Is the law sin? Far be it! On the contrary, I had not discovered, sin, save through law, for even, of coveting, I had not been aware if, the law, had not kept on saying—Thou shall not covet;
text.DRB: What shall we say then? Is the Law sin? God forbid. But sin I did not know, but by the Law: for concupiscence I knew not, unless the Law did say: Thou shalt not covet.
text.KJV: What shall we say then? Is the law sin? God forbid. Nay, I had not known sin, but by the law: for I had not known lust, except the law had said, Thou shalt not covet.
text.NABRE: Acquaintance with Sin Through the Law. What then can we say? That the law is sin? Of course not! Yet I did not know sin except through the law, and I did not know what it is to covet except that the law said, “You shall not covet.”

text.NRSV: But sin, seizing an opportunity in the commandment, produced in me all kinds of covetousness. Apart from the law sin lies dead.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit sin taking, occasion—through the commandment, wrought out in me all manner of coveting; for, apart from law, sin is dead;—
text.DRB: But occasion being taken, sin by the commandment wrought in me all concupiscence. For without the Law sin was dead.
text.KJV: But sin, taking occasion by the commandment, wrought in me all manner of concupiscence. For without the law sin was dead.
text.NABRE: But sin, finding an opportunity in the commandment, produced in me every kind of covetousness. Apart from the law sin is dead.

text.NRSV: I was once alive apart from the law, but when the commandment came, sin revived
text.Rotherham: And, I, was alive, apart from law, at one time, but, the commandment coming, sin sprang up to life,
text.DRB: And I lived without the Law sometime. But when the commandment was come, sin revived.
text.KJV: For I was alive without the law once: but when the commandment came, sin revived, and I died.
text.NABRE: I once lived outside the law, but when the commandment came, sin became alive;

text.NRSV: and I died, and the very commandment that promised life proved to be death to me.
text.Rotherham: Whereas, I, died,—and the commandment which was unto life was found by me to be, itself, unto death;
text.DRB: And I was dead. And the commandment, that was unto life, the same to me was found to be unto death.
text.KJV: And the commandment, which was ordained to life, I found to be unto death.
text.NABRE: then I died, and the commandment that was for life turned out to be death for me.

text.NRSV: For sin, seizing an opportunity in the commandment, deceived me and through it killed me.
text.Rotherham: For, sin,—taking occasion—through the commandment, completely deceived me and, through it, slew me:
text.DRB: For sin taking occasion by the commandment, seduced me, and by it killed me.
text.KJV: For sin, taking occasion by the commandment, deceived me, and by it slew me.
text.NABRE: For sin, seizing an opportunity in the commandment, deceived me and through it put me to death.

text.NRSV: So the law is holy, and the commandment is holy and just and good.
text.Rotherham: So that, the law, indeed, is holy, and the commandment, holy, and righteous and good.
text.DRB: Therefore the Law indeed is holy, and the commandment holy, and just, and good.
text.KJV: Wherefore the law is holy, and the commandment holy, and just, and good.
text.NABRE: So then the law is holy, and the commandment is holy and righteous and good. Sin and Death.

text.NRSV: Did what is good, then, bring death to me? By no means! It was sin, working death in me through what is good, in order that sin might be shown to be sin, and through the commandment might become sinful beyond measure.
text.Rotherham: Did, then, that which is good, unto me become death? Far be it! But [it was] sin, that it might appear sin, through that which was good unto me working out death, in order that, exceeding sinful, might sin become through the commandment.
text.DRB: That then which is good, to me was it made death? God forbid. But sin, that it may appear sin, by the good thing wrought me death: that sin might become sinning above measure by the commandment.
text.KJV: Was then that which is good made death unto me? God forbid. But sin, that it might appear sin, working death in me by that which is good; that sin by the commandment might become exceeding sinful.
text.NABRE: Did the good, then, become death for me? Of course not! Sin, in order that it might be shown to be sin, worked death in me through the good, so that sin might become sinful beyond measure through the commandment.

text.NRSV: For we know that the law is spiritual; but I am of the flesh, sold into slavery under sin.
text.Rotherham: For we know that, the law, is spiritual,—I, however, am a creature of flesh, sold under sin;
text.DRB: For we know that the Law is spiritual, but I am carnal, sold under sin.
text.KJV: For we know that the law is spiritual: but I am carnal, sold under sin.
text.NABRE: We know that the law is spiritual; but I am carnal, sold into slavery to sin.

text.NRSV: I do not understand my own actions. For I do not do what I want, but I do the very thing I hate.
text.Rotherham: For, that which I am working out, I do not approve,—for not, what I wish, the same I practise, but, what I hate, the same I do:
text.DRB: For that which I work, I understand not. For not that which I will, the same do I, but which I hate, that I do.
text.KJV: For that which I do I allow not: for what I would, that do I not; but what I hate, that do I.
text.NABRE: What I do, I do not understand. For I do not do what I want, but I do what I hate.

text.NRSV: Now if I do what I do not want, I agree that the law is good.
text.Rotherham: Now, if what I wish not the same I do, I consent unto the law that [it is] right.
text.DRB: And if that which I will not, the same I do; I consent to the Law, that it is good.
text.KJV: If then I do that which I would not, I consent unto the law that it is good.
text.NABRE: Now if I do what I do not want, I concur that the law is good.

text.NRSV: But in fact it is no longer I that do it, but sin that dwells within me.
text.Rotherham: Now, however, no longer am, I, working it out, but the, sin, that dwelleth in me:
text.DRB: But now, not I work it any more, but the sin that dwelleth in me.
text.KJV: Now then it is no more I that do it, but sin that dwelleth in me.
text.NABRE: So now it is no longer I who do it, but sin that dwells in me.

text.NRSV: For I know that nothing good dwells within me, that is, in my flesh. I can will what is right, but I cannot do it.
text.Rotherham: I know, in fact, that there dwelleth not in me, that is, in my flesh, anything good; for, the wishing, lieth near me, but, the working out of what is right, not!
text.DRB: For I know that there dwelleth not in me, that is to say, in my flesh, good. For to will, is present with me, but to accomplish that which is good, I find not.
text.KJV: For I know that in me (that is, in my flesh,) dwelleth no good thing: for to will is present with me; but how to perform that which is good I find not.
text.NABRE: For I know that good does not dwell in me, that is, in my flesh. The willing is ready at hand, but doing the good is not.

text.NRSV: For I do not do the good I want, but the evil I do not want is what I do.
text.Rotherham: For not, the good that I wish, I do, but, the evil that I do not wish, the same I practise.
text.DRB: For not the good which I will, that do I; but the evil which I will not, that I do.
text.KJV: For the good that I would I do not: but the evil which I would not, that I do.
text.NABRE: For I do not do the good I want, but I do the evil I do not want.

text.NRSV: Now if I do what I do not want, it is no longer I that do it, but sin that dwells within me.
text.Rotherham: Now, if what I wish not, the same, I do, no longer am, I, working it out, but the, sin, that dwelleth in me.
text.DRB: And if that which I will not, the same I do: now not I work it, but the sin that dwelleth in me.
text.KJV: Now if I do that I would not, it is no more I that do it, but sin that dwelleth in me.
text.NABRE: Now if [I] do what I do not want, it is no longer I who do it, but sin that dwells in me.

text.NRSV: So I find it to be a law that when I want to do what is good, evil lies close at hand.
text.Rotherham: Hence, I find the law, to me who wish to be doing the right, that, unto me, the wrong lieth near:
text.DRB: Ifind therefore, the Law, to me having a will to do good, that evil is present with me.
text.KJV: I find then a law, that, when I would do good, evil is present with me.
text.NABRE: So, then, I discover the principle that when I want to do right, evil is at hand.

text.NRSV: For I delight in the law of God in my inmost self,
text.Rotherham: I have, in fact, a sympathetic pleasure in the law of God; according to the inner man.
text.DRB: For I am delighted with the Law of God according to the inward man:
text.KJV: For I delight in the law of God after the inward man:
text.NABRE: For I take delight in the law of God, in my inner self,

text.NRSV: but I see in my members another law at war with the law of my mind, making me captive to the law of sin that dwells in my members.
text.Rotherham: But I behold a diverse law in my members, warring against the law of my mind and taking me captive in the law of sin which existeth in my members:—
text.DRB: but I see another law in my members, repugning to the law of my mind, and captiving me in the law of sin that is in my members.
text.KJV: But I see another law in my members, warring against the law of my mind, and bringing me into captivity to the law of sin which is in my members.
text.NABRE: but I see in my members another principle at war with the law of my mind, taking me captive to the law of sin that dwells in my members.

text.NRSV: Wretched man that I am! Who will rescue me from this body of death?
text.Rotherham: Wretched, man am, I! Who shall rescue me out of this body doomed to death?
text.DRB: Unhappy man that I am, who shall deliver me from the body of this death?
text.KJV: O wretched man that I am! who shall deliver me from the body of this death?
text.NABRE: Miserable one that I am! Who will deliver me from this mortal body?

text.NRSV: Thanks be to God through Jesus Christ our Lord! So then, with my mind I am a slave to the law of God, but with my flesh I am a slave to the law of sin.
text.Rotherham: [But] thanks be unto God!—Through Jesus Christ our Lord. Hence, then,—I myself, with the mind, indeed, am in servitude unto a law of God; but; with the flesh; unto a law of sin.
text.DRB: The grace of God by Jesus Christ our Lord. Therefore I myself with the mind serve the law of God, but with the flesh, the law of sin.
text.KJV: I thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord. So then with the mind I myself serve the law of God; but with the flesh the law of sin.
text.NABRE: Thanks be to God through Jesus Christ our Lord. Therefore, I myself, with my mind, serve the law of God but, with my flesh, the law of sin.

text.NRSV: There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: Hence there is now, no, condemnation unto them who are in Christ Jesus;
text.DRB: There is now therefore no damnation to them that are in Christ Jesus; that walk not according to the flesh.
text.KJV: There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.
text.NABRE: Chapter 8 The Flesh and the Spirit. Hence, now there is no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and of death.
text.Rotherham: For, the law of the spirit of life in Christ Jesus, hath set thee free from the law of sin and of death;—
text.DRB: For the Law of the spirit of life in Christ Jesus, hath delivered me from the law of sin and of death.
text.KJV: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus hath made me free from the law of sin and death.
text.NABRE: For the law of the spirit of life in Christ Jesus has freed you from the law of sin and death.

text.NRSV: For God has done what the law, weakened by the flesh, could not do: by sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh, and to deal with sin, he condemned sin in the flesh,
text.Rotherham: For, what was impossible by the law in that it was weak through the flesh, God, by sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh and concerning sin, condemned sin in the flesh,
text.DRB: For that which was impossible to the Law, in that it was weakened by the flesh; God sending his Son in the similitude of the flesh of sin, even of sin damned sin in the flesh,
text.KJV: For what the law could not do, in that it was weak through the flesh, God sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh, and for sin, condemned sin in the flesh:
text.NABRE: For what the law, weakened by the flesh, was powerless to do, this God has done: by sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh and for the sake of sin, he condemned sin in the flesh,

text.NRSV: so that the just requirement of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not according to the flesh but according to the Spirit.
text.Rotherham: In order that, the righteous requirement of the law, might be fulfilled in us—who, not according to flesh, do walk, but according to spirit;
text.DRB: that the justification of the Law might be fulfilled in us who walk not according to the flesh, but according to the spirit.
text.KJV: That the righteousness of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.
text.NABRE: so that the righteous decree of the law might be fulfilled in us, who live not according to the flesh but according to the spirit.

text.NRSV: For those who live according to the flesh set their minds on the things of the flesh, but those who live according to the Spirit set their minds on the things of the Spirit.
text.Rotherham: For, they who according to flesh have their being, the things of the flesh do prefer, but, they according to the spirit, the things of the spirit;
text.DRB: For they that are according to the flesh, are affected to the things that are of the flesh; but they that are according to the spirit are affected to the things that are of the spirit.
text.KJV: For they that are after the flesh do mind the things of the flesh; but they that are after the Spirit the things of the Spirit.
text.NABRE: For those who live according to the flesh are concerned with the things of the flesh, but those who live according to the spirit with the things of the spirit.

text.NRSV: To set the mind on the flesh is death, but to set the mind on the Spirit is life and peace.
text.Rotherham: For, what is preferred by the flesh, [is] death, whereas, what is preferred by the spirit, [is] life and peace;—
text.DRB: For the wisdom of the flesh is death; but the wisdom of the spirit, life and peace.
text.KJV: For to be carnally minded is death; but to be spiritually minded is life and peace.
text.NABRE: The concern of the flesh is death, but the concern of the spirit is life and peace.

text.NRSV: For this reason the mind that is set on the flesh is hostile to God; it does not submit to God’s law—indeed it cannot,
text.Rotherham: Inasmuch as, what is preferred by the flesh, [is] hostile towards God, for, unto the law of God, it doth not submit itself, neither in fact can it.—
text.DRB: Because the wisdom of the flesh, is an enemy to God: for to the Law of Got it is not subject, neither can it be.
text.KJV: Because the carnal mind is enmity against God: for it is not subject to the law of God, neither indeed can be.
text.NABRE: For the concern of the flesh is hostility toward God; it does not submit to the law of God, nor can it;

text.NRSV: and those who are in the flesh cannot please God.
text.Rotherham: They, moreover, who in flesh have their being, cannot please, God.
text.DRB: And they that are in the flesh, cannot please God.
text.KJV: So then they that are in the flesh cannot please God.
text.NABRE: and those who are in the flesh cannot please God.

text.NRSV: But you are not in the flesh; you are in the Spirit, since the Spirit of God dwells in you. Anyone who does not have the Spirit of Christ does not belong to him.
text.Rotherham: But, ye, have not your being in flesh, but in spirit,—if at least, God’s Spirit, dwelleth in you; and, if anyone hath not Christ’s Spirit, the same, is not his;—
text.DRB: But you are not in the flesh, but in the spirit, yet if the Spirit of God dwell in you. But if any man have not the Spirit of Christ, the same is not his.
text.KJV: But ye are not in the flesh, but in the Spirit, if so be that the Spirit of God dwell in you. Now if any man have not the Spirit of Christ, he is none of his.
text.NABRE: But you are not in the flesh; on the contrary, you are in the spirit, if only the Spirit of God dwells in you. Whoever does not have the Spirit of Christ does not belong to him.

text.NRSV: But if Christ is in you, though the body is dead because of sin, the Spirit is life because of righteousness.
text.Rotherham: But, if Christ is in you, the body, indeed, is dead by reason of sin, whereas, the spirit, is life by reason of righteousness;
text.DRB: But if Christ be in you; the body indeed is dead because of sin, but the spirit liveth because of justification.
text.KJV: And if Christ be in you, the body is dead because of sin; but the Spirit is life because of righteousness.
text.NABRE: But if Christ is in you, although the body is dead because of sin, the spirit is alive because of righteousness.

text.NRSV: If the Spirit of him who raised Jesus from the dead dwells in you, he who raised Christ from the dead will give life to your mortal bodies also through his Spirit that dwells in you.
text.Rotherham: If, moreover, the Spirit of him that raised Jesus from among the dead dwelleth in you, he that raised from among the dead Christ Jesus, shall make alive [even] your death-doomed bodies, through means of his indwelling Spirit within you.
text.DRB: And if the Spirit of him that raised up Jesus from the dead, dwell in you; he that raised up Jesus Christ from the dead, shall quicken also your mortal bodies, because of his Spirit dwelling in you.
text.KJV: But if the Spirit of him that raised up Jesus from the dead dwell in you, he that raised up Christ from the dead shall also quicken your mortal bodies by his Spirit that dwelleth in you.
text.NABRE: If the Spirit of the one who raised Jesus from the dead dwells in you, the one who raised Christ from the dead will give life to your mortal bodies also, through his Spirit that dwells in you.

text.NRSV: So then, brothers and sisters, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live according to the flesh—
text.Rotherham: Hence, then, brethren—debtors, we are, not unto the flesh, that, according to flesh, we should live,—
text.DRB: Therefore brethren, we are debters, not to the flesh, to live according to the flesh.
text.KJV: Therefore, brethren, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live after the flesh.
text.NABRE: Consequently, brothers, we are not debtors to the flesh, to live according to the flesh.

text.NRSV: for if you live according to the flesh, you will die; but if by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live.
text.Rotherham: For, if according to flesh ye live, ye are about to die, whereas, if in spirit, the practices of the flesh, ye are putting to death, ye shall attain unto life;
text.DRB: For if you live according to the flesh, you shall die. But if by the spirit, you mortify the deeds of the flesh, you shall live.
text.KJV: For if ye live after the flesh, ye shall die: but if ye through the Spirit do mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live.
text.NABRE: For if you live according to the flesh, you will die, but if by the spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live. Children of God Through Adoption.

text.NRSV: For all who are led by the Spirit of God are children of God.
text.Rotherham: For, as many as by God’s Spirit are being led, the same, are God’s sons,—
text.DRB: For whosoever are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.
text.KJV: For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.
text.NABRE: For those who are led by the Spirit of God are children of God.

text.NRSV: For you did not receive a spirit of slavery to fall back into fear, but you have received a spirit of adoption. When we cry, “Abba! Father!”
text.Rotherham: For ye have not received a spirit of servitude, leading back into fear, but ye have received a spirit of sonship, whereby we are exclaiming—Abba! Oh Father!
text.DRB: For you have not received the spirit of servitude again in fear; but you have received the spirit of adoption of sons, wherein we cry: Abba, (Father).
text.KJV: For ye have not received the spirit of bondage again to fear; but ye have received the Spirit of adoption, whereby we cry, Abba, Father.
text.NABRE: For you did not receive a spirit of slavery to fall back into fear, but you received a spirit of adoption, through which we cry, “Abba, Father!”

text.NRSV: it is that very Spirit bearing witness with our spirit that we are children of God,
text.Rotherham: The Spirit itself, beareth witness together with our spirit that we are children of God;
text.DRB: For the Spirit himself, giveth testimony to our spirit, that we are the sons of God.
text.KJV: The Spirit itself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God:
text.NABRE: The Spirit itself bears witness with our spirit that we are children of God,

text.NRSV: and if children, then heirs, heirs of God and joint heirs with Christ—if, in fact, we suffer with him so that we may also be glorified with him.
text.Rotherham: And, if children, heirs also—heirs, indeed, of God, but co-heirs with Christ,—if, at least, we are suffering together, in order that we may also be glorified together.
text.DRB: And if sons, heirs also; heirs truly of God, and coheirs of Christ: yet if we suffer with him, that we may be also glorified with him.
text.KJV: And if children, then heirs; heirs of God, and joint-heirs with Christ; if so be that we suffer with him, that we may be also glorified together.
text.NABRE: and if children, then heirs, heirs of God and joint heirs with Christ, if only we suffer with him so that we may also be glorified with him. Destiny of Glory.

text.NRSV: I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worth comparing with the glory about to be revealed to us.
text.Rotherham: For I reckon that unworthy are the sufferings of the present season to be compared with the glory about to be revealed towards us;
text.DRB: For I think that the passions of this time are not condign to the glory to come that shall be revealed in us.
text.KJV: For I reckon that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be revealed in us.
text.NABRE: I consider that the sufferings of this present time are as nothing compared with the glory to be revealed for us.

text.NRSV: For the creation waits with eager longing for the revealing of the children of God;
text.Rotherham: For, the eager outlook of creation, ardently awaiteth the revealing of the sons of God,—
text.DRB: For the expectation of the creature, expecteth the revelation of the sons of God.
text.KJV: For the earnest expectation of the creature waiteth for the manifestation of the sons of God.
text.NABRE: For creation awaits with eager expectation the revelation of the children of God;

text.NRSV: for the creation was subjected to futility, not of its own will but by the will of the one who subjected it, in hope
text.Rotherham: For, unto vanity, hath creation been made subject—not by choice, but by reason of him that made it subject, in hope
text.DRB: For the creature is made subject to vanity, not willing, but for him that made it subject in hope:
text.KJV: For the creature was made subject to vanity, not willingly, but by reason of him who hath subjected the same in hope,
text.NABRE: for creation was made subject to futility, not of its own accord but because of the one who subjected it, in hope

text.NRSV: that the creation itself will be set free from its bondage to decay and will obtain the freedom of the glory of the children of God.
text.Rotherham: That, creation itself also, shall be freed—from the bondage of the decay into the freedom of the glory of the sons of God;
text.DRB: because the creature also itself shall be delivered from the servitude of corruption, into the liberty of the glory of the children of God.
text.KJV: Because the creature itself also shall be delivered from the bondage of corruption into the glorious liberty of the children of God.
text.NABRE: that creation itself would be set free from slavery to corruption and share in the glorious freedom of the children of God.

text.NRSV: We know that the whole creation has been groaning in labor pains until now;
text.Rotherham: For we know that, all creation, is sighing together, and travailing-in-birth-throes together until the present,—
text.DRB: For we know that every creature groaneth, & travaileth even till now.
text.KJV: For we know that the whole creation groaneth and travaileth in pain together until now.
text.NABRE: We know that all creation is groaning in labor pains even until now;

text.NRSV: and not only the creation, but we ourselves, who have the first fruits of the Spirit, groan inwardly while we wait for adoption, the redemption of our bodies.
text.Rotherham: And, not only so, but, we ourselves, also, who have the first-fruit of the Spirit— [we] even ourselves, within our own selves do sigh,—sonship ardently awaiting—the redeeming of our body;—
text.DRB: And not only it, but we also ourselves having the first fruits of the spirit, we also groan within ourselves, expecting the adoption of the sons of God, the redemption of our body.
text.KJV: And not only they, but ourselves also, which have the firstfruits of the Spirit, even we ourselves groan within ourselves, waiting for the adoption, to wit, the redemption of our body.
text.NABRE: and not only that, but we ourselves, who have the firstfruits of the Spirit, we also groan within ourselves as we wait for adoption, the redemption of our bodies.

text.NRSV: For in hope we were saved. Now hope that is seen is not hope. For who hopes for what is seen?
text.Rotherham: For, by our hope, have we been saved,—but, hope beheld, is not hope, for, what one beholdeth, why doth he hope for?
text.DRB: For by hope we are saved. But hope that is seen, is not hope. For that which a man seeth, wherefore doth he hope it?
text.KJV: For we are saved by hope: but hope that is seen is not hope: for what a man seeth, why doth he yet hope for?
text.NABRE: For in hope we were saved. Now hope that sees for itself is not hope. For who hopes for what one sees?

text.NRSV: But if we hope for what we do not see, we wait for it with patience.
text.Rotherham: If, however, what we do not behold we hope for, with endurance, are we ardently awaiting [it];—
text.DRB: But if we hope for that which we see not; we expect by patience.
text.KJV: But if we hope for that we see not, then do we with patience wait for it.
text.NABRE: But if we hope for what we do not see, we wait with endurance.

text.NRSV: Likewise the Spirit helps us in our weakness; for we do not know how to pray as we ought, but that very Spirit intercedes with sighs too deep for words.
text.Rotherham: In the selfsame way moreover, even the Spirit, helpeth together in our weakness,—for, what we should pray for as we ought, we know not, but, the Spirit itself, maketh intercession with sighings unutterable,
text.DRB: And in like manner also the Spirit helpeth our infirmity. For, what we should pray as we ought, we know not: but the Spirit himself requesteth for us with groanings unspeakable.
text.KJV: Likewise the Spirit also helpeth our infirmities: for we know not what we should pray for as we ought: but the Spirit itself maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered.
text.NABRE: In the same way, the Spirit too comes to the aid of our weakness; for we do not know how to pray as we ought, but the Spirit itself intercedes with inexpressible groanings.

text.NRSV: And God, who searches the heart, knows what is the mind of the Spirit, because the Spirit intercedes for the saints according to the will of God.
text.Rotherham: And, he that searcheth the hearts, knoweth what is preferred by the Spirit—that, according to God, he maketh intercession in behalf of saints;
text.DRB: And he that searcheth the hearts, knoweth what the Spirit desireth: because according to God he requesteth for the saints.
text.KJV: And he that searcheth the hearts knoweth what is the mind of the Spirit, because he maketh intercession for the saints according to the will of God.
text.NABRE: And the one who searches hearts knows what is the intention of the Spirit, because it intercedes for the holy ones according to God’s will.

text.NRSV: We know that all things work together for good for those who love God, who are called according to his purpose.
text.Rotherham: We know, further, that, unto them who love God, God causeth all things to work together for good,—unto them who, according to purpose, are such as he hath called;
text.DRB: And we know that to them that love God, all things cooperate unto good, to such as according to purpose are called to be saints.
text.KJV: And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God, to them who are the called according to his purpose.
text.NABRE: God’s Indomitable Love in Christ. We know that all things work for good for those who love God, who are called according to his purpose.

text.NRSV: For those whom he foreknew he also predestined to be conformed to the image of his Son, in order that he might be the firstborn within a large family.
text.Rotherham: For, whom he fore-approved, he also fore-appointed to be conformed unto the image of his Son, that he might be firstborn among many brethren,—
text.DRB: For whom he hath foreknown, he hath also predestinated to be made conformable to the image of his Son: that he might be the first-born in many brethren.
text.KJV: For whom he did foreknow, he also did predestinate to be conformed to the image of his Son, that he might be the firstborn among many brethren.
text.NABRE: For those he foreknew he also predestined to be conformed to the image of his Son, so that he might be the firstborn among many brothers.

text.NRSV: And those whom he predestined he also called; and those whom he called he also justified; and those whom he justified he also glorified.
text.Rotherham: And, whom he fore-appointed, the same, he also called, and, whom he called, the same, he also declared righteous, and, whom he declared righteous, the same, he also made glorious:—
text.DRB: And whom he hath predestinated; them also he hath called. And whom he hath called; them also he hath justified. And whom he hath justified; them also hath he glorified.
text.KJV: Moreover whom he did predestinate, them he also called: and whom he called, them he also justified: and whom he justified, them he also glorified.
text.NABRE: And those he predestined he also called; and those he called he also justified; and those he justified he also glorified.

text.NRSV: What then are we to say about these things? If God is for us, who is against us?
text.Rotherham: What, then, shall we say to a these things? If God [is] for us, who [shall be] against us?
text.DRB: What shall we then say to these things? If God be for us, who is against us?
text.KJV: What shall we then say to these things? If God be for us, who can be against us?
text.NABRE: What then shall we say to this? If God is for us, who can be against us?

text.NRSV: He who did not withhold his own Son, but gave him up for all of us, will he not with him also give us everything else?
text.Rotherham: He, at least, who his own Son did not spare, but in behalf of us all delivered him up, how shall he not also, with him, all things upon us in favour bestow?
text.DRB: He that spared not also his own Son, but for us all delivered him; how hath he not also with him given us all things?
text.KJV: He that spared not his own Son, but delivered him up for us all, how shall he not with him also freely give us all things?
text.NABRE: He who did not spare his own Son but handed him over for us all, how will he not also give us everything else along with him?

text.NRSV: Who will bring any charge against God’s elect? It is God who justifies.
text.Rotherham: Who shall bring an accusation against the chosen ones of God? God, who declareth righteous?
text.DRB: Who shall accuse against the elect of God? God that justifieth.
text.KJV: Who shall lay any thing to the charge of God’s elect? It is God that justifieth.
text.NABRE: Who will bring a charge against God’s chosen ones? It is God who acquits us.

text.NRSV: Who is to condemn? It is Christ Jesus, who died, yes, who was raised, who is at the right hand of God, who indeed intercedes for us.
text.Rotherham: Who is he that condemneth? Christ [Jesus] who died?—Nay! rather was raised [from among the dead],—who is on the right hand of God, who also is making intercession in our behalf?
text.DRB: Who is he that shall condemn? Christ Jesus that died, yea that is risen also again, who is on the right hand of God, who also maketh intercession for us.
text.KJV: Who is he that condemneth? It is Christ that died, yea rather, that is risen again, who is even at the right hand of God, who also maketh intercession for us.
text.NABRE: Who will condemn? It is Christ [Jesus] who died, rather, was raised, who also is at the right hand of God, who indeed intercedes for us.

text.NRSV: Who will separate us from the love of Christ? Will hardship, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?
text.Rotherham: Who shall separate us from the love of the Christ? Shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?—
text.DRB: Who then shall separate us from the charity of Christ? tribulation? or distress? or famine? or nakedness? or danger? or persecution? or the sword?
text.KJV: Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?
text.NABRE: What will separate us from the love of Christ? Will anguish, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or the sword?

text.NRSV: As it is written, “For your sake we are being killed all day long; we are accounted as sheep to be slaughtered.”
text.Rotherham: According as it is written—For thy sake, are we being put to death all the day long, we have been reckoned as sheep for slaughter.—
text.DRB: (as it is written: For we are killed for thy sake all the day: we are esteemed as sheep of slaughter. )
text.KJV: As it is written, For thy sake we are killed all the day long; we are accounted as sheep for the slaughter.
text.NABRE: As it is written: “For your sake we are being slain all the day; we are looked upon as sheep to be slaughtered.”

text.NRSV: No, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him who loved us.
text.Rotherham: Nay, in all these things, we are more than conquering through him that hath loved us.
text.DRB: But in all these things we overcome because of him that hath loved us.
text.KJV: Nay, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him that loved us.
text.NABRE: No, in all these things we conquer overwhelmingly through him who loved us.

text.NRSV: For I am convinced that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor rulers, nor things present, nor things to come, nor powers,
text.Rotherham: For I am persuaded that—neither death nor life, nor messengers nor principalities, nor things present nor things to come, nor powers,
text.DRB: For I am sure that neither death, nor life, nor Angels, nor Principalities, nor Powers, neither things present, nor things to come, neither might,
text.KJV: For I am persuaded, that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor powers, nor things present, nor things to come,
text.NABRE: For I am convinced that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor present things, nor future things, nor powers,

text.NRSV: nor height, nor depth, nor anything else in all creation, will be able to separate us from the love of God in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.Rotherham: Nor height nor depth, nor any other created thing, shall be able to separate us from the love of God which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.DRB: nor height, nor depth, nor other creature, shall be able to separate us from the charity of God which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.KJV: Nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature, shall be able to separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.NABRE: nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature will be able to separate us from the love of God in Christ Jesus our Lord.

text.NRSV: I am speaking the truth in Christ—I am not lying; my conscience confirms it by the Holy Spirit—
text.Rotherham: Truth, say I, in Christ, I utter no falsehood,—my conscience bearing witness with me in the Holy Spirit,—
text.DRB: I speak the verity in Christ, I lie not, my conscience bearing me witness in the Holy Ghost,
text.KJV: I say the truth in Christ, I lie not, my conscience also bearing me witness in the Holy Ghost,
text.NABRE: V. Jews and Gentiles in God’s Plan Chapter 9 Paul’s Love for Israel. I speak the truth in Christ, I do not lie; my conscience joins with the holy Spirit in bearing me witness

text.NRSV: I have great sorrow and unceasing anguish in my heart.
text.Rotherham: That I have great grief and incessant travail in my heart;
text.DRB: that I have great sadness & continual sorrow in my heart.
text.KJV: That I have great heaviness and continual sorrow in my heart.
text.NABRE: that I have great sorrow and constant anguish in my heart.

text.NRSV: For I could wish that I myself were accursed and cut off from Christ for the sake of my own people, my kindred according to the flesh.
text.Rotherham: For I could have wished to be accursed, even I myself, from the Christ, in behalf of my brethren my kinsmen according to the flesh;—
text.DRB: For I wished myself to be an anathema from Christ for my brethren, who are my kinsmen according to the flesh,
text.KJV: For I could wish that myself were accursed from Christ for my brethren, my kinsmen according to the flesh:
text.NABRE: For I could wish that I myself were accursed and separated from Christ for the sake of my brothers, my kin according to the flesh.

text.NRSV: They are Israelites, and to them belong the adoption, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the law, the worship, and the promises;
text.Rotherham: Who, indeed, are Israelites, whose are the sonship, and the glory, and the covenants, and the legislation, and the divine service, and the promises,
text.DRB: who are Israelites, whose is the adoption of sons, and the glory, and the testament, and the law-giving, & the service, and the promises:
text.KJV: Who are Israelites; to whom pertaineth the adoption, and the glory, and the covenants, and the giving of the law, and the service of God, and the promises;
text.NABRE: They are Israelites; theirs the adoption, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the law, the worship, and the promises;

text.NRSV: to them belong the patriarchs, and from them, according to the flesh, comes the Messiah, who is over all, God blessed forever. Amen.
text.Rotherham: Whose are the fathers, and of whom is the Christ—according to the flesh—he who is over all, God, blessed unto the ages. Amen.
text.DRB: whose are the fathers, & of whom Christ is according to the flesh, who is above all things God blessed for ever. Amen.
text.KJV: Whose are the fathers, and of whom as concerning the flesh Christ came, who is over all, God blessed for ever. Amen.
text.NABRE: theirs the patriarchs, and from them, according to the flesh, is the Messiah. God who is over all be blessed forever. Amen.

text.NRSV: It is not as though the word of God had failed. For not all Israelites truly belong to Israel,
text.Rotherham: It is not, however, as though the word of God had failed; for, not all they who are of Israel, the same are Israel.
text.DRB: But not that the word of God is frustrate. For, not all that are of Israel, they be Israelites:
text.KJV: Not as though the word of God hath taken none effect. For they are not all Israel, which are of Israel:
text.NABRE: God’s Free Choice. But it is not that the word of God has failed. For not all who are of Israel are Israel,

text.NRSV: and not all of Abraham’s children are his true descendants; but “It is through Isaac that descendants shall be named for you.”
text.Rotherham: Neither, because they are seed of Abraham, are all children,—but, In Isaac, shall there be called unto thee a seed.
text.DRB: nor they that are the seed of Abraham, all be children: but in Isaac shall the seed be called unto thee:
text.KJV: Neither, because they are the seed of Abraham, are they all children: but, In Isaac shall thy seed be called.
text.NABRE: nor are they all children of Abraham because they are his descendants; but “It is through Isaac that descendants shall bear your name.”

text.NRSV: This means that it is not the children of the flesh who are the children of God, but the children of the promise are counted as descendants.
text.Rotherham: That is—not the children of the flesh, the same are children of God; but, the children of the promise, are reckoned as a seed.
text.DRB: that is to say, not they that are the children of the flesh, they are the children of God: but they that are the children of the promise, are esteemed for the seed.
text.KJV: That is, They which are the children of the flesh, these are not the children of God: but the children of the promise are counted for the seed.
text.NABRE: This means that it is not the children of the flesh who are the children of God, but the children of the promise are counted as descendants.

text.NRSV: For this is what the promise said, “About this time I will return and Sarah shall have a son.”
text.Rotherham: For, of promise, is this word—According to this season, will I come, and Sarah shall have a son.
text.DRB: For the word of the promise is this: According to this time will I come; & Sara shall have a son.
text.KJV: For this is the word of promise, At this time will I come, and Sara shall have a son.
text.NABRE: For this is the wording of the promise, “About this time I shall return and Sarah will have a son.”

text.NRSV: Nor is that all; something similar happened to Rebecca when she had conceived children by one husband, our ancestor Isaac.
text.Rotherham: And, not only so, but, when, Rebekah also, was with child, of one—Isaac our father,—
text.DRB: And not only she, But Rebecca also conceiving of one copulation, of Isaac our father.
text.KJV: And not only this; but when Rebecca also had conceived by one, even by our father Isaac;
text.NABRE: And not only that, but also when Rebecca had conceived children by one husband, our father Isaac —

text.NRSV: Even before they had been born or had done anything good or bad (so that God’s purpose of election might continue,
text.Rotherham: They, in fact, not being yet born, nor having practised anything good or bad,—in order that the purpose of God by way of election might stand,—not by works but by him that was calling,
text.DRB: For when they were not yet born, nor had done any good or evil (that the purpose of God according to election might stand)
text.KJV: (For the children being not yet born, neither having done any good or evil, that the purpose of God according to election might stand, not of works, but of him that calleth;)
text.NABRE: before they had yet been born or had done anything, good or bad, in order that God’s elective plan might continue,

text.NRSV: not by works but by his call) she was told, “The elder shall serve the younger.”
text.Rotherham: It was said unto her—The elder, shall serve the younger;
text.DRB: not of works, but of the caller it was said to her: That the elder shall serve the younger,
text.KJV: It was said unto her, The elder shall serve the younger.
text.NABRE: not by works but by his call—she was told, “The older shall serve the younger.”

text.NRSV: As it is written, “I have loved Jacob, but I have hated Esau.”
text.Rotherham: Even as it is written—Jacob, have I loved, but, Esau, have I hated.
text.DRB: as it is written: Jacob I loved, but Esau I hated.
text.KJV: As it is written, Jacob have I loved, but Esau have I hated.
text.NABRE: As it is written: “I loved Jacob but hated Esau.”

text.NRSV: What then are we to say? Is there injustice on God’s part? By no means!
text.Rotherham: What, then, shall we say? Is there injustice with God? Far be it!
text.DRB: What shall we say then? Is there iniquity with God? God forbid.
text.KJV: What shall we say then? Is there unrighteousness with God? God forbid.
text.NABRE: What then are we to say? Is there injustice on the part of God? Of course not!

text.NRSV: For he says to Moses, “I will have mercy on whom I have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I have compassion.”
text.Rotherham: For, unto Moses, he saith—I will have mercy upon whomsoever I can have mercy, and I will have compassion upon whomsoever I can have compassion.
text.DRB: For to Moyses he saith: I will have mercy on whom I have mercy; and I will shew mercy to whom I will shew mercy.
text.KJV: For he saith to Moses, I will have mercy on whom I will have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I will have compassion.
text.NABRE: For he says to Moses: “I will show mercy to whom I will, I will take pity on whom I will.”

text.NRSV: So it depends not on human will or exertion, but on God who shows mercy.
text.Rotherham: Hence, then, it is nor of him that wisheth nor of him that runneth, but of the mercy-shewing God.
text.DRB: Therefore it is not of the willer, nor the runner, but of God that sheweth mercy.
text.KJV: So then it is not of him that willeth, nor of him that runneth, but of God that sheweth mercy.
text.NABRE: So it depends not upon a person’s will or exertion, but upon God, who shows mercy.

text.NRSV: For the scripture says to Pharaoh, “I have raised you up for the very purpose of showing my power in you, so that my name may be proclaimed in all the earth.”
text.Rotherham: For the Scripture saith unto Pharaoh—Unto this end, have I raised thee up, that I may thus shew in thee my power, and that I may declare my name in all the earth.
text.DRB: For the Scripture saith to Pharao: That to this purpose have I raised thee, that in thee I may shew my power; and that my name may be renowned in the whole earth.
text.KJV: For the scripture saith unto Pharaoh, Even for this same purpose have I raised thee up, that I might shew my power in thee, and that my name might be declared throughout all the earth.
text.NABRE: For the scripture says to Pharaoh, “This is why I have raised you up, to show my power through you that my name may be proclaimed throughout the earth.”

text.NRSV: So then he has mercy on whomever he chooses, and he hardens the heart of whomever he chooses.
text.Rotherham: Hence, then,—on whom he pleaseth, he hath mercy, and, whom he pleaseth, he doth harden.
text.DRB: Therefore on whom he will, he hath mercy; and whom he will, he doth indurate.
text.KJV: Therefore hath he mercy on whom he will have mercy, and whom he will he hardeneth.
text.NABRE: Consequently, he has mercy upon whom he wills, and he hardens whom he wills.

text.NRSV: You will say to me then, “Why then does he still find fault? For who can resist his will?”
text.Rotherham: Thou wilt say to me, then—Why longer findeth he fault? For, his purpose, who hath withstood?
text.DRB: Thou sayst therefore unto me: Why doth he yet complain? for who resisteth his will?
text.KJV: Thou wilt say then unto me, Why doth he yet find fault? For who hath resisted his will?
text.NABRE: You will say to me then, “Why [then] does he still find fault? For who can oppose his will?”

text.NRSV: But who indeed are you, a human being, to argue with God? Will what is molded say to the one who molded it, “Why have you made me like this?”
text.Rotherham: O man! Who, nevertheless, art, thou, that art answering again unto God? Shall the thing formed say unto him that formed it—Why didst thou make me thus?
text.DRB: O man, who art thou that doest answer God? Doth the work say to him that wrought it: Why hast thou made me thus?
text.KJV: Nay but, O man, who art thou that repliest against God? Shall the thing formed say to him that formed it, Why hast thou made me thus?
text.NABRE: But who indeed are you, a human being, to talk back to God? Will what is made say to its maker, “Why have you created me so?”

text.NRSV: Has the potter no right over the clay, to make out of the same lump one object for special use and another for ordinary use?
text.Rotherham: Or hath not the potter a right over the clay—out of the same lump, to make some, indeed, into a vessel for honour, and some for dishonour?
text.DRB: Or hath not the potter of clay, power, of the same mass to make one vessel unto honour, and another unto contumely?
text.KJV: Hath not the potter power over the clay, of the same lump to make one vessel unto honour, and another unto dishonour?
text.NABRE: Or does not the potter have a right over the clay, to make out of the same lump one vessel for a noble purpose and another for an ignoble one?

text.NRSV: What if God, desiring to show his wrath and to make known his power, has endured with much patience the objects of wrath that are made for destruction;
text.Rotherham: And, if God—wishing to shew his anger and to make known his power—bare, in much patience, with vessels of anger already fitted for destruction,
