text.DRB: And if God willing to shew wrath, & to make his might known, sustained in much patience the vessels of wrath apt to destruction,
text.KJV: What if God, willing to shew his wrath, and to make his power known, endured with much longsuffering the vessels of wrath fitted to destruction:
text.NABRE: What if God, wishing to show his wrath and make known his power, has endured with much patience the vessels of wrath made for destruction?

text.NRSV: and what if he has done so in order to make known the riches of his glory for the objects of mercy, which he has prepared beforehand for glory—
text.Rotherham: In order that he might make known the riches of his glory upon vessels of mercy which he prepared beforehand for glory,—
text.DRB: that he might shew the riches of his glory upon the vessels of mercy which he prepared unto glory.
text.KJV: And that he might make known the riches of his glory on the vessels of mercy, which he had afore prepared unto glory,
text.NABRE: This was to make known the riches of his glory to the vessels of mercy, which he has prepared previously for glory,

text.NRSV: including us whom he has called, not from the Jews only but also from the Gentiles?
text.Rotherham: Whom he also called, even us, not only from among Jews, but also from among the nations, [what then?]
text.DRB: Whom also he hath called, us, not only of the Jews, but also of the Gentiles,
text.KJV: Even us, whom he hath called, not of the Jews only, but also of the Gentiles?
text.NABRE: namely, us whom he has called, not only from the Jews but also from the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: As indeed he says in Hosea, “Those who were not my people I will call ‘my people,’ and her who was not beloved I will call ‘beloved.’”
text.Rotherham: As also in Hosea he saith—I will call the not-my-people, My people, and the not-beloved, beloved,
text.DRB: as in Osee he saith: I will call that which is not my people, my people; & her that was not beloved, beloved: & her that hath not obtained mercy, having obtained mercy.
text.KJV: As he saith also in Osee, I will call them my people, which were not my people; and her beloved, which was not beloved.
text.NABRE: Witness of the Prophets. As indeed he says in Hosea: “Those who were not my people I will call ‘my people,’ and her who was not beloved I will call ‘beloved.’

text.NRSV: “And in the very place where it was said to them, ‘You are not my people,’ there they shall be called children of the living God.”
text.Rotherham: And it shall be—in the place where it was said [to them] —not my people are, ye! there, shall they be called—Sons of a Living God.
text.DRB: And it shall be, in the place where it was said to them, you are not my people: there they shall be called the sons of the living God.
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass, that in the place where it was said unto them, Ye are not my people; there shall they be called the children of the living God.
text.NABRE: And in the very place where it was said to them, ‘You are not my people,’ there they shall be called children of the living God.”

text.NRSV: And Isaiah cries out concerning Israel, “Though the number of the children of Israel were like the sand of the sea, only a remnant of them will be saved;
text.Rotherham: Isaiah, moreover, exclaimeth over Israel—Though the number of the sons of Israel be as the sand of the sea, the remnant, shall be saved;
text.DRB: And Esaie cryeth for Israel: If the number of the children of Israel be as the sand of the sea, the remains shall be saved.
text.KJV: Esaias also crieth concerning Israel, Though the number of the children of Israel be as the sand of the sea, a remnant shall be saved:
text.NABRE: And Isaiah cries out concerning Israel, “Though the number of the Israelites were like the sand of the sea, only a remnant will be saved;

text.NRSV: for the Lord will execute his sentence on the earth quickly and decisively.”
text.Rotherham: For, a complete and concise account, will the Lord make upon the earth.
text.DRB: For consummating a word, and abridging it in equity: because a word abridged shall our Lord make upon the earth.
text.KJV: For he will finish the work, and cut it short in righteousness: because a short work will the Lord make upon the earth.
text.NABRE: for decisively and quickly will the Lord execute sentence upon the earth.”

text.NRSV: And as Isaiah predicted, “If the Lord of hosts had not left survivors to us, we would have fared like Sodom and been made like Gomorrah.”
text.Rotherham: And, even as Isaiah hath before said,—If, the Lord of hosts, had not left us a seed, as Sodom, had we become, and, as Gomorrha, had we been made like.
text.DRB: And as Esay foretold: unless the Lord of Sabaoth had left us seed, we had been made like Sodom, and we had been like as Gomorrha.
text.KJV: And as Esaias said before, Except the Lord of Sabaoth had left us a seed, we had been as Sodoma, and been made like unto Gomorrha.
text.NABRE: And as Isaiah predicted: “Unless the Lord of hosts had left us descendants, we would have become like Sodom and have been made like Gomorrah.” Righteousness Based on Faith.

text.NRSV: What then are we to say? Gentiles, who did not strive for righteousness, have attained it, that is, righteousness through faith;
text.Rotherham: What, then, shall we say? That, they of the nations, who were not in pursuit of righteousness, have laid hold of righteousness,—a righteousness, however, which is by faith;
text.DRB: What shall we say then? That the Gentiles which pursued not after justice, have apprehended justice, but the justice that is of faith.
text.KJV: What shall we say then? That the Gentiles, which followed not after righteousness, have attained to righteousness, even the righteousness which is of faith.
text.NABRE: What then shall we say? That Gentiles, who did not pursue righteousness, have achieved it, that is, righteousness that comes from faith;

text.NRSV: but Israel, who did strive for the righteousness that is based on the law, did not succeed in fulfilling that law.
text.Rotherham: Whereas, Israel, though in pursuit of a law of righteousness, unto a law, have not attained.
text.DRB: But Israel in pursuing the law of justice, is not come unto the law of justice.
text.KJV: But Israel, which followed after the law of righteousness, hath not attained to the law of righteousness.
text.NABRE: but that Israel, who pursued the law of righteousness, did not attain to that law?

text.NRSV: Why not? Because they did not strive for it on the basis of faith, but as if it were based on works. They have stumbled over the stumbling stone,
text.Rotherham: Wherefore? Because, not by faith but as by works, [have they sought it]: they have stumbled at the stone of stumbling,
text.DRB: Why so? Because not of faith, but as it were of works. For they have stumbled at the stone of stumbling,
text.KJV: Wherefore? Because they sought it not by faith, but as it were by the works of the law. For they stumbled at that stumblingstone;
text.NABRE: Why not? Because they did it not by faith, but as if it could be done by works. They stumbled over the stone that causes stumbling,

text.NRSV: as it is written, “See, I am laying in Zion a stone that will make people stumble, a rock that will make them fall, and whoever believes in him will not be put to shame.”
text.Rotherham: Even as it is written—Lo! I lay in Zion, a stone to strike against and a rock to stumble over, and, he that resteth faith thereupon, shall not be put to shame.
text.DRB: as it is written: Behold I put in Sion a stone of stumbling, and a rock of scandal: and whosoever believeth in him, shall not be confounded.
text.KJV: As it is written, Behold, I lay in Sion a stumblingstone and rock of offence: and whosoever believeth on him shall not be ashamed.
text.NABRE: as it is written: “Behold, I am laying a stone in Zion that will make people stumble and a rock that will make them fall, and whoever believes in him shall not be put to shame.”

text.NRSV: Brothers and sisters, my heart’s desire and prayer to God for them is that they may be saved.
text.Rotherham: Brethren! the delight of my own heart and my supplication God-ward in their behalf, are for salvation;
text.DRB: Brethren, the will of my heart surely and prayer to God, is for them unto salvation.
text.KJV: Brethren, my heart’s desire and prayer to God for Israel is, that they might be saved.
text.NABRE: Chapter 10 Brothers, my heart’s desire and prayer to God on their behalf is for salvation.

text.NRSV: I can testify that they have a zeal for God, but it is not enlightened.
text.Rotherham: For I bear them witness that they have, a zeal for God,—but not according to correct knowledge;
text.DRB: For I give them testimony that they have zeal of God, but not according to knowledge.
text.KJV: For I bear them record that they have a zeal of God, but not according to knowledge.
text.NABRE: I testify with regard to them that they have zeal for God, but it is not discerning.

text.NRSV: For, being ignorant of the righteousness that comes from God, and seeking to establish their own, they have not submitted to God’s righteousness.
text.Rotherham: For, not knowing God’s righteousness and, their own, seeking to establish, unto the righteousness of God, have they not submitted;
text.DRB: For not knowing the justice of God, and seeking to establish their own, they have not been subject to the justice of God.
text.KJV: For they being ignorant of God’s righteousness, and going about to establish their own righteousness, have not submitted themselves unto the righteousness of God.
text.NABRE: For, in their unawareness of the righteousness that comes from God and their attempt to establish their own [righteousness], they did not submit to the righteousness of God.

text.NRSV: For Christ is the end of the law so that there may be righteousness for everyone who believes.
text.Rotherham: For Christ is an end of law, for righteousness, unto every one that believeth.
text.DRB: For, the end of the Law is Christ; unto justice to every one that believeth.
text.KJV: For Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to every one that believeth.
text.NABRE: For Christ is the end of the law for the justification of everyone who has faith.

text.NRSV: Moses writes concerning the righteousness that comes from the law, that “the person who does these things will live by them.”
text.Rotherham: For, Moses, writeth that—as touching the righteousness that is by law, The man that hath done [it], shall live thereby;
text.DRB: For Moyses wrote, that, the justice which is of the Law, the man that hath done it, shall live in it.
text.KJV: For Moses describeth the righteousness which is of the law, That the man which doeth those things shall live by them.
text.NABRE: Moses writes about the righteousness that comes from [the] law, “The one who does these things will live by them.”

text.NRSV: But the righteousness that comes from faith says, “Do not say in your heart, ‘Who will ascend into heaven?’ ” (that is, to bring Christ down)
text.Rotherham: Whereas, the righteousness by faith, thus speaketh—Do not say in thy heart, Who shall ascend into heaven? That is, to bring, Christ, down,
text.DRB: But the justice which is of faith, saith thus: Say not in thy heart, Who shall ascend into Heaven? that is to bring Christ down.
text.KJV: But the righteousness which is of faith speaketh on this wise, Say not in thine heart, Who shall ascend into heaven? (that is, to bring Christ down from above:)
text.NABRE: But the righteousness that comes from faith says, “Do not say in your heart, ‘Who will go up into heaven?’ (that is, to bring Christ down)

text.NRSV: “or ‘Who will descend into the abyss?’ ” (that is, to bring Christ up from the dead).
text.Rotherham: Or, Who shall descend into the abyss? That is, to bring up, Christ, from among the dead;
text.DRB: Or who descendeth into the depth? that is to call Christ again from the dead.
text.KJV: Or, Who shall descend into the deep? (that is, to bring up Christ again from the dead.)
text.NABRE: or ‘Who will go down into the abyss?’ (that is, to bring Christ up from the dead).”

text.NRSV: But what does it say? “The word is near you, on your lips and in your heart” (that is, the word of faith that we proclaim);
text.Rotherham: But what saith it? Near thee, is the declaration, In thy mouth and in thy heart,—that is, the declaration of the faith, which we proclaim:—
text.DRB: But what saith the Scripture? The word is nigh, in thy mouth, and in thy heart. This is the word of faith which we preach.
text.KJV: But what saith it? The word is nigh thee, even in thy mouth, and in thy heart: that is, the word of faith, which we preach;
text.NABRE: But what does it say? “The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart” (that is, the word of faith that we preach),

text.NRSV: because if you confess with your lips that Jesus is Lord and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
text.Rotherham: That, if thou shalt confess the declaration with thy mouth—That Jesus is, Lord, and shalt believe with thy hear—That, God, raised him from among the dead, thou shalt be saved;—
text.DRB: For if thou confess with thy mouth our Lord Jesus, and in thy heart believe that God hath raised him up from the dead, thou shalt be saved.
text.KJV: That if thou shalt confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and shalt believe in thine heart that God hath raised him from the dead, thou shalt be saved.
text.NABRE: for, if you confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

text.NRSV: For one believes with the heart and so is justified, and one confesses with the mouth and so is saved.
text.Rotherham: For, in heart, it is to be believed unto righteousness, and, by mouth, it is to be confessed unto salvation.
text.DRB: For with the heart we believe unto justice; but with the mouth confession is made to salvation.
text.KJV: For with the heart man believeth unto righteousness; and with the mouth confession is made unto salvation.
text.NABRE: For one believes with the heart and so is justified, and one confesses with the mouth and so is saved.

text.NRSV: The scripture says, “No one who believes in him will be put to shame.”
text.Rotherham: For the scripture saith—No one that resteth faith thereupon, shall be put to shame?
text.DRB: For the Scripture saith: Whosoever believeth in him, shall not be confounded.
text.KJV: For the scripture saith, Whosoever believeth on him shall not be ashamed.
text.NABRE: For the scripture says, “No one who believes in him will be put to shame.”

text.NRSV: For there is no distinction between Jew and Greek; the same Lord is Lord of all and is generous to all who call on him.
text.Rotherham: For there is no distinction—of Jew or Greek,—for, the same [Lord], is Lord of all, being rich unto all who call upon him;
text.DRB: For there is no distinction of the Jew and the Greek: for one is Lord of all, rich toward all that invocate him.
text.KJV: For there is no difference between the Jew and the Greek: for the same Lord over all is rich unto all that call upon him.
text.NABRE: For there is no distinction between Jew and Greek; the same Lord is Lord of all, enriching all who call upon him.

text.NRSV: For, “Everyone who calls on the name of the Lord shall be saved.”
text.Rotherham: For, Whosoever shall call upon the name of the Lord, shall be saved:—
text.DRB: For every one whosoever shall invocate the name of our Lord, shall be saved.
text.KJV: For whosoever shall call upon the name of the Lord shall be saved.
text.NABRE: For “everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved.”

text.NRSV: But how are they to call on one in whom they have not believed? And how are they to believe in one of whom they have never heard? And how are they to hear without someone to proclaim him?
text.Rotherham: How, then, shall men call upon one in whom they have not believed? And how shall they believe in one of whom they have not heard? And how shall they hear, without one to proclaim?
text.DRB: How then shall they invocate in whom they have not believed? Or how shall they believe him whom they have not heard? And how shall they hear without a preacher?
text.KJV: How then shall they call on him in whom they have not believed? and how shall they believe in him of whom they have not heard? and how shall they hear without a preacher?
text.NABRE: But how can they call on him in whom they have not believed? And how can they believe in him of whom they have not heard? And how can they hear without someone to preach?

text.NRSV: And how are they to proclaim him unless they are sent? As it is written, “How beautiful are the feet of those who bring good news!”
text.Rotherham: And how shall they proclaim, except they be sent? Even as it is written—How beautiful the feet of them that bring glad tidings of good things!
text.DRB: But how shall they preach unless they be sent? as it is written: How beautiful are the feet of them that evangelize peace, of them that evangelize good things?
text.KJV: And how shall they preach, except they be sent? as it is written, How beautiful are the feet of them that preach the gospel of peace, and bring glad tidings of good things!
text.NABRE: And how can people preach unless they are sent? As it is written, “How beautiful are the feet of those who bring [the] good news!”

text.NRSV: But not all have obeyed the good news; for Isaiah says, “Lord, who has believed our message?”
text.Rotherham: But, not all, have become obedient unto the glad tidings;—for, Isaiah, saith—Lord! who believed what we have heard?
text.DRB: But all do not obey the Gospel. For Esay saith, Lord, who hath believed the hearing of us?
text.KJV: But they have not all obeyed the gospel. For Esaias saith, Lord, who hath believed our report?
text.NABRE: But not everyone has heeded the good news; for Isaiah says, “Lord, who has believed what was heard from us?”

text.NRSV: So faith comes from what is heard, and what is heard comes through the word of Christ.
text.Rotherham: Hence, our faith, cometh by something heard, and, that which is heard, through a declaration of Christ.
text.DRB: Faith then, is by hearing: and hearing is by the word of Christ.
text.KJV: So then faith cometh by hearing, and hearing by the word of God.
text.NABRE: Thus faith comes from what is heard, and what is heard comes through the word of Christ.

text.NRSV: But I ask, have they not heard? Indeed they have; for “Their voice has gone out to all the earth, and their words to the ends of the world.”
text.Rotherham: But I say: Have they not heard? Yea indeed,—into all the earth, hath gone forth their sound, and, unto the ends of the inhabited world, their declarations.
text.DRB: But I say, have they not heard? And certes into all the earth hath the sound of them gone forth: and unto the ends of the whole world the words of them.
text.KJV: But I say, Have they not heard? Yes verily, their sound went into all the earth, and their words unto the ends of the world.
text.NABRE: But I ask, did they not hear? Certainly they did; for “Their voice has gone forth to all the earth, and their words to the ends of the world.”

text.NRSV: Again I ask, did Israel not understand? First Moses says, “I will make you jealous of those who are not a nation; with a foolish nation I will make you angry.”
text.Rotherham: But I say: Hath, Israel, not come to know? First, Moses, saith—I, will provoke you to jealousy on account of a no-nation, on account of an undiscerning nation, will I make you very angry.
text.DRB: But I say, hath not Israel known? Moyses first saith: I will bring you to emulation in that which is not a nation: in a foolish nation, I will drive you into anger.
text.KJV: But I say, Did not Israel know? First Moses saith, I will provoke you to jealousy by them that are no people, and by a foolish nation I will anger you.
text.NABRE: But I ask, did not Israel understand? First Moses says: “I will make you jealous of those who are not a nation; with a senseless nation I will make you angry.”

text.NRSV: Then Isaiah is so bold as to say, “I have been found by those who did not seek me; I have shown myself to those who did not ask for me.”
text.Rotherham: Isaiah, however, waxeth daring, and saith,—I have been found by them who after me were not seeking, manifest have I become unto them who for me were not enquiring;
text.DRB: But Esay is bold, and saith: I was found of them that did not seek me: openly I appeared to them that asked not of me.
text.KJV: But Esaias is very bold, and saith, I was found of them that sought me not; I was made manifest unto them that asked not after me.
text.NABRE: Then Isaiah speaks boldly and says: “I was found [by] those who were not seeking me; I revealed myself to those who were not asking for me.”

text.NRSV: But of Israel he says, “All day long I have held out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people.”
text.Rotherham: Whereas, regarding Israel, he saith—All day long, have I stretched forth my hands unto a people unyielding and contradicting.
text.DRB: But to Israel he saith: All the day have I spread my hands to a people that believeth not, and contradicteth me.
text.KJV: But to Israel he saith, All day long I have stretched forth my hands unto a disobedient and gainsaying people.
text.NABRE: But regarding Israel he says, “All day long I stretched out my hands to a disobedient and contentious people.”

text.NRSV: I ask, then, has God rejected his people? By no means! I myself am an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, a member of the tribe of Benjamin.
text.Rotherham: I say, then—Hath God cast off his people? Far be it! For, I also, am an Israelite,—of the seed of Abraham, of the tribe of Benjamin:
text.DRB: I say then: Hath God rejected his people? God forbid. For I also am an Israelite, of the seed of Abraham, of the tribe of Ben-jamin.
text.KJV: I say then, Hath God cast away his people? God forbid. For I also am an Israelite, of the seed of Abraham, of the tribe of Benjamin.
text.NABRE: Chapter 11 The Remnant of Israel. I ask, then, has God rejected his people? Of course not! For I too am an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, of the tribe of Benjamin.

text.NRSV: God has not rejected his people whom he foreknew. Do you not know what the scripture says of Elijah, how he pleads with God against Israel?
text.Rotherham: God hath not cast off his people, whom he fore approved. Or know ye not, in [the account of] Elijah what the scripture saith, when he intercedeth with God against Israel?
text.DRB: God hath not rejected his people which he foreknew. Or know you not in Elias what the Scripture saith; how he requesteth God against Israel?
text.KJV: God hath not cast away his people which he foreknew. Wot ye not what the scripture saith of Elias? how he maketh intercession to God against Israel, saying,
text.NABRE: God has not rejected his people whom he foreknew. Do you not know what the scripture says about Elijah, how he pleads with God against Israel?

text.NRSV: “Lord, they have killed your prophets, they have demolished your altars; I alone am left, and they are seeking my life.”
text.Rotherham: Lord! Thy prophets, have they slain, thine altars, have they overthrown, and, I, am left alone, and they are seeking my life!
text.DRB: Lord, they have slain thy Prophets, they have digged down thine Altars: and I am left alone, & they seek my life.
text.KJV: Lord, they have killed thy prophets, and digged down thine altars; and I am left alone, and they seek my life.
text.NABRE: “Lord, they have killed your prophets, they have torn down your altars, and I alone am left, and they are seeking my life.”

text.NRSV: But what is the divine reply to him? “I have kept for myself seven thousand who have not bowed the knee to Baal.”
text.Rotherham: But what saith unto him the response? I have left for myself seven thousand men, who, indeed, have not bowed a knee unto Baal.
text.DRB: But what saith the divine answer unto him? I have left me seven thousand men, that have not bowed their knees to Baal?
text.KJV: But what saith the answer of God unto him? I have reserved to myself seven thousand men, who have not bowed the knee to the image of Baal.
text.NABRE: But what is God’s response to him? “I have left for myself seven thousand men who have not knelt to Baal.”

text.NRSV: So too at the present time there is a remnant, chosen by grace.
text.Rotherham: Thus, then, in the present season also, a remnant, by way of an election of favour, hath come into being.
text.DRB: So therefore at this time also, there are remains saved according to the election of grace.
text.KJV: Even so then at this present time also there is a remnant according to the election of grace.
text.NABRE: So also at the present time there is a remnant, chosen by grace.

text.NRSV: But if it is by grace, it is no longer on the basis of works, otherwise grace would no longer be grace.
text.Rotherham: If, however, by favour, no longer of works; else, favour, no longer proveth to be favour!
text.DRB: And if by grace, not now of works. Otherwise grace now is not grace.
text.KJV: And if by grace, then is it no more of works: otherwise grace is no more grace. But if it be of works, then is it no more grace: otherwise work is no more work.
text.NABRE: But if by grace, it is no longer because of works; otherwise grace would no longer be grace.

text.NRSV: What then? Israel failed to obtain what it was seeking. The elect obtained it, but the rest were hardened,
text.Rotherham: What then? That which Israel seeketh after, the same, it hath not obtained:—the election, however, have obtained it, and, the rest, have been hardened;—
text.DRB: What then? that which Israel sought, the same he hath not obtained; but the election hath obtained, and the rest were blinded:
text.KJV: What then? Israel hath not obtained that which he seeketh for; but the election hath obtained it, and the rest were blinded
text.NABRE: What then? What Israel was seeking it did not attain, but the elect attained it; the rest were hardened,

text.NRSV: as it is written, “God gave them a sluggish spirit, eyes that would not see and ears that would not hear, down to this very day.”
text.Rotherham: Even as it is written—God hath given unto them a spirit of stupor,—eyes not to see, and ears not to hear,—until this very day;
text.DRB: as it is written: God hath given them the spirit of compunction; eyes, that they may not see, and ears, that they may not hear; until this present day.
text.KJV: (According as it is written, God hath given them the spirit of slumber, eyes that they should not see, and ears that they should not hear;) unto this day.
text.NABRE: as it is written: “God gave them a spirit of deep sleep, eyes that should not see and ears that should not hear, down to this very day.”

text.NRSV: And David says, “Let their table become a snare and a trap, a stumbling block and a retribution for them;
text.Rotherham: And, David, saith—Let their table be turned into a snare, and into gin, and into a trap, and into a recompense unto them,
text.DRB: And David saith: Be their table made for a snare and for a trap & for a scandal & for a retribution unto them.
text.KJV: And David saith, Let their table be made a snare, and a trap, and a stumblingblock, and a recompence unto them:
text.NABRE: And David says: “Let their table become a snare and a trap, a stumbling block and a retribution for them;

text.NRSV: let their eyes be darkened so that they cannot see, and keep their backs forever bent.”
text.Rotherham: Darkened be their eyes, not to see, and, their back, do thou continually bow down.
text.DRB: Be their eyes darkened, that they may not see: & their back make thou always crooked.
text.KJV: Let their eyes be darkened, that they may not see, and bow down their back alway.
text.NABRE: let their eyes grow dim so that they may not see, and keep their backs bent forever.”

text.NRSV: So I ask, have they stumbled so as to fall? By no means! But through their stumbling salvation has come to the Gentiles, so as to make Israel jealous.
text.Rotherham: I say then—Did they stumble in order that they might fall? Far be it! But, by their fall, salvation [hath come] unto the nations, to the end of provoking them to jealousy.
text.DRB: Isay then, have they so stumbled, that they should fall? God forbid. But by their offence, salvation is to the Gentiles, that they may emulate them.
text.KJV: I say then, Have they stumbled that they should fall? God forbid: but rather through their fall salvation is come unto the Gentiles, for to provoke them to jealousy.
text.NABRE: The Gentiles’ Salvation. Hence I ask, did they stumble so as to fall? Of course not! But through their transgression salvation has come to the Gentiles, so as to make them jealous.

text.NRSV: Now if their stumbling means riches for the world, and if their defeat means riches for Gentiles, how much more will their full inclusion mean!
text.Rotherham: If, moreover, their fall, is the riches of a world, and their loss, the riches of nations, how much rather their fullness?
text.DRB: And if the offence of them be the riches of the world, and the diminution of them the riches of the Gentiles; how much more the fullness of them?
text.KJV: Now if the fall of them be the riches of the world, and the diminishing of them the riches of the Gentiles; how much more their fulness?
text.NABRE: Now if their transgression is enrichment for the world, and if their diminished number is enrichment for the Gentiles, how much more their full number.

text.NRSV: Now I am speaking to you Gentiles. Inasmuch then as I am an apostle to the Gentiles, I glorify my ministry
text.Rotherham: Unto you, however, am I speaking,—you of the nations; inasmuch, indeed, then, as, I, am an apostle to the nations, my ministry, I glorify,
text.DRB: For to you Gentiles I say, as long verily as I am the Apostle of the Gentiles, I will honour my ministry,
text.KJV: For I speak to you Gentiles, inasmuch as I am the apostle of the Gentiles, I magnify mine office:
text.NABRE: Now I am speaking to you Gentiles. Inasmuch then as I am the apostle to the Gentiles, I glory in my ministry

text.NRSV: in order to make my own people jealous, and thus save some of them.
text.Rotherham: If by any means I may provoke to jealousy my own flesh, and save some from among them;—
text.DRB: if by any means I may provoke my flesh to emulation, and may save some of them.
text.KJV: If by any means I may provoke to emulation them which are my flesh, and might save some of them.
text.NABRE: in order to make my race jealous and thus save some of them.

text.NRSV: For if their rejection is the reconciliation of the world, what will their acceptance be but life from the dead?
text.Rotherham: For, if, the casting away of them, hath become the reconciling of a world, what shall, the taking of them in addition, be, but life from among the dead?
text.DRB: For if the loss of them be the reconciliation of the world; what shall the receiving be, but life from the dead?
text.KJV: For if the casting away of them be the reconciling of the world, what shall the receiving of them be, but life from the dead?
text.NABRE: For if their rejection is the reconciliation of the world, what will their acceptance be but life from the dead?

text.NRSV: If the part of the dough offered as first fruits is holy, then the whole batch is holy; and if the root is holy, then the branches also are holy.
text.Rotherham: If, moreover, the first fruit [is] holy, the lump [shall be] also; and, if the root [is] holy, the branches [shall be] also.
text.DRB: And if the first fruit be holy, the mass also: and if the root be holy, the boughs also.
text.KJV: For if the firstfruit be holy, the lump is also holy: and if the root be holy, so are the branches.
text.NABRE: If the firstfruits are holy, so is the whole batch of dough; and if the root is holy, so are the branches.

text.NRSV: But if some of the branches were broken off, and you, a wild olive shoot, were grafted in their place to share the rich root of the olive tree,
text.Rotherham: If, however, some of the branches, have been broken out, and, thou, being a wild olive hast been grafted in among them, and hast become a joint partaker of the root of the fatness of the olive,
text.DRB: And if some of the boughs be broken, and thou whereas thou wast a wild olive, art graffed in them, and art made partaker of the root and of the fatness of the olive,
text.KJV: And if some of the branches be broken off, and thou, being a wild olive tree, wert graffed in among them, and with them partakest of the root and fatness of the olive tree;
text.NABRE: But if some of the branches were broken off, and you, a wild olive shoot, were grafted in their place and have come to share in the rich root of the olive tree,

text.NRSV: do not boast over the branches. If you do boast, remember that it is not you that support the root, but the root that supports you.
text.Rotherham: Be not boasting over the branches! Howbeit, if thou boast, it is not, thou, that bearest the root, but the root, thee!
text.DRB: glory not against the boughs. And if thou glory; not thou bearest the root, but the root thee.
text.KJV: Boast not against the branches. But if thou boast, thou bearest not the root, but the root thee.
text.NABRE: do not boast against the branches. If you do boast, consider that you do not support the root; the root supports you.

text.NRSV: You will say, “Branches were broken off so that I might be grafted in.”
text.Rotherham: Thou wilt say, then—Branches were broken out in order that, I, might he grafted in.
text.DRB: Thou sayest then: The boughs were broken, that I might be graffed in.
text.KJV: Thou wilt say then, The branches were broken off, that I might be graffed in.
text.NABRE: Indeed you will say, “Branches were broken off so that I might be grafted in.”

text.NRSV: That is true. They were broken off because of their unbelief, but you stand only through faith. So do not become proud, but stand in awe.
text.Rotherham: Well: by their want of faith, they have been broken out,—and, thou, by thy faith, dost stand!—Regard not lofty things, but be afraid;
text.DRB: Well: because of incredulity they were broken, but thou by faith doest stand: be not too highly wise; but fear.
text.KJV: Well; because of unbelief they were broken off, and thou standest by faith. Be not highminded, but fear:
text.NABRE: That is so. They were broken off because of unbelief, but you are there because of faith. So do not become haughty, but stand in awe.

text.NRSV: For if God did not spare the natural branches, perhaps he will not spare you.
text.Rotherham: For, if, God, hath not spared, the natural branches, neither, thee, will he spare!
text.DRB: For if God hath not spared the natural boughs; lest perhaps he will not spare thee neither.
text.KJV: For if God spared not the natural branches, take heed lest he also spare not thee.
text.NABRE: For if God did not spare the natural branches, [perhaps] he will not spare you either.

text.NRSV: Note then the kindness and the severity of God: severity toward those who have fallen, but God’s kindness toward you, provided you continue in his kindness; otherwise you also will be cut off.
text.Rotherham: See, then, the kindness and the severity of God: upon them who have fallen, severity,—but, upon thee, the kindness of God, if thou abide still in the kindness,—otherwise, thou also, shalt he cut out;
text.DRB: See then the goodness and the severity of God: upon them surely that are fallen, the severity; but upon thee the goodness of God, if thou abide in his goodness; otherwise thou also shalt be cut off.
text.KJV: Behold therefore the goodness and severity of God: on them which fell, severity; but toward thee, goodness, if thou continue in his goodness: otherwise thou also shalt be cut off.
text.NABRE: See, then, the kindness and severity of God: severity toward those who fell, but God’s kindness to you, provided you remain in his kindness; otherwise you too will be cut off.

text.NRSV: And even those of Israel, if they do not persist in unbelief, will be grafted in, for God has the power to graft them in again.
text.Rotherham: Whereas, they also, unless they abide still in their want of faith, shall be grafted in, for God is, able, again to engraft them!
text.DRB: But they also, if they do not abide in incredulity, shall be graffed in. For God is able to graff them in again.
text.KJV: And they also, if they abide not still in unbelief, shall be graffed in: for God is able to graff them in again.
text.NABRE: And they also, if they do not remain in unbelief, will be grafted in, for God is able to graft them in again.

text.NRSV: For if you have been cut from what is by nature a wild olive tree and grafted, contrary to nature, into a cultivated olive tree, how much more will these natural branches be grafted back into their own olive tree.
text.Rotherham: For, if, thou, out of the naturally wild olive was cut out, and, beyond nature, hast been engrafted into the good olive, how much rather, shall these, the natural [branches] be engrafted into their own olive tree?
text.DRB: For if thou wast cut out of the natural wild olive, and contrary to nature wast graffed into the good olive; how much more they that are according to nature shall be graffed into their own olive?
text.KJV: For if thou wert cut out of the olive tree which is wild by nature, and wert graffed contrary to nature into a good olive tree: how much more shall these, which be the natural branches, be graffed into their own olive tree?
text.NABRE: For if you were cut from what is by nature a wild olive tree, and grafted, contrary to nature, into a cultivated one, how much more will they who belong to it by nature be grafted back into their own olive tree. God’s Irrevocable Call.

text.NRSV: So that you may not claim to be wiser than you are, brothers and sisters, I want you to understand this mystery: a hardening has come upon part of Israel, until the full number of the Gentiles has come in.
text.Rotherham: For I wish not, ye should be ignorant, brethren, of this sacred secret, lest within yourselves ye be presumptuous, that, a hardening in part, hath befallen Israel, until, the full measure of the nations, shall come in;
text.DRB: For I will not have you ignorant, brethren, of this mystery (that you be not wise in yourselves) that blindness in part hath chanced in Israel, until the fullness of the Gentiles might enter:
text.KJV: For I would not, brethren, that ye should be ignorant of this mystery, lest ye should be wise in your own conceits; that blindness in part is happened to Israel, until the fulness of the Gentiles be come in.
text.NABRE: I do not want you to be unaware of this mystery, brothers, so that you will not become wise [in] your own estimation: a hardening has come upon Israel in part, until the full number of the Gentiles comes in,

text.NRSV: And so all Israel will be saved; as it is written, “Out of Zion will come the Deliverer; he will banish ungodliness from Jacob.”
text.Rotherham: And, so, all Israel shall be saved: even as it is written—There shall have come out of Zion the Deliverer,—He will turn away ungodliness from Jacob;
text.DRB: and so all Israel might be saved, as it is written: There shall come out of Sion, he that shall deliver, and shall avert impiety from Jacob.
text.KJV: And so all Israel shall be saved: as it is written, There shall come out of Sion the Deliverer, and shall turn away ungodliness from Jacob:
text.NABRE: and thus all Israel will be saved, as it is written: “The deliverer will come out of Zion, he will turn away godlessness from Jacob;

text.NRSV: “And this is my covenant with them, when I take away their sins.”
text.Rotherham: And, this, for them, is the covenant from me, as soon as I take away their sins.
text.DRB: And this to them the testament from me: when I shall have taken away their sins.
text.KJV: For this is my covenant unto them, when I shall take away their sins.
text.NABRE: and this is my covenant with them when I take away their sins.”

text.NRSV: As regards the gospel they are enemies of God for your sake; but as regards election they are beloved, for the sake of their ancestors;
text.Rotherham: As touching the joyful-message, indeed, they are enemies for your sake, but, as touching the election, beloved for their father’s sake;
text.DRB: According to the Gospel indeed enemies for you: but according to the election, most dear for the fathers.
text.KJV: As concerning the gospel, they are enemies for your sakes: but as touching the election, they are beloved for the fathers’ sakes.
text.NABRE: In respect to the gospel, they are enemies on your account; but in respect to election, they are beloved because of the patriarchs.

text.NRSV: for the gifts and the calling of God are irrevocable.
text.Rotherham: For, not to be regretted, are the gifts and the calling of God:—
text.DRB: For without repentance are the gifts & the vocation of God.
text.KJV: For the gifts and calling of God are without repentance.
text.NABRE: For the gifts and the call of God are irrevocable.

text.NRSV: Just as you were once disobedient to God but have now received mercy because of their disobedience,
text.Rotherham: For, just as, ye, at one time had not yielded unto God, and yet now have received mercy by their refusal to yield,
text.DRB: For as you also sometime did not believe God, but now have obtained mercy because of their incredulity;
text.KJV: For as ye in times past have not believed God, yet have now obtained mercy through their unbelief:
text.NABRE: Triumph of God’s Mercy. Just as you once disobeyed God but have now received mercy because of their disobedience,

text.NRSV: so they have now been disobedient in order that, by the mercy shown to you, they too may now receive mercy.
text.Rotherham: So, these also, have now refused to yield, by your own mercy, in order that, themselves also, should now become objects of mercy;
text.DRB: so these also now have not believed, for your mercy, that they also may obtain mercy.
text.KJV: Even so have these also now not believed, that through your mercy they also may obtain mercy.
text.NABRE: so they have now disobeyed in order that, by virtue of the mercy shown to you, they too may [now] receive mercy.

text.NRSV: For God has imprisoned all in disobedience so that he may be merciful to all.
text.Rotherham: For God hath shut up all together, in a refusal to yield, in order that, upon all, he may bestow mercy.
text.DRB: For God hath concluded all into incredulity, that he may have mercy on all.
text.KJV: For God hath concluded them all in unbelief, that he might have mercy upon all.
text.NABRE: For God delivered all to disobedience, that he might have mercy upon all.

text.NRSV: O the depth of the riches and wisdom and knowledge of God! How unsearchable are his judgments and how inscrutable his ways!
text.Rotherham: Oh! the depth of the riches and wisdom and knowledge of God! How unsearchable his judgments! and untraceable his ways!
text.DRB: O depth of the riches of the wisdom and of the knowledge of God: how incomprehensible are his judgements, and his ways unsearchable?
text.KJV: O the depth of the riches both of the wisdom and knowledge of God! how unsearchable are his judgments, and his ways past finding out!
text.NABRE: Oh, the depth of the riches and wisdom and knowledge of God! How inscrutable are his judgments and how unsearchable his ways!

text.NRSV: “For who has known the mind of the Lord? Or who has been his counselor?”
text.Rotherham: For who hath come to know the mind of the Lord? Or who hath become his counselor?
text.DRB: For who hath known the mind of our Lord? or who hath been his Counseller?
text.KJV: For who hath known the mind of the Lord? or who hath been his counsellor?
text.NABRE: “For who has known the mind of the Lord or who has been his counselor?”

text.NRSV: “Or who has given a gift to him, to receive a gift in return?”
text.Rotherham: Or who hath first given unto him, and it shall be recompensed to him again?
text.DRB: Or who hath first given to him, and retribution shall be made him?
text.KJV: Or who hath first given to him, and it shall be recompensed unto him again?
text.NABRE: “Or who has given him anything that he may be repaid?”

text.NRSV: For from him and through him and to him are all things. To him be the glory forever. Amen.
text.Rotherham: Because, of him, and through him, and unto him, are all things:—unto him, be the glory, unto the ages. Amen!
text.DRB: For of him, and by him, and in him are all things: to him be glory for ever. Amen.
text.KJV: For of him, and through him, and to him, are all things: to whom be glory for ever. Amen.
text.NABRE: For from him and through him and for him are all things. To him be glory forever. Amen.

text.NRSV: I appeal to you therefore, brothers and sisters, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and acceptable to God, which is your spiritual worship.
text.Rotherham: I beseech you therefore, brethren, through the compassions of God, to present your bodies a living, holy sacrifice, unto God acceptable,—your rational divine service;
text.DRB: I Beseech you therefore, brethren, by the mercy of God, that you exhibit your bodies a living host, holy, pleasing to God, your reasonable service.
text.KJV: I beseech you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that ye present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable unto God, which is your reasonable service.
text.NABRE: VI. The Duties of Christians Chapter 12 Sacrifice of Body and Mind. I urge you therefore, brothers, by the mercies of God, to offer your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God, your spiritual worship.

text.NRSV: Do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your minds, so that you may discern what is the will of God—what is good and acceptable and perfect.
text.Rotherham: And be not configuring yourselves unto this age, but be transforming yourselves by the renewing of your mind, to the end ye may be proving what is the thing willed by God—the good and acceptable and perfect.
text.DRB: And be not conformed to this world; but be reformed in the newness of your mind, that you may prove what the good, & acceptable, & perfect will of God is.
text.KJV: And be not conformed to this world: but be ye transformed by the renewing of your mind, that ye may prove what is that good, and acceptable, and perfect, will of God.
text.NABRE: Do not conform yourselves to this age but be transformed by the renewal of your mind, that you may discern what is the will of God, what is good and pleasing and perfect.

text.NRSV: For by the grace given to me I say to everyone among you not to think of yourself more highly than you ought to think, but to think with sober judgment, each according to the measure of faith that God has assigned.
text.Rotherham: For I say, through the favour which hath been given me, unto every one who is among you,—not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think, but so to think as to think soberly—as, unto each one, God hath dealt a measure of faith.
text.DRB: For I say by the grace that is given me, to all that are among you, not to be more wise than behoveth to be wise, but to be wise unto sobriety, to every one as God hath divided the measure of faith.
text.KJV: For I say, through the grace given unto me, to every man that is among you, not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think; but to think soberly, according as God hath dealt to every man the measure of faith.
text.NABRE: Many Parts in One Body. For by the grace given to me I tell everyone among you not to think of himself more highly than one ought to think, but to think soberly, each according to the measure of faith that God has apportioned.

text.NRSV: For as in one body we have many members, and not all the members have the same function,
text.Rotherham: For, just as in one body we have many members, yet, all the members, have not the same office,
text.DRB: For as in one body we have many members, but all the members have not one action;
text.KJV: For as we have many members in one body, and all members have not the same office:
text.NABRE: For as in one body we have many parts, and all the parts do not have the same function,

text.NRSV: so we, who are many, are one body in Christ, and individually we are members one of another.
text.Rotherham: So, we, the many, are one body in Christ, yet, severally, members one of another.
text.DRB: so we being many, are one body in Christ, & each one another's members.
text.KJV: So we, being many, are one body in Christ, and every one members one of another.
text.NABRE: so we, though many, are one body in Christ and individually parts of one another.

text.NRSV: We have gifts that differ according to the grace given to us: prophecy, in proportion to faith;
text.Rotherham: Now, having gifts of favour, according to the favour given unto us, differing—whether prophesying, [let us use it] according to the proportion of our faith,
text.DRB: And having gifts, according to the grace that is given us, different, either prophecy according to the rule of faith,
text.KJV: Having then gifts differing according to the grace that is given to us, whether prophecy, let us prophesy according to the proportion of faith;
text.NABRE: Since we have gifts that differ according to the grace given to us, let us exercise them: if prophecy, in proportion to the faith;

text.NRSV: ministry, in ministering; the teacher, in teaching;
text.Rotherham: Or ministering, [use it] in our ministering, or he that teacheth, [let him use his gift] in his teaching,
text.DRB: or ministry in ministering, or he that teacheth in doctrine,
text.KJV: Or ministry, let us wait on our ministering: or he that teacheth, on teaching;
text.NABRE: if ministry, in ministering; if one is a teacher, in teaching;

text.NRSV: the exhorter, in exhortation; the giver, in generosity; the leader, in diligence; the compassionate, in cheerfulness.
text.Rotherham: Or he that exhorteth, in his exhorting, he that imparteth, [let it be] with liberality, he that taketh a lead, with diligence, he that sheweth mercy, with cheerfulness.
text.DRB: he that exhorteth in exhorting, he that giveth in simplicity, he that ruleth in carefulness, he that sheweth mercy in cheerfulness.
text.KJV: Or he that exhorteth, on exhortation: he that giveth, let him do it with simplicity; he that ruleth, with diligence; he that sheweth mercy, with cheerfulness.
text.NABRE: if one exhorts, in exhortation; if one contributes, in generosity; if one is over others, with diligence; if one does acts of mercy, with cheerfulness.

text.NRSV: Let love be genuine; hate what is evil, hold fast to what is good;
text.Rotherham: Your love, [be] without hypocrisy,—loathing that which is wicked, cleaving to that which is good;
text.DRB: Love without simulation. Hating evil, cleaving to good.
text.KJV: Let love be without dissimulation. Abhor that which is evil; cleave to that which is good.
text.NABRE: Mutual Love. Let love be sincere; hate what is evil, hold on to what is good;

text.NRSV: love one another with mutual affection; outdo one another in showing honor.
text.Rotherham: In your brotherly love, unto one another, being tenderly affectioned, in honour, unto one another, giving preference;
text.DRB: Loving the charity of the brotherhood one toward another, with honour preventing one another.
text.KJV: Be kindly affectioned one to another with brotherly love; in honour preferring one another;
text.NABRE: love one another with mutual affection; anticipate one another in showing honor.

text.NRSV: Do not lag in zeal, be ardent in spirit, serve the Lord.
text.Rotherham: In business, not slothful, in spirit, fervent, to the Lord, doing service,
text.DRB: In carefulness not slothful. In spirit fervent. Serving our Lord.
text.KJV: Not slothful in business; fervent in spirit; serving the Lord;
text.NABRE: Do not grow slack in zeal, be fervent in spirit, serve the Lord.

text.NRSV: Rejoice in hope, be patient in suffering, persevere in prayer.
text.Rotherham: In hope, rejoicing, in tribulation, enduring, in prayer, persevering,
text.DRB: Rejoicing in hope. Patient in tribulation. Instant in prayer.
text.KJV: Rejoicing in hope; patient in tribulation; continuing instant in prayer;
text.NABRE: Rejoice in hope, endure in affliction, persevere in prayer.

text.NRSV: Contribute to the needs of the saints; extend hospitality to strangers.
text.Rotherham: With the needs of the saints, having fellowship,—hospitality, pursuing.
text.DRB: Communicating to the necessities of the saints. Pursuing hospitality.
text.KJV: Distributing to the necessity of saints; given to hospitality.
text.NABRE: Contribute to the needs of the holy ones, exercise hospitality.

text.NRSV: Bless those who persecute you; bless and do not curse them.
text.Rotherham: Bless them that persecute, bless, and do not curse.
text.DRB: Bless them that persecute you: bless, and curse not.
text.KJV: Bless them which persecute you: bless, and curse not.
text.NABRE: Bless those who persecute [you], bless and do not curse them.

text.NRSV: Rejoice with those who rejoice, weep with those who weep.
text.Rotherham: Rejoice with them that rejoice, weep with them that weep:
text.DRB: To rejoice with them that rejoice, to weep with them that weep.
text.KJV: Rejoice with them that do rejoice, and weep with them that weep.
text.NABRE: Rejoice with those who rejoice, weep with those who weep.

text.NRSV: Live in harmony with one another; do not be haughty, but associate with the lowly; do not claim to be wiser than you are.
text.Rotherham: The same thing, one to another regarding,—not the lofty things regarding, but, by the lowly, being led along. Be not getting presumptuous in your own opinion:
text.DRB: Being of one mind one toward another. Not minding high things, but consenting to the humble. Be not wise in your own conceit.
text.KJV: Be of the same mind one toward another. Mind not high things, but condescend to men of low estate. Be not wise in your own conceits.
text.NABRE: Have the same regard for one another; do not be haughty but associate with the lowly; do not be wise in your own estimation.

text.NRSV: Do not repay anyone evil for evil, but take thought for what is noble in the sight of all.
text.Rotherham: Unto no one, evil for evil rendering: providing honourable things before all men:
text.DRB: To no man rendering evil for evil. Providing good things not only before God, but also before all men.
text.KJV: Recompense to no man evil for evil. Provide things honest in the sight of all men.
text.NABRE: Do not repay anyone evil for evil; be concerned for what is noble in the sight of all.

text.NRSV: If it is possible, so far as it depends on you, live peaceably with all.
text.Rotherham: If possible—so far as dependeth on you, with all men being at peace:
text.DRB: If it may be, as much as is in you, having peace with all men.
text.KJV: If it be possible, as much as lieth in you, live peaceably with all men.
text.NABRE: If possible, on your part, live at peace with all.

text.NRSV: Beloved, never avenge yourselves, but leave room for the wrath of God; for it is written, “Vengeance is mine, I will repay, says the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Not avenging, yourselves, beloved, but give place unto their anger; for it is written—Mine, is avenging, I, will recompense;—saith the Lord;
text.DRB: Not revenging yourselves, my dearest, but give place unto wrath, for it is written: Revenge to me; I will reward, saith our Lord.
text.KJV: Dearly beloved, avenge not yourselves, but rather give place unto wrath: for it is written, Vengeance is mine; I will repay, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Beloved, do not look for revenge but leave room for the wrath; for it is written, “Vengeance is mine, I will repay, says the Lord.”

text.NRSV: No, “if your enemies are hungry, feed them; if they are thirsty, give them something to drink; for by doing this you will heap burning coals on their heads.”
text.Rotherham: But—if thine enemy hunger, be feeding him, if he thirst, be giving him drink; for, this doing, coals of fire, shalt thou heap upon his head.
text.DRB: but if thine enemy hunger, give him meat: if he thirst, give him drink. For, doing this, thou shalt heap coals of fire upon his head.
text.KJV: Therefore if thine enemy hunger, feed him; if he thirst, give him drink: for in so doing thou shalt heap coals of fire on his head.
text.NABRE: Rather, “if your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him something to drink; for by so doing you will heap burning coals upon his head.”

text.NRSV: Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.
text.Rotherham: Be not overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.
text.DRB: Be not overcome of evil, but overcome in good the evil.
text.KJV: Be not overcome of evil, but overcome evil with good.
text.NABRE: Do not be conquered by evil but conquer evil with good.

text.NRSV: Let every person be subject to the governing authorities; for there is no authority except from God, and those authorities that exist have been instituted by God.
text.Rotherham: Let, every soul, unto protecting authorities be in subjection; for there is no authority save by God, and, they that are in being, have by God been arranged,—
text.DRB: Let every soul be subject to higher powers, for there is no power but of God. And those that are, of God are ordained.
text.KJV: Let every soul be subject unto the higher powers. For there is no power but of God: the powers that be are ordained of God.
text.NABRE: Chapter 13 Obedience to Authority. Let every person be subordinate to the higher authorities, for there is no authority except from God, and those that exist have been established by God.

text.NRSV: Therefore whoever resists authority resists what God has appointed, and those who resist will incur judgment.
text.Rotherham: So that, he who rangeth himself against the authority, against the arrangement of God opposeth himself, and, they who oppose, shall unto themselves a sentence of judgment receive.
text.DRB: Therefore he that resisteth the power, resisteth the ordinance of God. And they that resist, purchase to themselves damnation.
text.KJV: Whosoever therefore resisteth the power, resisteth the ordinance of God: and they that resist shall receive to themselves damnation.
text.NABRE: Therefore, whoever resists authority opposes what God has appointed, and those who oppose it will bring judgment upon themselves.

text.NRSV: For rulers are not a terror to good conduct, but to bad. Do you wish to have no fear of the authority? Then do what is good, and you will receive its approval;
text.Rotherham: For, they who bear rule, are not a terror unto the good work but unto the evil. Wouldst thou not be afraid of the authority? That which is good, be thou doing, and thou shall have praise of the same;
text.DRB: For princes are no fear to the good work, but to the evil. But wilt thou not fear the power? Do good: and thou shalt have praise of the same.
text.KJV: For rulers are not a terror to good works, but to the evil. Wilt thou then not be afraid of the power? do that which is good, and thou shalt have praise of the same:
text.NABRE: For rulers are not a cause of fear to good conduct, but to evil. Do you wish to have no fear of authority? Then do what is good and you will receive approval from it,

text.NRSV: for it is God’s servant for your good. But if you do what is wrong, you should be afraid, for the authority does not bear the sword in vain! It is the servant of God to execute wrath on the wrongdoer.
text.Rotherham: For, God’s minister, is he unto thee for that which is good. But, if, that which is evil, thou be doing, be afraid! For, not in vain, the sword he beareth; for, God’s minister, he is,—an avenger, unto anger, to him who practiseth what is evil.
text.DRB: For he is God's minister unto thee for good. But if thou do evil, fear; for he beareth not the sword without cause. For he is God's minister: a revenger unto wrath, to him that doeth evil.
text.KJV: For he is the minister of God to thee for good. But if thou do that which is evil, be afraid; for he beareth not the sword in vain: for he is the minister of God, a revenger to execute wrath upon him that doeth evil.
text.NABRE: for it is a servant of God for your good. But if you do evil, be afraid, for it does not bear the sword without purpose; it is the servant of God to inflict wrath on the evildoer.

text.NRSV: Therefore one must be subject, not only because of wrath but also because of conscience.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore it is necessary to be in subjection,—not only because of the anger, but also because of the conscience;
text.DRB: Therefore be subject of necessity, not only for wrath, but also for conscience sake.
text.KJV: Wherefore ye must needs be subject, not only for wrath, but also for conscience sake.
text.NABRE: Therefore, it is necessary to be subject not only because of the wrath but also because of conscience.

text.NRSV: For the same reason you also pay taxes, for the authorities are God’s servants, busy with this very thing.
text.Rotherham: For, because of this, are ye paying tribute also,—for, God’s ministers of state, they are, unto this very thing, giving constant attendance.
text.DRB: For therefore you give tributes also. For they are the ministers of God, serving unto this purpose.
text.KJV: For for this cause pay ye tribute also: for they are God’s ministers, attending continually upon this very thing.
text.NABRE: This is why you also pay taxes, for the authorities are ministers of God, devoting themselves to this very thing.

text.NRSV: Pay to all what is due them—taxes to whom taxes are due, revenue to whom revenue is due, respect to whom respect is due, honor to whom honor is due.
text.Rotherham: Render unto all their dues,—unto whom tribute, tribute, unto whom tax, tax, unto whom fear, fear, unto whom honour, honour.
text.DRB: Render therefore to all men their dew: to whom tribute, tribute: to whom custom, custom: to whom fear, fear; to whom honour, honour.
text.KJV: Render therefore to all their dues: tribute to whom tribute is due; custom to whom custom; fear to whom fear; honour to whom honour.
text.NABRE: Pay to all their dues, taxes to whom taxes are due, toll to whom toll is due, respect to whom respect is due, honor to whom honor is due. Love Fulfills the Law.

text.NRSV: Owe no one anything, except to love one another; for the one who loves another has fulfilled the law.
text.Rotherham: Nothing to any, be owing—save to be loving one another; for, he that loveth his neighbour, hath given to, law, its fulfillment.
text.DRB: Owe no man any thing: but that you love one another. For he that loveth his neighbour, hath fulfilled the law.
text.KJV: Owe no man any thing, but to love one another: for he that loveth another hath fulfilled the law.
text.NABRE: Owe nothing to anyone, except to love one another; for the one who loves another has fulfilled the law.

text.NRSV: The commandments, “You shall not commit adultery; You shall not murder; You shall not steal; You shall not covet”; and any other commandment, are summed up in this word, “Love your neighbor as yourself.”
text.Rotherham: For, this—Thou shall not commit adultery, Thou shall not commit murder, Thou shall not steal, Thou shall not covet, and if there is any different commandment, in this word, is summed up, [namely] —Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.
text.DRB: For, Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt not kill, Thou shalt not steal. Thou shalt not bear false witness, Thou shalt not covet, and if there be any other commandment, it is comprised in this word, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.
text.KJV: For this, Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt not kill, Thou shalt not steal, Thou shalt not bear false witness, Thou shalt not covet; and if there be any other commandment, it is briefly comprehended in this saying, namely, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.
text.NABRE: The commandments, “You shall not commit adultery; you shall not kill; you shall not steal; you shall not covet,” and whatever other commandment there may be, are summed up in this saying, [namely] “You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”

text.NRSV: Love does no wrong to a neighbor; therefore, love is the fulfilling of the law.
text.Rotherham: Love, unto one’s neighbour, worketh not ill; Law’s fullness, therefore, is, love.
text.DRB: The love of thy neighbour, worketh no evil. Love therefore is the fullness of the Law.
text.KJV: Love worketh no ill to his neighbour: therefore love is the fulfilling of the law.
text.NABRE: Love does no evil to the neighbor; hence, love is the fulfillment of the law. Awareness of the End of Time.

text.NRSV: Besides this, you know what time it is, how it is now the moment for you to wake from sleep. For salvation is nearer to us now than when we became believers;
text.Rotherham: And, this besides,—knowing the season—that it is an hour already for you out of sleep to be wakened; for, now, is our salvation nearer than when we believed:
text.DRB: And that knowing the season, that it is now the hour for us to rise from sleep. For now our salvation is nearer than when we believed.
text.KJV: And that, knowing the time, that now it is high time to awake out of sleep: for now is our salvation nearer than when we believed.
text.NABRE: And do this because you know the time; it is the hour now for you to awake from sleep. For our salvation is nearer now than when we first believed;

text.NRSV: the night is far gone, the day is near. Let us then lay aside the works of darkness and put on the armor of light;
text.Rotherham: The night, is far spent and, the day, hath drawn near; let us, then, cast off the works of darkness, [and] let us put on the armour of light,—
text.DRB: The night is passed, and the day is at hand. Let us therefore cast off the works of darkness, & do on the armour of light.
text.KJV: The night is far spent, the day is at hand: let us therefore cast off the works of darkness, and let us put on the armour of light.
text.NABRE: the night is advanced, the day is at hand. Let us then throw off the works of darkness [and] put on the armor of light;

text.NRSV: let us live honorably as in the day, not in reveling and drunkenness, not in debauchery and licentiousness, not in quarreling and jealousy.
text.Rotherham: As in daytime, becomingly let us walk: not in revellings and in drunken bouts, not in chamberings and in wanton deeds, not in strife and envy;—
text.DRB: As in the day let us walk honestly: not in banquetings and drunkenness, not in chamberings and impudicities, not in contention and emulation:
text.KJV: Let us walk honestly, as in the day; not in rioting and drunkenness, not in chambering and wantonness, not in strife and envying.
text.NABRE: let us conduct ourselves properly as in the day, not in orgies and drunkenness, not in promiscuity and licentiousness, not in rivalry and jealousy.

text.NRSV: Instead, put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh, to gratify its desires.
text.Rotherham: But put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and, for the flesh, take not forethought to fulfil its covetings.
text.DRB: but do ye on our Lord Jesus Christ, and make not provision for the flesh in concupiscences.
text.KJV: But put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make not provision for the flesh, to fulfil the lusts thereof.
text.NABRE: But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the desires of the flesh.

text.NRSV: Welcome those who are weak in faith, but not for the purpose of quarreling over opinions.
text.Rotherham: Him that is weak in his faith, receive ye,—not for disputing opinions:—
text.DRB: And him that is weak in faith, take unto you; not in disputations of cogitations.
text.KJV: Him that is weak in the faith receive ye, but not to doubtful disputations.
text.NABRE: Chapter 14 To Live and Die for Christ. Welcome anyone who is weak in faith, but not for disputes over opinions.

text.NRSV: Some believe in eating anything, while the weak eat only vegetables.
text.Rotherham: One, indeed, hath faith to eat all things, whereas, he that is weak, eateth herbs:
text.DRB: For one believeth that he may eat all things: but he that is weak, let him eat herbs.
text.KJV: For one believeth that he may eat all things: another, who is weak, eateth herbs.
text.NABRE: One person believes that one may eat anything, while the weak person eats only vegetables.

text.NRSV: Those who eat must not despise those who abstain, and those who abstain must not judge those who eat; for God has welcomed them.
text.Rotherham: Let not, him that eateth, despise him that eateth not, and let not, him that eateth not, judge him that eateth; for, God, hath received him.
text.DRB: Let not him that eateth, despise him that eateth not: and he that eateth not, let him not judge him that eateth. For God hath taken him to him.
text.KJV: Let not him that eateth despise him that eateth not; and let not him which eateth not judge him that eateth: for God hath received him.
text.NABRE: The one who eats must not despise the one who abstains, and the one who abstains must not pass judgment on the one who eats; for God has welcomed him.

text.NRSV: Who are you to judge someone else’s servant? It is before their own lord that they stand or fall. And they will be upheld, for the Lord is able to make them stand.
text.Rotherham: Who art, thou, that judgest another’s domestic? To his own master, he standeth or falleth; he shall, however, be made to stand,—for his master is able to make him stand.
text.DRB: Who art thou that judgest another man's servant? To his own Lord he standeth or falleth. And he shall stand: for God is able to make him stand.
text.KJV: Who art thou that judgest another man’s servant? to his own master he standeth or falleth. Yea, he shall be holden up: for God is able to make him stand.
text.NABRE: Who are you to pass judgment on someone else’s servant? Before his own master he stands or falls. And he will be upheld, for the Lord is able to make him stand.

text.NRSV: Some judge one day to be better than another, while others judge all days to be alike. Let all be fully convinced in their own minds.
text.Rotherham: [For], one, indeed esteemeth one day beyond another, whereas, another, esteemeth every day:—let, each one, in his own mind be fully persuaded.
text.DRB: For one judgeth between day and day; and another judgeth every day: let every one abound in his own sense.
text.KJV: One man esteemeth one day above another: another esteemeth every day alike. Let every man be fully persuaded in his own mind.
text.NABRE: [For] one person considers one day more important than another, while another person considers all days alike. Let everyone be fully persuaded in his own mind.

text.NRSV: Those who observe the day, observe it in honor of the Lord. Also those who eat, eat in honor of the Lord, since they give thanks to God; while those who abstain, abstain in honor of the Lord and give thanks to God.
text.Rotherham: He that regardeth the day, unto the Lord, regardeth it,—and, he that eateth, unto the Lord, doth eat, for he giveth thanks unto God; and, he that eateth not, unto the Lord, doth not eat and give God thanks.
text.DRB: He that respecteth the day, respecteth to our Lord. And he that eateth, eateth to our Lord: for he giveth thanks to God. And he that eateth not, to our Lord he eateth not, and giveth thanks to God.
text.KJV: He that regardeth the day, regardeth it unto the Lord; and he that regardeth not the day, to the Lord he doth not regard it. He that eateth, eateth to the Lord, for he giveth God thanks; and he that eateth not, to the Lord he eateth not, and giveth God thanks.
text.NABRE: Whoever observes the day, observes it for the Lord. Also whoever eats, eats for the Lord, since he gives thanks to God; while whoever abstains, abstains for the Lord and gives thanks to God.

text.NRSV: We do not live to ourselves, and we do not die to ourselves.
text.Rotherham: For, none of us, unto himself liveth, and, none, unto himself dieth;
text.DRB: For none of us liveth to himself: and no man dieth to himself.
text.KJV: For none of us liveth to himself, and no man dieth to himself.
text.NABRE: None of us lives for oneself, and no one dies for oneself.

text.NRSV: If we live, we live to the Lord, and if we die, we die to the Lord; so then, whether we live or whether we die, we are the Lord’s.
text.Rotherham: For both, if we live, unto the Lord, we live, and, if we die, unto the Lord, we die; whether therefore we live, the Lord’s, we are; or whether we die, the Lord’s, we are;
text.DRB: For whether we live, we live to our Lord; or whether we die, we die to our Lord. Therefore whether we live, or whether we die, we are our Lord's.
text.KJV: For whether we live, we live unto the Lord; and whether we die, we die unto the Lord: whether we live therefore, or die, we are the Lord’s.
text.NABRE: For if we live, we live for the Lord, and if we die, we die for the Lord; so then, whether we live or die, we are the Lord’s.

text.NRSV: For to this end Christ died and lived again, so that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living.
text.Rotherham: For, to this end, Christ died and lived, in order that, both of dead and living, he might have lordship.
text.DRB: For to this end Christ died and rose again; that he may have dominion both of the dead and of the living.
text.KJV: For to this end Christ both died, and rose, and revived, that he might be Lord both of the dead and living.
text.NABRE: For this is why Christ died and came to life, that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living.

text.NRSV: Why do you pass judgment on your brother or sister? Or you, why do you despise your brother or sister? For we will all stand before the judgment seat of God.
text.Rotherham: But, thou, why dost thou judge thy brother? Aye! and thou, why dost thou despise thy brother? For, all of us, shall present ourselves unto the judgment seat of God;
text.DRB: But thou, why judgest thou thy brother? or thou, why doest thou despise thy brother? For we shall all stand before the judgement seat of Christ.
text.KJV: But why dost thou judge thy brother? or why dost thou set at nought thy brother? for we shall all stand before the judgment seat of Christ.
text.NABRE: Why then do you judge your brother? Or you, why do you look down on your brother? For we shall all stand before the judgment seat of God;

text.NRSV: For it is written, “As I live, says the Lord, every knee shall bow to me, and every tongue shall give praise to God.”
text.Rotherham: For it is written—Living am, I, saith the Lord, unto me, shall bow every knee, and, every tongue, shall openly confess unto God.
text.DRB: For it is written: Live I, saith our Lord, that every knee shall bow to me; & every tongue shall confess to God.
text.KJV: For it is written, As I live, saith the Lord, every knee shall bow to me, and every tongue shall confess to God.
text.NABRE: for it is written: “As I live, says the Lord, every knee shall bend before me, and every tongue shall give praise to God.”

text.NRSV: So then, each of us will be accountable to God.
text.Rotherham: Hence, [then], each one of us, of himself shall give account unto God.
text.DRB: Therefore every one of us for himself shall render account to God.
text.KJV: So then every one of us shall give account of himself to God.
text.NABRE: So [then] each of us shall give an account of himself [to God].

text.NRSV: Let us therefore no longer pass judgment on one another, but resolve instead never to put a stumbling block or hindrance in the way of another.
text.Rotherham: No longer, then, upon one another, let us be sitting in judgment, but, this, judge ye rather—not to be putting a cause of stumbling before your brother or an occasion to fall.
text.DRB: Let us therefore no more judge one another. But this judge ye rather, that you put not a stumbling block or a scandal to your brother.
text.KJV: Let us not therefore judge one another any more: but judge this rather, that no man put a stumblingblock or an occasion to fall in his brother’s way.
text.NABRE: Consideration for the Weak Conscience. Then let us no longer judge one another, but rather resolve never to put a stumbling block or hindrance in the way of a brother.

text.NRSV: I know and am persuaded in the Lord Jesus that nothing is unclean in itself; but it is unclean for anyone who thinks it unclean.
text.Rotherham: I know and am persuaded in the Lord Jesus—that, nothing, is profane of itself,—save to him who reckoneth anything to be profane, unto that man, [it is] profane,
text.DRB: I know and am persuaded in our Lord Jesus Christ, that nothing is common of itself, but to him that supposeth any thing to be common, to him it is common.
text.KJV: I know, and am persuaded by the Lord Jesus, that there is nothing unclean of itself: but to him that esteemeth any thing to be unclean, to him it is unclean.
text.NABRE: I know and am convinced in the Lord Jesus that nothing is unclean in itself; still, it is unclean for someone who thinks it unclean.

text.NRSV: If your brother or sister is being injured by what you eat, you are no longer walking in love. Do not let what you eat cause the ruin of one for whom Christ died.
text.Rotherham: If, in fact, because of food, thy brother is being grieved, no longer, by the rule of love, art thou walking:—do not, by thy food, that man, be destroying, on whose behalf Christ died!
text.DRB: For if because of meat thy brother be grieved; now thou walkest not according to charity. Do not with thy meat destroy him for whom Christ died.
text.KJV: But if thy brother be grieved with thy meat, now walkest thou not charitably. Destroy not him with thy meat, for whom Christ died.
text.NABRE: If your brother is being hurt by what you eat, your conduct is no longer in accord with love. Do not because of your food destroy him for whom Christ died.

text.NRSV: So do not let your good be spoken of as evil.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, suffer not to be defamed, your own good thing;
text.DRB: Let not then our good be blasphemed.
text.KJV: Let not then your good be evil spoken of:
text.NABRE: So do not let your good be reviled.

text.NRSV: For the kingdom of God is not food and drink but righteousness and peace and joy in the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: For the kingdom of God is not eating and drinking, but righteousness and peace and joy in Holy Spirit;
text.DRB: For the Kingdom of God is not meat and drink; but justice, and peace, and joy in the Holy Ghost.
text.KJV: For the kingdom of God is not meat and drink; but righteousness, and peace, and joy in the Holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: For the kingdom of God is not a matter of food and drink, but of righteousness, peace, and joy in the holy Spirit;

text.NRSV: The one who thus serves Christ is acceptable to God and has human approval.
text.Rotherham: For, he that in this doeth service unto the Christ, is acceptable unto God, and approved unto men.
text.DRB: For he that in this serveth Christ, pleaseth God, and is acceptable to men.
text.KJV: For he that in these things serveth Christ is acceptable to God, and approved of men.
text.NABRE: whoever serves Christ in this way is pleasing to God and approved by others.

text.NRSV: Let us then pursue what makes for peace and for mutual upbuilding.
text.Rotherham: Hence, then, the things pertaining to peace, let us pursue, and the things which belong to the upbuilding one of another:
