text.DRB: Therefore the things that are of peace let us pursue: and the things that are of edifying one toward another let us keep.
text.KJV: Let us therefore follow after the things which make for peace, and things wherewith one may edify another.
text.NABRE: Let us then pursue what leads to peace and to building up one another.

text.NRSV: Do not, for the sake of food, destroy the work of God. Everything is indeed clean, but it is wrong for you to make others fall by what you eat;
text.Rotherham: Do not, for the sake of food, be throwing down the work of God! All things, indeed, are pure; but, ill, is it for the man who with occasion of stumbling doth eat,—
text.DRB: Destroy not the work of God for meat. All things indeed are clean: but it is ill for the man that eateth by giving offence.
text.KJV: For meat destroy not the work of God. All things indeed are pure; but it is evil for that man who eateth with offence.
text.NABRE: For the sake of food, do not destroy the work of God. Everything is indeed clean, but it is wrong for anyone to become a stumbling block by eating;

text.NRSV: it is good not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything that makes your brother or sister stumble.
text.Rotherham: Well, is it not to eat flesh nor to drink wine nor [to do aught] whereby thy brother is caused to stumble.
text.DRB: It is good not to eat flesh, and not to drink wine, nor that wherein thy brother is offended, or scandalized, or weakened.
text.KJV: It is good neither to eat flesh, nor to drink wine, nor any thing whereby thy brother stumbleth, or is offended, or is made weak.
text.NABRE: it is good not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything that causes your brother to stumble.

text.NRSV: The faith that you have, have as your own conviction before God. Blessed are those who have no reason to condemn themselves because of what they approve.
text.Rotherham: The faith which thou hast, have to thyself before God: happy, he that bringeth not judgment upon himself by that which he approveth;
text.DRB: Hast thou faith? have it with thyself before God. Blessed is he that judgeth not himself in that which he approveth.
text.KJV: Hast thou faith? have it to thyself before God. Happy is he that condemneth not himself in that thing which he alloweth.
text.NABRE: Keep the faith [that] you have to yourself in the presence of God; blessed is the one who does not condemn himself for what he approves.

text.NRSV: But those who have doubts are condemned if they eat, because they do not eat from faith; for whatever does not proceed from faith is sin.
text.Rotherham: But, he that is in doubt, if he eat, hath condemned himself,—because, [it was] not of faith, and, everything which is not of faith, is sin.
text.DRB: But he that discerneth, if he eat, is damned; because not of faith. For all that is not of faith, is sin.
text.KJV: And he that doubteth is damned if he eat, because he eateth not of faith: for whatsoever is not of faith is sin.
text.NABRE: But whoever has doubts is condemned if he eats, because this is not from faith; for whatever is not from faith is sin.

text.NRSV: We who are strong ought to put up with the failings of the weak, and not to please ourselves.
text.Rotherham: We are bound, however, we, who are strong, the weakness of them who are not strong to be bearing, and not, unto ourselves, to give pleasure.
text.DRB: And we that are the stronger, must sustain the infirmities of the weak, & not please ourselves.
text.KJV: We then that are strong ought to bear the infirmities of the weak, and not to please ourselves.
text.NABRE: Chapter 15 Patience and Self-Denial. We who are strong ought to put up with the failings of the weak and not to please ourselves;

text.NRSV: Each of us must please our neighbor for the good purpose of building up the neighbor.
text.Rotherham: Let, each one of us, unto his neighbour give pleasure—for what is good unto upbuilding;
text.DRB: Let every one of you please his neighbour unto good, to edification.
text.KJV: Let every one of us please his neighbour for his good to edification.
text.NABRE: let each of us please our neighbor for the good, for building up.

text.NRSV: For Christ did not please himself; but as it is written, “The insults of those who insult you have fallen on me.”
text.Rotherham: For, even the Christ, not unto himself gave pleasure, but, even as it is written—The reproaches of them that we reproaching thee, fell upon me:
text.DRB: For, Christ did not please himself, but as it is written: The reproaches of them that reproached thee, fell upon me.
text.KJV: For even Christ pleased not himself; but, as it is written, The reproaches of them that reproached thee fell on me.
text.NABRE: For Christ did not please himself; but, as it is written, “The insults of those who insult you fall upon me.”

text.NRSV: For whatever was written in former days was written for our instruction, so that by steadfastness and by the encouragement of the scriptures we might have hope.
text.Rotherham: Whatsoever things, in fact, were written aforetime, [all] for our own instruction were written,—in order that, through endurance and through the encouragement of the Scriptures, we might have their hope.
text.DRB: For what things soever have been written, to our learning they are written: that by the patience and consolation of the Scriptures, we may have hope.
text.KJV: For whatsoever things were written aforetime were written for our learning, that we through patience and comfort of the scriptures might have hope.
text.NABRE: For whatever was written previously was written for our instruction, that by endurance and by the encouragement of the scriptures we might have hope.

text.NRSV: May the God of steadfastness and encouragement grant you to live in such harmony with one another, in accord with Christ Jesus,
text.Rotherham: Now may, the God of the endurance and of the encouragement, give you, the same thing, to be regarding amongst one another, according to Christ Jesus;
text.DRB: And the God of patience and of comfort give you to be of one mind one toward another according to Jesus Christ:
text.KJV: Now the God of patience and consolation grant you to be likeminded one toward another according to Christ Jesus:
text.NABRE: May the God of endurance and encouragement grant you to think in harmony with one another, in keeping with Christ Jesus,

text.NRSV: so that together you may with one voice glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: In order that, with one accord, with one mouth, ye maybe glorifying the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: that of one mind, with one mouth you may glorify God & the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: That ye may with one mind and one mouth glorify God, even the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: that with one accord you may with one voice glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. God’s Fidelity and Mercy.

text.NRSV: Welcome one another, therefore, just as Christ has welcomed you, for the glory of God.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore receive ye one another, even as, Christ also, hath received us unto the glory of God:—
text.DRB: For the which cause receive one another; as Christ also hath received you unto the honour of God.
text.KJV: Wherefore receive ye one another, as Christ also received us to the glory of God.
text.NABRE: Welcome one another, then, as Christ welcomed you, for the glory of God.

text.NRSV: For I tell you that Christ has become a servant of the circumcised on behalf of the truth of God in order that he might confirm the promises given to the patriarchs,
text.Rotherham: For I affirm Christ to have become a minister of circumcision in behalf of the truth of God,—to confirm the promises of the fathers,
text.DRB: For I say Christ Jesus to have been minister of the circumcision for the verity of God to confirm the promises of the fathers.
text.KJV: Now I say that Jesus Christ was a minister of the circumcision for the truth of God, to confirm the promises made unto the fathers:
text.NABRE: For I say that Christ became a minister of the circumcised to show God’s truthfulness, to confirm the promises to the patriarchs,

text.NRSV: and in order that the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy. As it is written, “Therefore I will confess you among the Gentiles, and sing praises to your name”;
text.Rotherham: And that, the nations, for mercy should glorify God:—even as it is written—For this cause, will I openly confess unto thee among nations, and, unto thy name, will strike the strings;
text.DRB: But the Gentiles to honour God for his mercy, as it is written: Therefore will I confess to thee in the Gentiles o Lord, and will sing to thy name.
text.KJV: And that the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy; as it is written, For this cause I will confess to thee among the Gentiles, and sing unto thy name.
text.NABRE: but so that the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy. As it is written: “Therefore, I will praise you among the Gentiles and sing praises to your name.”

text.NRSV: and again he says, “Rejoice, O Gentiles, with his people”;
text.Rotherham: And again he saith—Be glad ye nations with his people;
text.DRB: And again he saith: Rejoice ye Gentiles with his people.
text.KJV: And again he saith, Rejoice, ye Gentiles, with his people.
text.NABRE: And again it says: “Rejoice, O Gentiles, with his people.”

text.NRSV: and again, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and let all the peoples praise him”;
text.Rotherham: And again,—Be giving praise all ye nations unto the Lord, and let all the peoples repeat his praise;
text.DRB: And again: Praise all ye Gentiles our Lord; and magnify him all ye peoples.
text.KJV: And again, Praise the Lord, all ye Gentiles; and laud him, all ye people.
text.NABRE: And again: “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and let all the peoples praise him.”

text.NRSV: and again Isaiah says, “The root of Jesse shall come, the one who rises to rule the Gentiles; in him the Gentiles shall hope.”
text.Rotherham: And, again, Isaiah, saith—There shall be the root of Jesse, and he that ariseth to rule nations,—upon, him, shall nations hope.
text.DRB: And again Esaie saith: There shall be the root of Jesse; and he that shall rise up to rule the Gentiles, in him the Gentiles shall hope.
text.KJV: And again, Esaias saith, There shall be a root of Jesse, and he that shall rise to reign over the Gentiles; in him shall the Gentiles trust.
text.NABRE: And again Isaiah says: “The root of Jesse shall come, raised up to rule the Gentiles; in him shall the Gentiles hope.”

text.NRSV: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that you may abound in hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: Now, the God of the hope, fill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that ye may surpass in the hope, in the power of Holy Spirit.
text.DRB: And the God of hope replenish you with all joy and peace in believing; that you may abound in hope, and in the virtue of the Holy Ghost.
text.KJV: Now the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that ye may abound in hope, through the power of the Holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that you may abound in hope by the power of the holy Spirit.

text.NRSV: I myself feel confident about you, my brothers and sisters, that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, and able to instruct one another.
text.Rotherham: But I am persuaded my brethren,—even, I myself, concerning you: that, ye yourselves, also are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, able also, unto one another, to be ministering admonition:
text.DRB: And I myself also, my brethren, am assured of you, that you also are full of love, replenished with all knowledge, so that you are able to admonish one another.
text.KJV: And I myself also am persuaded of you, my brethren, that ye also are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, able also to admonish one another.
text.NABRE: VII. Conclusion Apostle to the Gentiles. I myself am convinced about you, my brothers, that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, and able to admonish one another.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless on some points I have written to you rather boldly by way of reminder, because of the grace given me by God
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, the more boldly, have I written unto you,—in some measure, as bringing it back to your minds,—by reason of the favour given unto me from God,—
text.DRB: But I have written to you (brethren) more boldly in part, as it were putting you in remembrance; for the grace which is given me of God,
text.KJV: Nevertheless, brethren, I have written the more boldly unto you in some sort, as putting you in mind, because of the grace that is given to me of God,
text.NABRE: But I have written to you rather boldly in some respects to remind you, because of the grace given me by God

text.NRSV: to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles in the priestly service of the gospel of God, so that the offering of the Gentiles may be acceptable, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: That I should be a public minister of Christ Jesus unto the nations, doing priestly service with the glad-message of God, in order that the offering up of the nations might prove to be acceptable, being hallowed in Holy Spirit:
text.DRB: to be the minister of Christ Jesus in the Gentiles: sanctifying the Gospel of God, that the oblation of the Gentiles may be made acceptable and sanctified in the Holy Ghost.
text.KJV: That I should be the minister of Jesus Christ to the Gentiles, ministering the gospel of God, that the offering up of the Gentiles might be acceptable, being sanctified by the Holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles in performing the priestly service of the gospel of God, so that the offering up of the Gentiles may be acceptable, sanctified by the holy Spirit.

text.NRSV: In Christ Jesus, then, I have reason to boast of my work for God.
text.Rotherham: I have, therefore, [my] boasting in Christ Jesus in the things pertaining to God,—
text.DRB: I have therefore glory in Christ Jesus toward God.
text.KJV: I have therefore whereof I may glory through Jesus Christ in those things which pertain to God.
text.NABRE: In Christ Jesus, then, I have reason to boast in what pertains to God.

text.NRSV: For I will not venture to speak of anything except what Christ has accomplished through me to win obedience from the Gentiles, by word and deed,
text.Rotherham: For I wilt not dare to speak anything save of the things which Christ hath wrought out for himself through me for the obedience of nations,—by word and deed,
text.DRB: For I dare not speak any of those things which Christ worketh not by me for the obedience of the Gentiles, by word and deeds,
text.KJV: For I will not dare to speak of any of those things which Christ hath not wrought by me, to make the Gentiles obedient, by word and deed,
text.NABRE: For I will not dare to speak of anything except what Christ has accomplished through me to lead the Gentiles to obedience by word and deed,

text.NRSV: by the power of signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit of God, so that from Jerusalem and as far around as Illyricum I have fully proclaimed the good news of Christ.
text.Rotherham: By the power of signs and wonders, by the power of [Holy Spirit]; so that I, from Jerusalem and in a circuit as far as Illyricum, have fulfilled the glad-message of the Christ,—
text.DRB: in the virtue of signs and wonders, in the virtue of the Holy Ghost: so that from Jerusalem round about unto Illyricum I have replenished the Gospel of Christ.
text.KJV: Through mighty signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit of God; so that from Jerusalem, and round about unto Illyricum, I have fully preached the gospel of Christ.
text.NABRE: by the power of signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit [of God], so that from Jerusalem all the way around to Illyricum I have finished preaching the gospel of Christ.

text.NRSV: Thus I make it my ambition to proclaim the good news, not where Christ has already been named, so that I do not build on someone else’s foundation,
text.Rotherham: Although, thus,—as ambitious to be announcing the glad-message—not where Christ had been named, lest, upon another’s foundation, I should be building;
text.DRB: And I have so preached this Gospel, not where Christ was named, lest I should build upon another man's foundation;
text.KJV: Yea, so have I strived to preach the gospel, not where Christ was named, lest I should build upon another man’s foundation:
text.NABRE: Thus I aspire to proclaim the gospel not where Christ has already been named, so that I do not build on another’s foundation,

text.NRSV: but as it is written, “Those who have never been told of him shall see, and those who have never heard shall understand.”
text.Rotherham: But, even as it is written—They shall see unto whom had been announced no tidings concerning him, and, they who had not heard, shall understand.
text.DRB: but as it is written: They to whom it hath not been preached of him, shall see, and they that have not heard, shall understand.
text.KJV: But as it is written, To whom he was not spoken of, they shall see: and they that have not heard shall understand.
text.NABRE: but as it is written: “Those who have never been told of him shall see, and those who have never heard of him shall understand.”

text.NRSV: This is the reason that I have so often been hindered from coming to you.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, also, I have been hindered these many times from coming unto you;
text.DRB: For the which cause also I was hindered very much from coming unto you.
text.KJV: For which cause also I have been much hindered from coming to you.
text.NABRE: Paul’s Plans; Need for Prayers. That is why I have so often been prevented from coming to you.

text.NRSV: But now, with no further place for me in these regions, I have also for many years had a desire to come to you.
text.Rotherham: But, now, no longer having place in these regions, and having had, a longing, to come unto you a good many years,—
text.DRB: But now having no longer place in these countries, and having a desire to come unto you these many years now passed:
text.KJV: But now having no more place in these parts, and having a great desire these many years to come unto you;
text.NABRE: But now, since I no longer have any opportunity in these regions and since I have desired to come to you for many years,

text.NRSV: I hope to see you in passing as I go to Spain, and to be sent on my way there by you, once I have enjoyed your company for a little while.
text.Rotherham: As soon as I am journey to Spain,—I am hoping, in fact, when journeying through to get sight of you, and by you to be set forward thither,—if, with your company, I have first in some measure been satisfied.
text.DRB: when I shall begin to take my journey into Spain, I hope that as I pass, I shall see you, and be brought thither of you, if first in part I shall have enjoyed you.
text.KJV: Whensoever I take my journey into Spain, I will come to you: for I trust to see you in my journey, and to be brought on my way thitherward by you, if first I be somewhat filled with your company.
text.NABRE: I hope to see you in passing as I go to Spain and to be sent on my way there by you, after I have enjoyed being with you for a time.

text.NRSV: At present, however, I am going to Jerusalem in a ministry to the saints;
text.Rotherham: But, now, I am journeying unto Jerusalem, ministering unto the saints;
text.DRB: Now therefore I will go unto Jerusalem to minister to the saints.
text.KJV: But now I go unto Jerusalem to minister unto the saints.
text.NABRE: Now, however, I am going to Jerusalem to minister to the holy ones.

text.NRSV: for Macedonia and Achaia have been pleased to share their resources with the poor among the saints at Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: For Macedonia and Achaia have been well-pleased to make a certain fellowship for the destitute of the saints who are in Jerusalem,—
text.DRB: For Macedonia and Achaia have liked well to make some contribution upon the poor saints that are in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: For it hath pleased them of Macedonia and Achaia to make a certain contribution for the poor saints which are at Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: For Macedonia and Achaia have decided to make some contribution for the poor among the holy ones in Jerusalem;

text.NRSV: They were pleased to do this, and indeed they owe it to them; for if the Gentiles have come to share in their spiritual blessings, they ought also to be of service to them in material blessings.
text.Rotherham: They have been well-pleased, indeed,—and, their debtors, they are; for, if, with their spiritual things the nations have come into fellowship, they are bound also with their own carnal things to minister publicly unto them.
text.DRB: For it hath pleased them: and they are their debters. For if the Gentiles be made partakers of their spiritual things; they ought also in carnal things to minister unto them.
text.KJV: It hath pleased them verily; and their debtors they are. For if the Gentiles have been made partakers of their spiritual things, their duty is also to minister unto them in carnal things.
text.NABRE: they decided to do it, and in fact they are indebted to them, for if the Gentiles have come to share in their spiritual blessings, they ought also to serve them in material blessings.

text.NRSV: So, when I have completed this, and have delivered to them what has been collected, I will set out by way of you to Spain;
text.Rotherham: This, then, having completed, and sealed unto them this fruit, I will come back by you unto Spain:—
text.DRB: This therefore when I shall have accomplished, and signed them this fruit, I will go by you into Spain.
text.KJV: When therefore I have performed this, and have sealed to them this fruit, I will come by you into Spain.
text.NABRE: So when I have completed this and safely handed over this contribution to them, I shall set out by way of you to Spain;

text.NRSV: and I know that when I come to you, I will come in the fullness of the blessing of Christ.
text.Rotherham: And know that, when I come unto you, in the fullness of the blessing of Christ, I shall come.
text.DRB: And I know that coming to you, I shall come in aboundance of the blessing of Christ.
text.KJV: And I am sure that, when I come unto you, I shall come in the fulness of the blessing of the gospel of Christ.
text.NABRE: and I know that in coming to you I shall come in the fullness of Christ’s blessing.

text.NRSV: I appeal to you, brothers and sisters, by our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love of the Spirit, to join me in earnest prayer to God on my behalf,
text.Rotherham: I beseech you, moreover, [brethren], through our Lord Jesus Christ, and through the love of the Spirit, to strive together with me in your prayers on my behalf unto God,—
text.DRB: I beseech you therefore brethren by our Lord Jesus Christ, and by the charity of the Holy Ghost, that you help me in your prayers for me to God,
text.KJV: Now I beseech you, brethren, for the Lord Jesus Christ’s sake, and for the love of the Spirit, that ye strive together with me in your prayers to God for me;
text.NABRE: I urge you, [brothers,] by our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love of the Spirit, to join me in the struggle by your prayers to God on my behalf,

text.NRSV: that I may be rescued from the unbelievers in Judea, and that my ministry to Jerusalem may be acceptable to the saints,
text.Rotherham: In order that I may be delivered from them who do not yield in Judaea, and, my ministry which is unto Jerusalem, may prove to be acceptable unto the saints;
text.DRB: that I may be delivered from the infidels that are in Jewry, and the oblation of my service may become acceptable in Jerusalem to the saints,
text.KJV: That I may be delivered from them that do not believe in Judea; and that my service which I have for Jerusalem may be accepted of the saints;
text.NABRE: that I may be delivered from the disobedient in Judea, and that my ministry for Jerusalem may be acceptable to the holy ones,

text.NRSV: so that by God’s will I may come to you with joy and be refreshed in your company.
text.Rotherham: In order that, with joy coming unto you through God’s will, I may together with you find rest.
text.DRB: that I may come to you in joy by the will of God, that I may be refreshed with you.
text.KJV: That I may come unto you with joy by the will of God, and may with you be refreshed.
text.NABRE: so that I may come to you with joy by the will of God and be refreshed together with you.

text.NRSV: The God of peace be with all of you. Amen.
text.Rotherham: Now, the God of peace, be with you all. Amen.
text.DRB: And the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
text.KJV: Now the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
text.NABRE: The God of peace be with all of you. Amen.

text.KJV: I commend unto you Phebe our sister, which is a servant of the church which is at Cenchrea:
text.NABRE: Chapter 16 Phoebe Commended. I commend to you Phoebe our sister, who is [also] a minister of the church at Cenchreae,
text.NRSV: I commend to you our sister Phoebe, a deacon of the church at Cenchreae,
text.DRB: And I commend to you Phoebe our sister, who is in the ministry of the Church that is in Cenchris:
text.Rotherham: And I commend to you Phoebe our sister,—being a minister [also] of the assembly which is in Cenchreae;

text.KJV: That ye receive her in the Lord, as becometh saints, and that ye assist her in whatsoever business she hath need of you: for she hath been a succourer of many, and of myself also.
text.NABRE: that you may receive her in the Lord in a manner worthy of the holy ones, and help her in whatever she may need from you, for she has been a benefactor to many and to me as well.
text.NRSV: so that you may welcome her in the Lord as is fitting for the saints, and help her in whatever she may require from you, for she has been a benefactor of many and of myself as well.
text.DRB: that you receive her in our Lord as it is worthy for saints: and that you assist her in whatsoever business she shall need you. For she also hath assisted many, and myself.
text.Rotherham: In order that ye may give her welcome in the Lord in a manner worthy of the saints, and stand by her in any matter wherein she may have need of you; for, she also, hath proved to be a defender of many, and of my own self.

text.KJV: Greet Priscilla and Aquila my helpers in Christ Jesus:
text.NABRE: Paul’s Greetings. Greet Prisca and Aquila, my co-workers in Christ Jesus,
text.NRSV: Greet Prisca and Aquila, who work with me in Christ Jesus,
text.DRB: Salute Prisca & Aquila my helpers in Christ Jesus,
text.Rotherham: Salute ye Prisca and Aquila, my fellow-workers in Christ Jesus,—

text.KJV: Who have for my life laid down their own necks: unto whom not only I give thanks, but also all the churches of the Gentiles.
text.NABRE: who risked their necks for my life, to whom not only I am grateful but also all the churches of the Gentiles;
text.NRSV: and who risked their necks for my life, to whom not only I give thanks, but also all the churches of the Gentiles.
text.DRB: who for my life have laid down their necks; to whom not I only give thanks, but also all the Churches of the Gentiles,
text.Rotherham: Who, indeed, for my life, their own neck laid down,—unto whom—not, I only, give thanks, but also all the assemblies of the nations;

text.KJV: Likewise greet the church that is in their house. Salute my wellbeloved Epenetus, who is the firstfruits of Achaia unto Christ.
text.NABRE: greet also the church at their house. Greet my beloved Epaenetus, who was the firstfruits in Asia for Christ.
text.NRSV: Greet also the church in their house. Greet my beloved Epaenetus, who was the first convert in Asia for Christ.
text.DRB: and their domestical Church. Salute Epænetus my beloved: who is the first fruit of Asia in Christ.
text.Rotherham: [Salute] also the assembly at their house. Salute ye Epaenetus, my beloved, who is a first-fruit of Asia unto Christ.

text.KJV: Greet Mary, who bestowed much labour on us.
text.NABRE: Greet Mary, who has worked hard for you.
text.NRSV: Greet Mary, who has worked very hard among you.
text.DRB: Salute Mary who hath laboured much about us.
text.Rotherham: Salute Mary—who, indeed, hath toiled much for you.

text.KJV: Salute Andronicus and Junia, my kinsmen, and my fellowprisoners, who are of note among the apostles, who also were in Christ before me.
text.NABRE: Greet Andronicus and Junia, my relatives and my fellow prisoners; they are prominent among the apostles and they were in Christ before me.
text.NRSV: Greet Andronicus and Junia, my relatives who were in prison with me; they are prominent among the apostles, and they were in Christ before I was.
text.DRB: Salute Andronicus and Iulia my cousins and fellow captives: who are noble among the Apostles, who also before me were in Christ.
text.Rotherham: Salute Andronicus and Junias, my kinsmen and my fellow-captives, who, indeed, are of note among the Apostles, who also before me had come to be in Christ.

text.KJV: Greet Amplias my beloved in the Lord.
text.NABRE: Greet Ampliatus, my beloved in the Lord.
text.NRSV: Greet Ampliatus, my beloved in the Lord.
text.DRB: Salute Ampliatus my best beloved in our Lord.
text.Rotherham: Salute Ampliatus, my beloved in the Lord.

text.KJV: Salute Urbane, our helper in Christ, and Stachys my beloved.
text.NABRE: Greet Urbanus, our co-worker in Christ, and my beloved Stachys.
text.NRSV: Greet Urbanus, our co-worker in Christ, and my beloved Stachys.
text.DRB: Salute Urbanus our helper in Christ Jesus, and Stachys my beloved.
text.Rotherham: Salute Urbanus, our fellow-worker in Christ; and Stachys, my beloved.

text.KJV: Salute Apelles approved in Christ. Salute them which are of Aristobulus’ household.
text.NABRE: Greet Apelles, who is approved in Christ. Greet those who belong to the family of Aristobulus.
text.NRSV: Greet Apelles, who is approved in Christ. Greet those who belong to the family of Aristobulus.
text.DRB: Salute Apelles approved in Christ. Salute them that are of Aristobolus' house.
text.Rotherham: Salute Apelles, the approved in Christ. Salute them of [the household of] Aristobulus.

text.KJV: Salute Herodion my kinsman. Greet them that be of the household of Narcissus, which are in the Lord.
text.NABRE: Greet my relative Herodion. Greet those in the Lord who belong to the family of Narcissus.
text.NRSV: Greet my relative Herodion. Greet those in the Lord who belong to the family of Narcissus.
text.DRB: Salute Herodion my kinsman. Salute them that are of Narcissus' house, that are in our Lord.
text.Rotherham: Salute Herodion, my kinsman. Salute them of [the household of] Narcissus who are in the Lord.

text.KJV: Salute Tryphena and Tryphosa, who labour in the Lord. Salute the beloved Persis, which laboured much in the Lord.
text.NABRE: Greet those workers in the Lord, Tryphaena and Tryphosa. Greet the beloved Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.
text.NRSV: Greet those workers in the Lord, Tryphaena and Tryphosa. Greet the beloved Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.
text.DRB: Salute Tryphæna and Tryphosa: who labour in our Lord. Salute Persis the beloved, who hath much laboured in our Lord.
text.Rotherham: Salute Tryphaena and Tryphosa, [sisters] who have toiled in the Lord. Salute Persis, the beloved [sister], who, indeed, hath toiled much in the Lord.

text.KJV: Salute Rufus chosen in the Lord, and his mother and mine.
text.NABRE: Greet Rufus, chosen in the Lord, and his mother and mine.
text.NRSV: Greet Rufus, chosen in the Lord; and greet his mother—a mother to me also.
text.DRB: Salute Rufus the elect in our Lord and his mother and mine.
text.Rotherham: Salute Rufus, the chosen in the Lord; also his and my mother.

text.KJV: Salute Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermas, Patrobas, Hermes, and the brethren which are with them.
text.NABRE: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers who are with them.
text.NRSV: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers and sisters who are with them.
text.DRB: Salute Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermas, Patrobas, Hermes: and the brethren that are with them.
text.Rotherham: Salute Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brethren that are with them.

text.KJV: Salute Philologus, and Julia, Nereus, and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints which are with them.
text.NABRE: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the holy ones who are with them.
text.NRSV: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.
text.DRB: Salute Philologus and Iulia, Nereus, and his sister, and Olympias; and all the saints that are with them.
text.Rotherham: Salute Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympus, and all the saints that are with them.

text.KJV: Salute one another with an holy kiss. The churches of Christ salute you.
text.NABRE: Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ greet you.
text.NRSV: Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ greet you.
text.DRB: Salute one another in a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ salute you.
text.Rotherham: Salute ye one another, with a holy a kiss. All the assemblies of the Christ salute you.

text.KJV: Now I beseech you, brethren, mark them which cause divisions and offences contrary to the doctrine which ye have learned; and avoid them.
text.NABRE: Against Factions. I urge you, brothers, to watch out for those who create dissensions and obstacles, in opposition to the teaching that you learned; avoid them.
text.NRSV: I urge you, brothers and sisters, to keep an eye on those who cause dissensions and offenses, in opposition to the teaching that you have learned; avoid them.
text.DRB: And I desire you, brethren, to mark them that make dissensions and scandals contrary to the doctrine which you have learned, and avoid them.
text.Rotherham: But I beseech you, brethren, to keep an eye upon them who are causing divisions and occasions of stumbling, aside from the teaching which, ye, have learned,—and be turning away from them;

text.KJV: For they that are such serve not our Lord Jesus Christ, but their own belly; and by good words and fair speeches deceive the hearts of the simple.
text.NABRE: For such people do not serve our Lord Christ but their own appetites, and by fair and flattering speech they deceive the hearts of the innocent.
text.NRSV: For such persons do not serve our Lord Christ, but their own appetites, and by smooth talk and flattery they deceive the hearts of the simple-minded.
text.DRB: For such do not serve Christ our Lord, but their own belly: and by sweet speeches and benedictions seduce the hearts of innocents.
text.Rotherham: For, they who are such, unto our Lord Christ are not doing service, but unto their own belly,—and, through their smooth and flattering speech, deceive the hearts of the innocent.

text.KJV: For your obedience is come abroad unto all men. I am glad therefore on your behalf: but yet I would have you wise unto that which is good, and simple concerning evil.
text.NABRE: For while your obedience is known to all, so that I rejoice over you, I want you to be wise as to what is good, and simple as to what is evil;
text.NRSV: For while your obedience is known to all, so that I rejoice over you, I want you to be wise in what is good and guileless in what is evil.
text.DRB: For our obedience is published into every place. I rejoice therefore in you. But I would have you to be wise in good, and simple in evil.
text.Rotherham: For, your obedience, unto all hath reached,—over you, therefore, I rejoice! But I wish you—to be wise, [indeed], as to that which is good, yet pure, as to that which is evil.

text.KJV: And the God of peace shall bruise Satan under your feet shortly. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
text.NABRE: then the God of peace will quickly crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus be with you.
text.NRSV: The God of peace will shortly crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
text.DRB: And the God of peace crush Satan under your feet quickly. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, the God of peace, will crush Satan under your feet shortly. The favour of our Lord Jesus, be with you.

text.KJV: Timotheus my workfellow, and Lucius, and Jason, and Sosipater, my kinsmen, salute you.
text.NABRE: Greetings from Corinth. Timothy, my co-worker, greets you; so do Lucius and Jason and Sosipater, my relatives.
text.NRSV: Timothy, my co-worker, greets you; so do Lucius and Jason and Sosipater, my relatives.
text.DRB: Timothee my coadjutor saluteth you, and Lucius, and Jason, and Sosipater, my kinsmen.
text.Rotherham: There salute you—Timothy, my fellow-worker, and Lucius, and Jason, and Sosipater, my kinsmen.

text.KJV: I Tertius, who wrote this epistle, salute you in the Lord.
text.NABRE: I, Tertius, the writer of this letter, greet you in the Lord.
text.NRSV: I Tertius, the writer of this letter, greet you in the Lord.
text.DRB: I Tertius salute you, that wrote the epistle, in our Lord.
text.Rotherham: I, Tertius, who have written the epistle, salute you in the Lord.

text.KJV: Gaius mine host, and of the whole church, saluteth you. Erastus the chamberlain of the city saluteth you, and Quartus a brother.
text.NABRE: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, greets you. Erastus, the city treasurer, and our brother Quartus greet you.
text.NRSV: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, greets you. Erastus, the city treasurer, and our brother Quartus, greet you.
text.DRB: Caius mine host, and the whole Churches, saluteth you. Erastus the Cofferer of the city saluteth you, and Quartus, a brother.
text.Rotherham: There saluteth you—Gaius, my host and [the host] of the whole assembly. There salute you—Erastus, the steward of the city, and Quartus the brother.

text.NABRE: [ ] Doxology.
text.KJV: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
text.DRB: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all you, Amen.

text.KJV: Now to him that is of power to stablish you according to my gospel, and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery, which was kept secret since the world began,
text.NABRE: [ Now to him who can strengthen you, according to my gospel and the proclamation of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery kept secret for long ages
text.NRSV: Now to God who is able to strengthen you according to my gospel and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery that was kept secret for long ages
text.DRB: And to him that is able to confirm you according to my Gospel and preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery from eternal times kept secret,
text.Rotherham: Now, unto him who hath power to establish you, according to my glad-message—even the proclamation of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of a sacred secret, in age-past times kept silent,

text.KJV: But now is made manifest, and by the scriptures of the prophets, according to the commandment of the everlasting God, made known to all nations for the obedience of faith:
text.NABRE: but now manifested through the prophetic writings and, according to the command of the eternal God, made known to all nations to bring about the obedience of faith,
text.NRSV: but is now disclosed, and through the prophetic writings is made known to all the Gentiles, according to the command of the eternal God, to bring about the obedience of faith—
text.DRB: which now is opened by the Scriptures of the Prophets according to the precept of the eternal God, to the obedience of faith known in all Gentiles,
text.Rotherham: But now made manifest, and through means of prophetic scriptures, according to the command of the age-abiding God, for obedience of faith unto all the nations made known,

text.KJV: To God only wise, be glory through Jesus Christ for ever. Amen. Written to the Romans from Corinthus, and sent by Phebe servant of the church at Cenchrea.
text.NABRE: to the only wise God, through Jesus Christ be glory forever and ever. Amen.]
text.NRSV: to the only wise God, through Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory forever! Amen.
text.DRB: to God the only wise through Jesus Christ, to whom be honour & glory for ever and ever. Amen.
text.Rotherham: Unto a God, wise alone, through Jesus Christ, [unto whom] be the glory, unto the ages. Amen.

16.1: And I commend to you Phebe, our sister, who is in the ministry of the church, that is in Cenchrae:
16.2: That you receive her in the Lord as becometh saints; and that you assist her in whatsoever business she shall have need of you. For she also hath assisted many, and myself also.
16.3: Salute Prisca and Aquila, my helpers in Christ Jesus,
16.4: (Who have for my life laid down their own necks: to whom not I only give thanks, but also all the churches of the Gentiles,)
16.5: And the church which is in their house. Salute Epenetus, my beloved: who is the firstfruits of Asia in Christ.
16.6: Salute Mary, who hath laboured much among you.
16.7: Salute Andronicus and Junias, my kinsmen and fellow prisoners: who are of note among the apostles, who also were in Christ before me.
16.8: Salute Ampliatus, most beloved to me in the Lord.
16.9: Salute Urbanus, our helper in Christ Jesus, and Stachys, my beloved.
16.11: Salute them that are of Aristobulus' household. Salute Herodian, my kinsman. Salute them that are of Narcissus' household, who are in the Lord.
16.12: Salute Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who labour in the Lord. Salute Persis, the dearly beloved, who hath much laboured in the Lord.
16.13: Salute Rufus, elect in the Lord, and his mother and mine.
16.14: Salute Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermas, Patrobas, Hermes, and the brethren that are with them.
16.15: Salute Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympias; and all the saints that are with them.
16.16: Salute one another with an holy kiss. All the churches of Christ salute you.
16.17: Now I beseech you, brethren, to mark them who make dissensions and offences contrary to the doctrine which you have learned, and avoid them.
16.18: For they that are such, serve not Christ our Lord, but their own belly; and by pleasing speeches and good words, seduce the hearts of the innocent.
16.19: For your obedience is published in every place. I rejoice therefore in you. But I would have you to be wise in good, and simple in evil.
16.20: And the God of peace crush Satan under your feet speedily. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
16.21: Timothy, my fellow labourer, saluteth you, and Lucius, and Jason, and Sosipater, my kinsmen.
16.22: I Tertius, who wrote this epistle, salute you in the Lord.
16.23: Caius, my host, and the whole church, saluteth you. Erastus, the treasurer of the city, saluteth you, and Quartus, a brother.
16.24: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
16.25: Now to him that is able to establish you, according to my gospel, and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery, which was kept secret from eternity,
16.26: (Which now is made manifest by the scriptures of the prophets, according to the precept of the eternal God, for the obedience of faith,) known among all nations;
16.27: To God the only wise, through Jesus Christ, to whom be honour and glory for ever and ever. Amen.
